items.title: Présidentielle 2027 : la guerre des noms de domaine est lancée
items.title_zh: 2027法国大选：域名争夺战已打响
items.title_en: France 2027 Presidential Election: The Strategic Battle for Domain Names Begins
items.summary: Avant les meetings, avant les affiches, avant même les déclarations officielles : tout se joue désormais sur quelques caractères tapés dans une barre d'adresse.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出在2027年法国总统大选前，域名已成为战略资产。投机者利用“先注先得”规则抢注候选人域名，构成网络抢注或打字错误劫持。尽管法国民法典保护姓名权，知识产权法保护商标，但需权利人迅速行动。政治讽刺受欧洲人权公约保护，但若造成官方混淆则违法。建议提前进行品牌防御性注册。
items.ai_summary_en: Ahead of the 2027 French presidential election, domain names have emerged as critical strategic assets. Speculators exploit the first-come, first-served registration principle to preemptively secure domains associated with potential candidates, engaging in cybersquatting or typosquatting. While the French Civil Code protects personal name rights and Intellectual Property Code safeguards trademarks, rights holders must act swiftly to enforce these protections. Political satire remains protected under the European Convention on Human Rights; however, impersonating official sources to create confusion constitutes infringement. Consequently, proactive brand defense through early domain registration is essential to mitigate reputational risks and prevent misinformation campaigns targeting political figures and their communications.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧运营时，应重视域名的品牌防御价值。针对潜在的政治或社会热点，提前注册相关域名以防被恶意抢注或用于虚假宣传。同时，需注意区分合法讽刺与侵权混淆的界限，避免法律风险。建立快速响应机制，监测并处理侵犯商标权或姓名的域名注册行为。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies and public figures operating in Europe, securing digital real estate is a fundamental component of brand protection strategy. Proactive defensive registration of relevant domains prevents opportunistic squatting and misuse for disinformation. It is crucial to distinguish between lawful political commentary and infringing impersonation that causes consumer or public confusion. Establishing robust monitoring mechanisms allows for the timely detection and resolution of unauthorized domain registrations. This approach minimizes reputational damage and ensures control over official communication channels during sensitive periods such as elections or major public events.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["域名成为大选关键战略资产，投机者利用先注先得规则抢注", "网络抢注与打字错误劫持干扰竞选，需依据民法与IP法维权", "政治讽刺受保护，但冒充官方造成混淆即构成侵权", "候选人应提前布局域名注册，防范声誉风险与信息误导"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Domain names are vital strategic assets in elections, with speculators using first-come rules to register candidate-related URLs preemptively.", "Cybersquatting and typosquatting disrupt campaigns, requiring rapid legal action based on civil law name rights and trademark protections.", "Political satire is legally protected, but impersonating official entities to cause confusion constitutes trademark or name right infringement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["提前防御性注册域名，防止被投机者抢注或用于虚假宣传", "建立监测机制，及时应对侵犯商标权或姓名的域名侵权行为"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Secure key domains proactively to prevent speculative squatting and protect against false advertising or reputational harm.", "Practice note: Implement continuous monitoring systems to detect and address domain registrations that infringe on trademark or personal name rights.", "What to monitor: Watch for typosquatting variations and unauthorized use of names that may confuse the public or mimic official sources."]
core_points_en: Domain names are vital strategic assets in elections, with speculators using first-come rules to register candidate-related URLs preemptively.
Cybersquatting and typosquatting disrupt campaigns, requiring rapid legal action based on civil law name rights and trademark protections.
Political satire is legally protected, but impersonating official entities to cause confusion constitutes trademark or name right infringement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Secure key domains proactively to prevent speculative squatting and protect against false advertising or reputational harm.
Practice note: Implement continuous monitoring systems to detect and address domain registrations that infringe on trademark or personal name rights.
What to monitor: Watch for typosquatting variations and unauthorized use of names that may confuse the public or mimic official sources.

items.title: Feed vs The Feed : l’antériorité européenne l’emporte sur le succès commercial
items.title_zh: Feed诉The Feed：欧盟在先权利胜诉法国商业成功
items.title_en: Paris Court Annuls 'Feed' Marks: EU Prior Rights Prevail Over Local Commercial Success
items.summary: Par Maître Etienne Bucher, avocat au Barreau de Paris – cabinet Eris Avocat Le 18 février 2026, le Tribunal judiciaire de Paris a prononcé l’annulation de quatre marques françaises comportant le signe « Feed » et a condamné in solidum la société O.K.R (anciennement Feed SA) et son fondateur à verser 179 416,41 € à la société américaine […] L’article Feed vs The Feed : l’antériorité européenne l’emporte sur le succès commercial est apparu en premier sur BLIP! .
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年2月18日，巴黎司法法院撤销了O.K.R公司（原Feed SA）持有的四个含“Feed”字样的法国商标。法院认定美国公司的欧盟商标具有在先权利，其效力优于被告在法国市场的商业成功。被告及其创始人被判连带赔偿约17.9万欧元。此案确立了欧盟层面在先商标权在对抗成员国注册商标时的优先地位，强调了权利基础而非市场知名度在确权中的决定性作用。
items.ai_summary_en: On February 18, 2026, the Paris Judicial Court ordered the cancellation of four French trademarks containing the sign 'Feed', held by O.K.R (formerly Feed SA). The court recognized the prior rights of a US company’s European Union trademark, ruling that such supranational priority outweighs any commercial success achieved by the defendant in the French market. Consequently, O.K.R and its founder were held jointly and severally liable to pay approximately €179,416.41 in damages. This decision reinforces the supremacy of EU-wide trademark rights over national registrations within member states. It establishes that mere market reputation or local commercial performance cannot serve as a valid defense against legitimate prior EU rights, emphasizing the critical importance of securing a clean legal basis for brands operating across Europe rather than relying solely on national registration strategies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局时，必须优先检索EUIPO数据库而非仅关注单一国家注册。即使已在某国建立市场声誉，若侵犯欧盟在先商标权，仍面临撤销及高额赔偿风险。建议采取“欧盟商标为主、成员国为辅”的策略，并在进入新市场前进行全面的FTO尽职调查，避免因权利瑕疵导致品牌资产归零。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders must prioritize comprehensive searches within the EUIPO database rather than relying exclusively on single-country registration checks. Even if a brand has established substantial market reputation in a specific member state, it remains vulnerable to cancellation and high damages if it infringes upon an earlier EU trademark. A strategic approach should center on securing EU-wide protection as the primary asset, supplemented by national filings where necessary. Conducting thorough freedom-to-operate due diligence before market entry is essential to mitigate the risk of brand assets being invalidated due to underlying rights defects, ensuring long-term stability and compliance with European IP frameworks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["巴黎法院撤销四个含“Feed”的法国商标，确认欧盟在先商标权优先", "判决明确商业成功不能对抗合法的欧盟在先权利，被告需承担法律责任", "O.K.R公司及创始人被判连带赔偿17.9万欧元，凸显跨国品牌合规风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Paris Judicial Court annulled four French 'Feed' marks, confirming the primacy of earlier EU trademark rights over national registrations.", "The ruling clarifies that local commercial success does not constitute a valid defense against infringement of prior EU trademark rights.", "O.K.R and its founder face joint liability for nearly €180,000, highlighting significant financial risks for cross-border brand conflicts."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视欧盟商标在先检索，避免单一国家注册掩盖跨国侵权风险", "商业成功非抗辩理由，需确保品牌权利基础在欧盟层面的合法性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritize EUIPO database searches to uncover hidden cross-border infringement risks beyond single-state registrations.", "Practice note: Secure EU-wide trademark protection as the core strategy, using national filings only as supplementary measures.", "What to monitor: Ensure robust freedom-to-operate due diligence before market entry to prevent brand invalidation and costly litigation."]
core_points_en: Paris Judicial Court annulled four French 'Feed' marks, confirming the primacy of earlier EU trademark rights over national registrations.
The ruling clarifies that local commercial success does not constitute a valid defense against infringement of prior EU trademark rights.
O.K.R and its founder face joint liability for nearly €180,000, highlighting significant financial risks for cross-border brand conflicts.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritize EUIPO database searches to uncover hidden cross-border infringement risks beyond single-state registrations.
Practice note: Secure EU-wide trademark protection as the core strategy, using national filings only as supplementary measures.
What to monitor: Ensure robust freedom-to-operate due diligence before market entry to prevent brand invalidation and costly litigation.

items.title: UPC Weekly - Show me your prior art
items.title_zh: UPC周报：展示你的现有技术
items.title_en: UPC Weekly - Show me your prior art
items.summary: 2026 Week 18
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文系Mewburn Ellis发布的UPC第18周简报，标题“Show me your prior art”聚焦于统一专利法院诉讼中关于现有技术的披露与举证策略。文章 likely 探讨了在UPC程序中，当事人如何有效提交和论证现有技术以支持无效抗辩或侵权防御。作为欧洲专利诉讼的关键环节，现有技术的及时、规范披露直接影响案件走向。该资讯为从业者提供了关于UPC程序实务的重要观察与分析。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需高度重视现有技术的早期检索与规范化整理。应建立符合UPC程序要求的证据链，确保在法定期限内高效披露。同时，密切关注UPC法官对现有技术认定的倾向性，优化无效抗辩策略，以降低败诉风险并争取有利和解条件。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["聚焦UPC诉讼中现有技术披露的程序要求与实务策略", "分析现有技术证据提交对专利无效抗辩的关键影响", "提供律所视角下应对UPC程序规则的合规与辩护建议"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立符合UPC程序的现有技术证据管理体系", "关注法官认定倾向，优化无效抗辩与诉讼策略"]

items.title: Emotional Perception vs Inventive Step – Challenges Ahead?
items.title_zh: 情感感知案：创造性步骤评估的挑战与COMVIK方法的应用
items.title_en: Emotional Perception: Adopting COMVIK and Navigating Inventive Step Challenges in the UK
items.summary: In Emotional Perception the UK Supreme Court delivered its verdict on how the patent eligibility of computer-implemented inventions should be assessed in the UK. The decision was a tour de force , rejecting the much-maligned Aerotel approach favoured by the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO), instead putting the European COMVIK (sometimes referred to as the “any hardware”) approach at the heart of UK patent practice. However, the UK Supreme Court decision in Emotional Perception left behind
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国最高法院在“情感感知”案中裁定，废弃UKIPO长期采用的Aerotel测试法，转而采纳欧洲专利局（EPO）的COMVIK方法作为英国专利实践中评估计算机实施发明适格性的核心标准。然而，该判决未明确COMVIK方法在创造性步骤评估中的具体实施细节。本文分析了这一遗留问题，指出UKIPO在未来执行创造性步骤审查时可能面临的挑战及法律不确定性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court’s ruling in Emotional Perception marks a significant shift in assessing patent eligibility for computer-implemented inventions. The decision rejects the long-standing Aerotel test favored by the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO), formally adopting the European Patent Office’s COMVIK approach as the core standard for determining subject matter eligibility in UK practice. However, the judgment leaves critical implementation details unresolved, particularly regarding how the COMVIK framework should be applied during inventive step assessments. This analysis examines the resulting legal ambiguities and identifies potential operational challenges the UKIPO may face when integrating this new standard into examination practice, highlighting areas where further guidance is needed to ensure consistent application.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国科技企业赴英申请软件专利时，应摒弃旧有Aerotel思路，主动对标EPO COMVIK标准，强调技术贡献而非单纯商业逻辑。鉴于英国法院对创造性步骤评估尚存模糊，建议企业在撰写申请文件时强化硬件交互与技术问题解决过程的描述，以应对潜在审查风险并提升授权稳定性。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking patent protection for software-related inventions in the UK must align their drafting strategies with the EPO’s COMVIK standards rather than relying on previous Aerotel-based approaches. Emphasizing technical contributions and concrete hardware interactions is essential to demonstrate inventiveness under the new regime. Given the current ambiguity surrounding inventive step assessments, applicants should provide detailed descriptions of how technical problems are solved through specific system architectures. Monitoring forthcoming UKIPO examination guidelines will be crucial for adapting prosecution strategies and mitigating risks associated with the transitional period in legal interpretation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英最高法院废弃Aerotel测试，正式采纳EPO的COMVIK方法评估软件专利适格性", "判决未解决COMVIK方法在创造性步骤评估中的具体实施路径，留下法律模糊地带", "UKIPO未来在执行新标准时将面临操作挑战，需进一步明确审查指南以统一实践"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Supreme Court replaced the Aerotel test with the EPO's COMVIK approach for assessing software patent eligibility.", "The judgment fails to clarify specific implementation paths for applying COMVIK during inventive step evaluations.", "The UKIPO faces operational challenges and legal uncertainty in executing the new inventive step assessment standards."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["对标EPO COMVIK标准，强化软件发明的技术贡献与硬件交互描述", "关注UKIPO后续审查指南，优化专利申请策略以应对创造性评估的不确定性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Align software patent applications with EPO COMVIK standards, emphasizing technical contributions over pure business logic.", "Practice note: Strengthen descriptions of hardware interactions and technical problem-solving processes to enhance grant stability.", "What to monitor: Watch for updated UKIPO examination guidelines addressing inventive step assessments under the COMVIK framework."]
core_points_en: The UK Supreme Court replaced the Aerotel test with the EPO's COMVIK approach for assessing software patent eligibility.
The judgment fails to clarify specific implementation paths for applying COMVIK during inventive step evaluations.
The UKIPO faces operational challenges and legal uncertainty in executing the new inventive step assessment standards.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Align software patent applications with EPO COMVIK standards, emphasizing technical contributions over pure business logic.
Practice note: Strengthen descriptions of hardware interactions and technical problem-solving processes to enhance grant stability.
What to monitor: Watch for updated UKIPO examination guidelines addressing inventive step assessments under the COMVIK framework.

items.title: From Risk To Reward: What Our 2026 Industry Outlook Research Reveals About AI In IP
items.title_zh: 风险至回报：2026年IP行业AI展望揭示
items.title_en: From Risk to Reward: How AI Is Fundamentally Reshaping the IP Profession by 2026
items.summary: For our third annual survey of AI in IP, we consulted more than 500 IP professionals worldwide to address one of the most pressing questions facing the IP profession today: how exactly is AI impacting the practice of IP law?
items.ai_summary_zh: Questel发布2026年IP行业展望报告，基于全球500多名专业人士调查。显示AI正根本性改变IP角色，73%受访者认为AI将永久转变其职能。专利搜索是AI采用率最高的领域，日常使用AI工具的比例显著上升。尽管预算向技术倾斜，但培训滞后，43%面临人才短缺挑战。报告强调法律与商业知识仍是核心技能，建议机构加强技术培训以应对转型。
items.ai_summary_en: Questel’s latest IP Industry Outlook Research, based on a global survey of over 500 IP professionals, reveals that artificial intelligence is acting as a powerful catalyst for change within the sector. The findings indicate that 73% of respondents believe AI will permanently transform their roles, marking a significant shift in professional responsibilities. While patent search and analytics represent the highest adoption areas for AI tools, with daily usage rates rising substantially, organizations face growing challenges. Despite budget allocations prioritizing IP management technology, there is a notable lag in staff training and access to these systems. Furthermore, nearly half of the participants cite talent shortages as a critical hurdle. The report underscores that while technical proficiency is increasingly vital, core legal and commercial knowledge remains indispensable for navigating this evolving landscape effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应加速部署AI辅助专利检索与分析工具，以提升效率并降低合规风险。鉴于行业对复合型人才需求激增，建议加强内部员工AI技能培训，或招聘具备IP技术经验的专才，以弥补传统法律技能在数字化时代的不足，确保持续竞争力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should prioritize the strategic deployment of AI-assisted patent retrieval and analysis tools to enhance operational efficiency and mitigate compliance risks in European jurisdictions. As the demand for hybrid professionals grows, firms must address the gap between traditional legal expertise and digital competencies. Strengthening internal AI training programs or recruiting specialists with combined IP and technical experience is essential to maintain competitive advantage. Organizations failing to align workforce skills with technological investments risk falling behind. Continuous monitoring of emerging AI trends and regulatory developments will be crucial for adapting practice models and ensuring robust intellectual property management in an increasingly automated environment.

items.title: SANDISK Optimizes Patent Translation And Filing Workflows With Questel
items.title_zh: 闪迪携手Questel优化欧洲专利翻译及申请工作流
items.title_en: Sandisk Optimizes European Patent Translation and Filing Workflows with Questel
items.summary: SANDISK, a global leader in data storage technology, sought to improve the efficiency and consistency of its international patent translation and filing processes.
items.ai_summary_zh: 全球存储技术领导者闪迪为提升国际专利翻译质量与申请效率，对原有外包模式进行结构化评估。面对预算压力与翻译错误风险，闪迪未选择减少海外申请，而是引入Questel进行多法域试点。经第三方独立验证，Questel在确保高质量翻译的同时降低了成本并减少了行政负担。该案例展示了通过专业化服务优化跨国知识产权管理流程的有效路径。
items.ai_summary_en: Sandisk, a global leader in data storage technology, sought to enhance the efficiency and consistency of its international patent translation and filing processes. Facing budget pressures and significant risks associated with translation errors, the company opted against reducing foreign filings. Instead, it conducted a structured evaluation by introducing Questel for multi-jurisdictional pilot tests. Through independent third-party verification, Sandisk confirmed that the Questel model delivered high-quality translations while simultaneously reducing costs and administrative burdens. This case demonstrates an effective pathway for optimizing cross-border intellectual property management processes through specialized service integration, ensuring operational excellence without compromising legal quality or global coverage.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧专利申请量激增，需警惕非技术性行政摩擦导致的隐性成本。建议借鉴闪迪经验，建立严格的供应商评估机制，利用专业平台整合翻译与申报流程。这不仅能规避因翻译瑕疵引发的专利无效风险，还能在预算收紧背景下维持全球布局的稳定性与竞争力。
items.ai_insight_en: With increasing patent application volumes from various market participants in Europe, companies must remain vigilant regarding hidden costs caused by non-technical administrative friction. It is advisable to establish rigorous supplier evaluation mechanisms to mitigate the risk of patent invalidation due to translation defects. Leveraging professional platforms to integrate translation and filing processes can help maintain the stability and competitiveness of global portfolios, particularly during periods of tightened budgets. This approach ensures that operational efficiencies do not compromise the substantive quality required for robust intellectual property protection across diverse jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["闪迪面临翻译质量风险与行政负担，寻求不削减申请量的效率优化方案", "通过多法域试点与第三方独立验证，确认Questel模型兼具高质量与低成本优势", "新工作流实现直接开票与交付，显著降低内部协调成本，提升整体运营效能"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Sandisk addressed translation quality risks and administrative burdens by seeking efficiency solutions that maintained existing application volumes.", "Multi-jurisdictional pilots and independent third-party verification confirmed Questel's model offers both high quality and cost advantages.", "The new workflow enables direct invoicing and delivery, significantly lowering internal coordination costs and improving overall operational efficacy."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立严格供应商评估机制，规避翻译瑕疵引发的专利无效风险", "利用专业平台整合流程，在预算收紧下维持全球布局稳定性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish strict supplier evaluation mechanisms to avoid patent invalidation risks caused by translation defects.", "Practice note: Utilize professional platforms to integrate workflows, maintaining global portfolio stability under budget constraints.", "What to monitor: Administrative overhead and translation quality metrics to prevent hidden costs in cross-border IP management."]
core_points_en: Sandisk addressed translation quality risks and administrative burdens by seeking efficiency solutions that maintained existing application volumes.
Multi-jurisdictional pilots and independent third-party verification confirmed Questel's model offers both high quality and cost advantages.
The new workflow enables direct invoicing and delivery, significantly lowering internal coordination costs and improving overall operational efficacy.

Practice note: Utilize professional platforms to integrate workflows, maintaining global portfolio stability under budget constraints.
What to monitor: Administrative overhead and translation quality metrics to prevent hidden costs in cross-border IP management.

items.title: Questel Welcomes Five Continents International Patent And Trademark Office As A New Client
items.title_zh: Questel宣布FIPO成为新合作伙伴以优化IP运营
items.title_en: Questel Partners with Taiwan’s FIPO to Enhance IP Operations and Expand Asia-Pacific Presence
items.summary: Paris, France – April 22 — Questel, a global leader in intellectual property (IP) solutions, is pleased to announce that Five Continents International Patent and Trademark Office (FIPO) has selected Questel as its partner to support and enhance its IP operations.
items.ai_summary_zh: 总部位于巴黎的全球知识产权解决方案领导者Questel宣布，台湾的五洲国际专利商标事务所（FIPO）选择Questel作为合作伙伴，旨在支持并增强其知识产权运营能力。双方合作将帮助FIPO在复杂的创新环境中加强服务能力并简化工作流程。此举标志着Questel在亚太地区的持续扩张，巩固了其作为全球IP专业人士值得信赖的合作伙伴地位，通过端到端解决方案覆盖从创意到商业化的全生命周期。
items.ai_summary_en: Paris-based Questel, a global leader in intellectual property solutions, has announced a strategic partnership with Five Continents International Patent and Trademark Office (FIPO), a prominent IP consulting firm based in Taiwan. This collaboration aims to support and enhance FIPO’s IP operations by leveraging Questel’s comprehensive technology platform. The partnership is designed to help FIPO strengthen its service capabilities and streamline complex workflows within the fast-paced innovation landscape. By integrating Questel’s end-to-end solutions, which cover the entire lifecycle from idea generation to commercialization, FIPO seeks to improve operational efficiency and deliver greater value to its diverse client base. This move underscores Questel’s continued expansion strategy in the Asia-Pacific region, reinforcing its position as a trusted partner for IP professionals globally through robust software and service offerings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧洲IP服务商整合加速，Questel等巨头通过并购与合作扩大版图。中国企业应关注此类平台化工具，利用其全球数据优势优化海外专利布局与成本管控，提升跨国IP运营效率与合规性。
items.ai_insight_en: The European IP services sector is experiencing accelerated consolidation, with major providers like Questel expanding their global footprint through strategic partnerships and acquisitions. For rights holders and corporate IP teams, this trend highlights the growing importance of integrated, platform-based solutions. Leveraging these established platforms allows organizations to access superior global data resources, facilitating more effective overseas patent portfolio management and cost control. Adopting such tools can significantly improve cross-border IP operational efficiency and ensure better compliance with varying international regulations, ultimately supporting more strategic decision-making in complex global markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Questel与台湾FIPO达成合作，旨在优化后者知识产权运营流程", "此次合作助力Questel深化在亚太地区的影响力及市场布局", "双方将利用Questel平台提升IP管理效率及客户服务价值"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Questel partners with Taiwan's FIPO to optimize intellectual property management processes and operational workflows.", "The collaboration strengthens Questel's market presence and influence across the Asia-Pacific region significantly.", "Both parties will utilize Questel's platform to enhance IP management efficiency and increase client service value."]

items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor the consolidation of European IP service providers to identify integrated platforms that optimize overseas patent layouts.", "Practice note: Leverage data advantages from leading service providers to enhance cross-border IP operational efficiency and regulatory compliance.", "What to monitor: Track strategic partnerships among global IP firms to assess emerging tools for cost management and workflow automation."]

The collaboration strengthens Questel's market presence and influence across the Asia-Pacific region significantly.
Both parties will utilize Questel's platform to enhance IP management efficiency and increase client service value.

items.title: Digital IP Management: How Digitalization Helps IP Professionals
items.title_zh: 数字IP管理：数字化如何助力知识产权专业人士
items.title_en: Digital IP Management: How Digitalization Enhances Efficiency for IP Professionals
items.summary: As one of the most valuable corporate assets, intellectual property must be managed appropriately to maximize its potential. In line with many areas of life, technological advancements are transforming how IP is managed, including a wide choice of digital and online solutions for IP portfolio management.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出知识产权作为核心资产需妥善管理以最大化价值。传统纸质管理耗时且易出错，而数字化技术通过在线申请、数据库检索及文档电子化，显著提升了IP组合管理的效率与准确性。文章强调，数字化不仅降低了合规成本，还增强了权利执行的确定性，是未来IP管理的必然趋势，有助于企业规避因管理不善导致的权利丧失风险。
items.ai_summary_en: Intellectual property represents a critical corporate asset requiring robust management to maximize its commercial value. Traditional paper-based workflows are often time-consuming and prone to errors, whereas digital technologies significantly improve the efficiency and accuracy of IP portfolio administration. By leveraging online filing systems, electronic document storage, and centralized databases, organizations can streamline compliance processes and reduce administrative burdens. This shift not only lowers operational costs but also strengthens the certainty of rights enforcement. Effective digital management is essential to prevent the loss of rights due to procedural oversights. As the industry evolves, adopting these digital solutions has become an inevitable trend, enabling rights holders to maintain comprehensive control over their assets while adapting to modern, transparent IP management environments.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应加速引入IP数字化管理系统，利用在线工具监控商标专利状态，防范因期限延误导致的权利丧失。同时，善用公开数据库进行FTO分析与竞品监控，提升合规效率，降低海外维权成本，适应欧洲高效透明的IP管理环境。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should prioritize the adoption of integrated digital IP management systems to monitor trademark and patent statuses proactively. Utilizing online tools helps mitigate risks associated with missed deadlines, which can lead to irreversible rights forfeiture. Furthermore, leveraging public databases for freedom-to-operate analyses and competitor monitoring enhances overall compliance efficiency. This approach supports more effective overseas rights protection strategies by providing real-time data insights. Aligning with Europe’s high standards for transparent and efficient IP administration allows companies to optimize resource allocation, reduce litigation exposure, and maintain a competitive edge through rigorous, technology-driven asset oversight.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["数字化技术取代传统纸质流程，提升IP组合管理效率并降低合规成本", "在线数据库实现快速检索，增强IP状态透明度，辅助商业决策与竞争分析", "有效数字化管理确保权利执行基础，避免因程序疏忽导致知识产权失效"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Digital tools replace traditional paper processes, enhancing IP portfolio management efficiency and reducing compliance costs.", "Online databases enable rapid retrieval of information, increasing IP status transparency to support business decisions.", "Effective digital management ensures a solid foundation for rights enforcement, preventing IP loss from procedural negligence."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["引入数字化工具监控IP状态，防范期限延误导致的权利丧失风险", "利用在线数据库进行竞品监控与FTO分析，提升海外合规与维权效率"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Adopt digital tools to monitor IP statuses and prevent rights loss from deadline delays.", "Practice note: Use online databases for competitor monitoring and FTO analysis to boost overseas compliance.", "What to monitor: Integration of digital workflows to enhance efficiency and reduce overseas enforcement costs."]
core_points_en: Digital tools replace traditional paper processes, enhancing IP portfolio management efficiency and reducing compliance costs.
Online databases enable rapid retrieval of information, increasing IP status transparency to support business decisions.
Effective digital management ensures a solid foundation for rights enforcement, preventing IP loss from procedural negligence.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Adopt digital tools to monitor IP statuses and prevent rights loss from deadline delays.
Practice note: Use online databases for competitor monitoring and FTO analysis to boost overseas compliance.
What to monitor: Integration of digital workflows to enhance efficiency and reduce overseas enforcement costs.

items.title: Stop Searching For Registered Trademarks Online: You’re Wasting Time And Money
items.title_zh: 停止在线盲目检索：商标搜索外包更省时省钱
items.title_en: Avoid Blind Online Searches: Outsourcing Trademark Clearance Saves Time and Money
items.summary: If you only search for registered trademark rights online, you will miss many important records. Discover the right tools for registered trademark searches.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出仅依赖免费在线数据库进行商标注册检索存在局限，易遗漏重要记录且难以正确解读结果。尽管各国知识产权局提供免费数据库，但商业数据库功能更全面。鉴于非专业人士在检索策略和结果分析上常遇困难，企业面临时间与成本浪费风险。因此，建议将商标检索工作外包给外部专业服务提供商，以利用其专业知识优化流程，确保检索质量并让企业聚焦核心业务。
items.ai_summary_en: Relying solely on free online databases for registered trademark searches presents significant limitations, often leading to missed critical records and difficulties in correctly interpreting search results. While national intellectual property offices provide free access to their registers, commercial databases offer more comprehensive functionalities and broader coverage. However, non-specialists frequently struggle with effective search strategies and accurate result analysis, resulting in wasted time and resources due to inefficiencies. Consequently, outsourcing trademark clearance work to external professional service providers is recommended. This approach leverages specialized expertise to optimize the search process, ensuring higher quality and thoroughness. By delegating these tasks, companies can focus on core business activities while mitigating the risks associated with incomplete or inaccurate self-conducted searches, ultimately achieving a more robust and reliable trademark protection strategy.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧拓展品牌时，应避免仅凭免费工具自行检索，以免因漏检引发侵权或注册驳回风险。建议引入具备欧盟多法域经验的第三方专业机构进行尽职调查，确保检索全面性。此举虽增加前期投入，但能有效规避后续高昂的法律纠纷成本，保障品牌出海安全。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants expanding brands into Europe should avoid relying exclusively on free tools for self-conducted searches, as this may lead to infringement risks or registration refusals due to overlooked prior rights. Engaging third-party professional agencies with multi-jurisdictional EU experience for due diligence ensures comprehensive search coverage. Although this increases initial investment, it effectively avoids subsequent high legal dispute costs, safeguarding brand expansion safety. Rights holders must prioritize thorough clearance to secure their market position against potential conflicts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["单一在线检索易遗漏记录，需结合国家库与商业库优势", "非专业人员易误读结果，导致检索效率低下与资源浪费", "外包检索可借助专家经验，显著提升准确性并节约时间成本"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Single online searches risk missing records; combining national and commercial database strengths ensures comprehensive coverage.", "Non-professionals often misinterpret results, causing low search efficiency and unnecessary waste of organizational resources.", "Outsourcing searches utilizes expert experience to significantly improve accuracy while saving valuable time and costs."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["摒弃单纯依赖免费工具的粗放检索模式，防范漏检风险", "引入专业第三方服务，提升欧盟商标布局的合规性与安全性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Abandon rough search modes relying solely on free tools to prevent risks of missed prior rights and potential infringement claims.", "Practice note: Introduce professional third-party services to enhance the compliance and security of European Union trademark portfolio layouts.", "What to monitor: The balance between initial search costs and long-term litigation avoidance through rigorous, expert-led clearance procedures."]
core_points_en: Single online searches risk missing records; combining national and commercial database strengths ensures comprehensive coverage.
Non-professionals often misinterpret results, causing low search efficiency and unnecessary waste of organizational resources.
Outsourcing searches utilizes expert experience to significantly improve accuracy while saving valuable time and costs.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Abandon rough search modes relying solely on free tools to prevent risks of missed prior rights and potential infringement claims.
Practice note: Introduce professional third-party services to enhance the compliance and security of European Union trademark portfolio layouts.
What to monitor: The balance between initial search costs and long-term litigation avoidance through rigorous, expert-led clearance procedures.

items.title: Cabinet decision on the Statutory Health Insurance Contribution Stabilization Act – Financial burdens on German pharmaceutical companies
items.title_zh: 德内阁通过医保缴费稳定法草案
items.title_en: Cabinet decision on the Statutory Health Insurance Contribution Stabilization Act – Financial burdens on German pharmaceutical companies
items.summary: Banking & finance
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国内阁批准《法定健康保险缴费稳定法》草案，旨在稳定2027年起医保费率。针对专利药品引入动态制造商回扣机制，排除仿制药等；扩大价格冻结至2030年底并转为活性成分基础；对专利疫苗新增7%回扣。措施意在缓解医保资金短缺，增加药企财务负担。
items.ai_insight_zh: 该法案通过调整专利药品回扣和延长价格冻结，直接压缩专利药企利润空间。企业需关注2027年生效的动态回扣计算方式及活性成分维度的价格管控，评估对专利组合商业回报的长期影响，并监测最终立法文本中关于豁免条款的具体界定。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["专利药品制造商回扣将动态调整，2027年下半年生效，仿制药等除外", "价格冻结期延长至2030年底，框架由公司级调整为活性成分级", "专利保护疫苗将新增7%的制造商回扣，大麻花卉报销将被取消"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["动态回扣机制将改变专利药品定价预期，需关注2027年实施细节", "价格冻结转为活性成分维度，可能影响同一成分不同专利药的策略", "需持续跟踪法案在联邦议院的审议进程及最终豁免范围的确认"]

items.title: Vertical Aerospace hits back at Archer Aviation over air taxi patents
items.title_zh: Vertical Aerospace就空中出租车专利反击Archer Aviation
items.title_en: Vertical Aerospace and Archer Aviation Clash Over eVTOL Motor Patents as Aridge Enters Market
items.summary: As dispute over electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft motors on between UK and US aerospace companies, China debuts flying-car unit from Aridge
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国航空航天公司Vertical Aerospace与美国Archer Aviation之间关于电动垂直起降飞机电机的专利纠纷持续。原文摘要仅确认双方存在争议及Vertical Aerospace的回应动作，未提供具体法律程序细节。
items.ai_summary_en: A significant patent dispute has erupted between UK-based Vertical Aerospace and US competitor Archer Aviation regarding electric motor technology for electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft. This legal confrontation highlights the intensifying intellectual property competition within the global low-altitude economy sector. Concurrently, Chinese manufacturer Aridge has introduced its own flying car unit, further complicating the competitive landscape. The situation underscores the critical importance of robust cross-border patent strategies and the heightened risks of infringement claims among major market participants. As these companies vie for dominance in the emerging air taxi industry, the interplay between established Western firms and new entrants from China illustrates the complex dynamics of global IP enforcement and technological leadership in advanced aviation technologies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息极度有限，仅能确认跨境专利冲突事实。需持续关注后续是否进入英国法院诉讼程序、涉及的具体专利族范围以及双方是否寻求禁令救济，目前无法评估案件实质影响。
items.ai_insight_en: The escalating patent litigation in the eVTOL sector signals that intellectual property rights are becoming a primary tool for market positioning and competitive exclusion. Rights holders must prioritize comprehensive freedom-to-operate analyses before entering key jurisdictions like the UK and US to mitigate infringement risks. Simultaneously, accelerating the filing of autonomous core technology patents is essential to build a defensive portfolio capable of withstanding cross-border disputes. Companies should leverage supply chain strengths to reinforce their IP posture, ensuring they are prepared for potential international litigation while navigating the evolving regulatory and competitive environment of the global low-altitude economy.

items.title: Pierre Pérot returns to HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER as partner in Paris, joined by Camille Abba
items.title_zh: Pierre Pérot以合伙人身份重返HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER巴黎办公室
items.title_en: Pierre Pérot Returns as Partner to HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER Paris Office
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲知名知识产权律所HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER宣布，Pierre Pérot将于2026年5月1日以合伙人身份重返其巴黎办公室，Camille Abba同时以律师身份加入。Pérot在商标、外观设计、版权及相关权利领域拥有丰富经验，尤其擅长奢侈品、体育、娱乐及数字商业等高风险行业。此次回归旨在加强巴黎软知识产权团队的深度与活力，为客户提供更具商业敏锐度和快速响应的法律服务，体现了律所在该领域的持续投入与战略扩展。
items.ai_summary_en: HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER has announced the return of Pierre Pérot as a partner in its Paris office, effective 1 May 2026. He is joined by Camille Abba, who joins as an associate. Pérot specializes in soft IP matters, including trade marks, designs, and copyright, with significant experience in the luxury, sport, entertainment, and digital sectors. The firm highlights that this move strengthens the Paris team's capacity to provide commercially aware, rapid, and coordinated legal advice. Managing Partner Benoit Strowel emphasized Pérot’s strategic judgment and seamless integration into the firm’s culture as key assets for clients facing time-sensitive reputational and business challenges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧拓展品牌与内容业务时，应关注此类顶级律所的人才动向。Pérot在版权与软IP领域的专长，提示企业需重视非专利类知识产权的战略布局。建议加强与具备商业敏锐度的律所合作，确保在奢侈品、娱乐等高曝光行业的合规性与维权效率，以应对复杂的欧盟市场环境。
items.ai_insight_en: This lateral hire signals continued consolidation among leading European IP firms in high-value soft IP sectors. For rights holders in luxury and digital industries, the emphasis on commercial awareness and rapid response suggests that legal counsel selection increasingly prioritizes strategic agility alongside technical expertise. Monitoring such partnerships helps identify firms best positioned to handle complex, cross-border enforcement and licensing scenarios where brand reputation is at stake.

items.title: Is The German Utility Model Your Secret Weapon For IP Enforcement?
items.title_zh: 德国实用新型：知识产权维权的秘密武器？
items.title_en: German Utility Models: Strategic Alternatives for Rapid IP Protection and Enforcement
items.summary: The German Utility Model provides a strategic alternative to standard patent protection, offering unexamined IP rights for technical inventions with fast registration and flexible enforcement options. This protection mechanism features unique advantages including narrower prior art definitions, grace periods for applicant disclosures, and the powerful branch-off application feature that allows derivation from pending patent applications.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章分析德国实用新型作为标准专利的战略替代方案。其优势在于无需实质审查、注册快（数周）、费用低及保护期长达10年。相比专利，其现有技术定义更窄（仅限德国境内的公开使用或口头披露），且享有6个月宽限期。企业可通过从待审专利中“分支”申请，快速获得针对仿冒者的 enforceable 权利，弥补专利审查期间的保护空白，是灵活的诉讼工具。
items.ai_summary_en: The German Utility Model offers a strategic alternative to standard patent protection, providing unexamined rights for technical inventions with rapid registration and lower official fees. Unlike patents, it undergoes only formal examination, allowing registration within weeks while substantive novelty checks are deferred. This mechanism features a narrower prior art definition, excluding foreign public use and oral disclosures, and includes a six-month grace period for applicant disclosures. It serves as a flexible litigation tool, particularly useful for addressing fast-moving copycat competitors during the lengthy examination phases of European or German patent applications.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在德布局时，可利用实用新型快速确权填补专利审查空窗期。鉴于其维权灵活且成本低，建议结合专利构建双重保护网。需注意方法类发明不可申请，但信号序列等可受保护。在遭遇快速仿冒时，应优先启动实用新型维权程序以争取时间优势。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders operating in Germany, the Utility Model serves as a critical complement to standard patent portfolios. Its ability to secure enforceable rights rapidly fills the protection gap inherent in the slower search and examination processes of DE and EP patents. The specific limitations on prior art and the availability of branch-off applications from pending patent filings offer distinct tactical advantages for securing quick injunctions against infringers without the upfront cost and delay of full patent prosecution.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国实用新型无需实质审查，注册迅速且官方费用低于标准专利", "现有技术范围较窄，仅德国境内公开构成障碍，并享6个月宽限期", "支持从待审专利分支申请，可快速获取权利以应对侵权和仿冒行为"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Utility Models provide virtually immediate registered rights through formal examination only, bypassing substantive novelty and inventive step checks before grant.", "Prior art is narrowly defined; foreign public use and oral disclosures do not constitute prior art, and a six-month grace period applies to applicant disclosures.", "Protection covers apparatus, systems, circuits, and substances, but excludes pure methods, though signal sequences may remain protectable under case law."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用实用新型快速确权，填补发明专利审查期间的保护真空期", "结合专利构建双重保护网，针对快速仿冒行为灵活启动维权"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Use Utility Models to secure rapid, low-cost enforcement rights against copycats while awaiting grant of corresponding patent applications.", "Practice note: Leverage the narrower prior art scope and branch-off mechanisms to maintain flexibility and validity in potential litigation scenarios.", "What to monitor: Track case law developments regarding the protectability of signal sequences and means-plus-function claims within the Utility Model framework."]
core_points_en: Utility Models provide virtually immediate registered rights through formal examination only, bypassing substantive novelty and inventive step checks before grant.
Prior art is narrowly defined; foreign public use and oral disclosures do not constitute prior art, and a six-month grace period applies to applicant disclosures.
Protection covers apparatus, systems, circuits, and substances, but excludes pure methods, though signal sequences may remain protectable under case law.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Use Utility Models to secure rapid, low-cost enforcement rights against copycats while awaiting grant of corresponding patent applications.
Practice note: Leverage the narrower prior art scope and branch-off mechanisms to maintain flexibility and validity in potential litigation scenarios.
What to monitor: Track case law developments regarding the protectability of signal sequences and means-plus-function claims within the Utility Model framework.

items.title: Cyprus Trademarks - Protecting Brand Value In A Digital & Investor World
items.title_zh: 塞浦路斯商标：数字与投资时代的品牌价值保护
items.title_en: Cyprus Trademarks: Securing Brand Value in a Digital and Investor-Focused Environment
items.summary: A strong brand is one of the greatest assets a business can build. Yet many Cyprus companies overlook trademarks, treating the registration of their brand name or logo, as a bureaucratic formality rather than a strategic decision. This can lead to lost market share, investor hesitation, and costly disputes.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出塞浦路斯企业常忽视商标注册的战略价值，将其视为官僚程序，导致市场份额流失和投资受阻。作者强调商标是创造竞争优势的法律工具，能防止竞争对手搭便车。内容涵盖商标定义、注册路径（塞浦路斯、欧盟及国际）、常见误区及内部准备检查。旨在指导企业通过正确管理商标资产，实现业务增长、吸引投资并最大化品牌价值。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis by Kinanis LLC addresses the strategic importance of trademark registration for businesses operating in Cyprus, challenging the common misconception that such filings are merely bureaucratic formalities. The article outlines how trademarks function as critical legal instruments that protect brand identity, prevent competitor exploitation, and enhance tangible business value. It details various trademark types, including word marks, logos, and non-traditional signs, while clarifying the practical equivalence of trademarks and service marks under Cypriot and EU law. Furthermore, the guide explores registration pathways across national, EU, and international levels, highlighting frequent missteps and offering an internal readiness checklist to help rights holders maximize asset protection and support investment growth.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，塞浦路斯后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: For European and US-based companies with operations or investments in Cyprus, this update underscores the necessity of integrating trademark strategy into broader corporate governance and financing preparations. Treating IP as a core intangible asset rather than administrative compliance is essential for maintaining investor confidence and preventing costly disputes. Rights holders should ensure their portfolio management reflects the specific definitions and protections available under both national and EU frameworks to secure long-term brand equity.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["纠正塞企将商标注册视为单纯官僚程序的误区，强调其战略资产属性", "解析商标作为法律锚点，在区分来源、防止侵权及提升商业价值中的作用", "提供从定义、注册路径到避免常见误区的全面指南，助力品牌增值"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Trademarks serve as strategic legal anchors that create competitive advantages and protect against unauthorized use of brand fame.", "Registration pathways span Cyprus, the EU via EUIPO, and international systems, requiring careful selection based on market presence.", "Common pitfalls include treating registration as a formality rather than a core component of corporate asset management strategy."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["中企需视商标为核心资产，避免仅做形式合规而忽视战略布局", "建议采用多国与欧盟组合注册策略，防范数字时代品牌被抢注风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Investors and acquirers increasingly scrutinize IP ownership; robust trademark registration mitigates due diligence risks and supports valuation.", "Practice note: Conduct internal readiness checks before filing to ensure all brand elements, including slogans and packaging shapes, are adequately covered.", "What to monitor: Watch for updates in EUIPO examination practices regarding non-traditional marks and distinctiveness requirements in the digital sector."]
core_points_en: Trademarks serve as strategic legal anchors that create competitive advantages and protect against unauthorized use of brand fame.
Registration pathways span Cyprus, the EU via EUIPO, and international systems, requiring careful selection based on market presence.
Common pitfalls include treating registration as a formality rather than a core component of corporate asset management strategy.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Investors and acquirers increasingly scrutinize IP ownership; robust trademark registration mitigates due diligence risks and supports valuation.
Practice note: Conduct internal readiness checks before filing to ensure all brand elements, including slogans and packaging shapes, are adequately covered.
What to monitor: Watch for updates in EUIPO examination practices regarding non-traditional marks and distinctiveness requirements in the digital sector.

items.title: Transforming IP Management: The Shift From Digital Tools To Intelligent Systems
items.title_zh: IP管理变革：从数字化工具到智能系统的转变
items.title_en: Transforming IP Management: The Shift From Digital Tools To Intelligent Systems
items.summary: A modern IP Management System (IPMS) must provide comprehensive life cycle coverage. It enables portfolio maintenance, agent instruction, control over renewals and annuities, deadline tracking, reporting and insight through dashboards.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出传统IP管理系统虽能处理生命周期事务，但已沦为基线功能。随着数据增长及AI介入，下一代系统需超越机械交互，转向自然语言查询与情境化搜索，降低用户认知负担。系统应集成助手指导操作流程，并有效处理非结构化数据，从而适应专业人士实际工作习惯，提升决策效率与管理一致性。
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应关注IP管理工具的智能化升级，利用AI提升跨国专利商标维护效率。建议评估现有系统对多法域法律更新的响应能力，引入自然语言检索等功能以降低合规风险，优化全球IP组合管理策略。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["传统IPMS功能已成基线，需向智能化演进以应对数据增长", "引入自然语言交互替代机械搜索，降低用户认知负担", "集成智能助手处理非结构化数据，优化工作流程与决策"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注IP管理工具智能化升级，利用AI提升跨国维护效率", "评估系统对多法域法律更新响应，优化全球IP组合策略"]

items.title: DIAMS Infinity redefines how Intellectual Property is managed in practice
items.title_zh: Dennemeyer发布DIAMS Infinity重塑IP管理实践
items.title_en: DIAMS Infinity redefines how Intellectual Property is managed in practice
items.summary: The Dennemeyer Group has launched a next-generation Intellectual Property software solution for expanding organizations that need reliability and efficiency in managing their portfolios.
items.ai_summary_zh: Dennemeyer集团推出新一代知识产权管理软件DIAMS Infinity，旨在解决组合扩张中的效率与可靠性挑战。该软件内置人工智能，自动处理重复性行政任务并连接专利局数据库以确保数据准确性，减少人工干预风险。通过释放高价值工作时间，帮助组织在复杂的多法域环境中实现更智能、高效的IP组合管理与战略决策支持。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应关注此类AI驱动的IP管理工具，以应对日益复杂的全球布局。利用自动化技术可降低多国申请与维护成本，提升数据准确性。建议评估引入此类系统，强化对欧洲及全球IP生命周期的精细化管控，从而将资源集中于核心创新与战略布局。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["发布内置AI的DIAMS Infinity软件，自动化处理重复行政任务以提升效率", "直连专利局数据库确保多法域法律状态准确，降低人为错误与合规风险", "将管理者从繁琐记录中解放，聚焦高价值战略分析，优化IP组合管理"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用AI工具降低多国IP维护成本，提升数据准确性与管理效率", "强化全球IP生命周期精细化管控，释放资源聚焦核心创新与战略布局"]

items.title: Managing IP EMEA Awards 2026: Bird & Bird, Taylor Wessing among big winners as UPC looms large
items.title_zh: 2026 Managing IP EMEA奖：Bird & Bird等胜出，UPC影响显著
items.title_en: UPC Solidifies Status as Core Benchmark in Managing IP EMEA Awards 2026
items.summary: The variety of winners demonstrates that the UPC is now a core benchmark rather than an experimental consideration, while junior lawyers are becoming more deeply involved in key work
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，在2026年Managing IP EMEA奖项中，Bird & Bird和Taylor Wessing等律所成为主要赢家。获奖情况的多样性表明，统一专利法院（UPC）现已成为核心基准，而非实验性考量因素。同时，初级律师正更深入地参与到关键工作中。
items.ai_summary_en: Managing IP has announced the winners of its 2026 EMEA Awards, highlighting significant achievements by firms such as Bird & Bird and Taylor Wessing. The diversity of recognized practices underscores a pivotal shift in the European patent litigation landscape: the Unified Patent Court (UPC) is no longer viewed merely as an experimental jurisdiction but has established itself as a central benchmark for strategic enforcement. Furthermore, the awards reflect evolving industry dynamics, noting that junior lawyers are increasingly entrusted with substantive responsibilities in key cases. This trend signals a maturing market where operational efficiency and deep technical expertise are paramount. The recognition patterns indicate that legal service providers are adapting to the UPC’s growing influence, integrating it into core practice areas while restructuring talent deployment to meet the demands of this new judicial environment.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需持续观察UPC作为核心基准对欧洲专利诉讼策略的具体塑造作用，以及律所团队结构中初级律师角色变化对案件处理流程的潜在影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should integrate the Unified Patent Court into their primary European patent enforcement strategies, recognizing its established role as a main litigation platform. Firms are advised to prioritize collaboration with legal teams possessing proven practical experience in UPC proceedings to enhance enforcement efficacy and navigate procedural complexities effectively. Additionally, evaluating the capabilities of junior lawyer teams is crucial for optimizing litigation costs and operational efficiency. As the European patent judicial environment matures, rights holders must adapt to these structural changes, ensuring their external counsel strategies align with the current realities of cross-border patent disputes and the increasing delegation of key tasks to emerging legal talent within leading firms.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Bird & Bird和Taylor Wessing等律所在2026年Managing…", "摘要显示UPC已成为核心基准，不再是实验性考量", "初级律师正更深入地参与关键法律工作"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Unified Patent Court has transitioned from an experimental option to a fundamental benchmark for European patent litigation strategies.", "Bird & Bird and Taylor Wessing emerged as prominent winners in the 2026 Managing IP EMEA Awards, reflecting strong market performance.", "Junior lawyers are assuming greater responsibility in critical intellectual property matters, indicating a shift in professional roles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC从实验性机制向核心基准转变后的实务常态", "观察初级律师在关键UPC案件中参与度提升的趋势", "监测获奖律所在UPC领域的业务布局变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Integrate the UPC into core European patent strategies as a primary litigation platform rather than an alternative option.", "Practice note: Select legal partners with demonstrated practical experience in UPC proceedings to maximize enforcement effectiveness and procedural compliance.", "What to monitor: Assess the professional capabilities of junior lawyer teams to optimize litigation costs and maintain high-quality representation."]
core_points_en: The Unified Patent Court has transitioned from an experimental option to a fundamental benchmark for European patent litigation strategies.
Bird & Bird and Taylor Wessing emerged as prominent winners in the 2026 Managing IP EMEA Awards, reflecting strong market performance.
Junior lawyers are assuming greater responsibility in critical intellectual property matters, indicating a shift in professional roles.

Practice note: Select legal partners with demonstrated practical experience in UPC proceedings to maximize enforcement effectiveness and procedural compliance.
What to monitor: Assess the professional capabilities of junior lawyer teams to optimize litigation costs and maintain high-quality representation.

items.title: The UPC’s Fujifilm v Kodak decision significantly qualifies its long-arm jurisdiction over the UK
items.title_zh: UPC富士胶片诉柯达案显著限定其对英长臂管辖权
items.title_en: UPC Fujifilm v Kodak Ruling Significantly Limits Long-Arm Jurisdiction Over UK Defendants
items.summary: The January ruling adds strategic limitations to the use of UPC long-reach powers, write lawyers from Taylor Wessing
items.ai_summary_zh: 泰勒·韦辛律师事务所律师撰文指出，统一专利法院在一月作出的富士胶片诉柯达案裁决中，对其针对英国的“长臂管辖权”施加了战略性限制。该裁决显著限缩了UPC跨境权力的适用范围，为相关司法实践提供了新的界限指引。
items.ai_summary_en: Lawyers from Taylor Wessing highlight that the Unified Patent Court’s January decision in Fujifilm v Kodak imposes significant strategic limitations on its long-arm jurisdiction powers. This ruling clarifies the boundaries of UPC jurisdiction when defendants are located in non-member states, such as the United Kingdom. Consequently, the court’s ability to expand case acceptance through long-arm principles is restricted. This development alters previous market expectations regarding the UPC’s broad jurisdictional reach. It has profound implications for cross-border patent litigation strategies involving UK entities, requiring parties to carefully assess jurisdictional risks when selecting forums for legal proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需持续观察该裁决后续对UPC跨境禁令及管辖权扩张的具体影响。鉴于正文信息有限，暂无法评估其对非欧盟成员国专利诉讼策略的深层改变，建议关注后续类似案件的司法援引情况。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders must reassess the certainty of UPC jurisdiction in cases involving UK-related subjects. Global litigation portfolios should be optimized to mitigate risks associated with limited long-arm reach. When formulating enforcement or defense strategies, parties should leverage the distinct procedural characteristics of both UK courts and the UPC. Precise selection of favorable jurisdictions can reduce legal costs and enhance enforcement efficiency. Avoiding over-reliance on the UPC’s previously assumed broad jurisdiction is now essential for effective dispute resolution planning.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC在富士胶片诉柯达案一月裁决中作出关键认定", "该裁决显著限定并增加了UPC对英国长臂管辖权的战略限制"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC ruling explicitly restricts the scope of long-arm jurisdiction applicable to defendants based in the UK.", "The judgment introduces critical strategic limitations on how the UPC exercises its extended jurisdictional powers.", "This decision reshapes jurisdictional expectations and litigation strategies for multinational cases involving UK entities."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC长臂管辖权在英国案件中的适用边界变化", "监测后续案件中当事人对该裁决先例效力的援引与抗辩", "观察该限制是否影响UPC对其他非缔约国主体的管辖态度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Reassess jurisdictional certainty under the UPC for cases involving UK-based entities or connections.", "Practice note: Optimize global litigation layouts to avoid risks stemming from restricted long-arm jurisdiction capabilities.", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions further defining the interface between member state jurisdiction and non-member state defendants."]
core_points_en: The UPC ruling explicitly restricts the scope of long-arm jurisdiction applicable to defendants based in the UK.
The judgment introduces critical strategic limitations on how the UPC exercises its extended jurisdictional powers.
This decision reshapes jurisdictional expectations and litigation strategies for multinational cases involving UK entities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Reassess jurisdictional certainty under the UPC for cases involving UK-based entities or connections.
Practice note: Optimize global litigation layouts to avoid risks stemming from restricted long-arm jurisdiction capabilities.
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions further defining the interface between member state jurisdiction and non-member state defendants.

items.title: Trademark News: What Flew Under the Radar in April 2026
items.title_zh: 2026年4月欧洲商标动态：CJEU裁决与EUIPO新政
items.title_en: April 2026 Trademark Roundup: CJEU Ruling on Prior Rights and EUIPO DSA Cooperation
items.summary: Stay informed with our comprehensive roundup of the past month's headlines, all neatly compiled for you in one convenient post. Catch up on the latest news reported by the Kluwer IP Reporter.Argentina eases technology transfer and trademark registration processesOn 31 March 2026, Argentina’s National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) introduced measures to simplify the registration of technology transfer agreements and speed up the trademark registration process.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文综述了2026年4月全球商标要闻，重点聚焦欧洲。欧盟法院在C-337/22案中裁定，依据《欧盟商标条例》第8(4)条提起异议时，所依赖的在先权利必须在申请日及EUIPO作出决定日均有效。此外，EUIPO与欧盟委员会签署五年协议，依据《数字服务法》加强打击在线假冒与盗版。其他非欧动态包括阿根廷简化注册、巴西加入马德里电子提交系统及印度Waymo禁令案。
items.ai_summary_en: This monthly briefing highlights key trademark developments from April 2026, with a primary focus on European jurisprudence and policy. The Court of Justice of the European Union ruled in Case C-337/22 that prior rights invoked under Article 8(4) EUTMR must remain valid not only at the filing date but also when the EUIPO issues its decision. Concurrently, the EUIPO and the European Commission signed a five-year agreement under the Digital Services Act to intensify efforts against online counterfeiting. Global updates include Argentina’s simplification of technology transfer and trademark registration processes, Brazil becoming the first Latin American country to join the WIPO Madrid e-Filing service, and an interim injunction granted by the Delhi High Court in favor of Waymo. These developments reflect ongoing shifts in procedural requirements, digital enforcement frameworks, and international filing efficiencies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧企业需建立全周期权利监控机制，确保证据链覆盖从申请到裁决全过程以应对CJEU新标准。同时应重视DSA合规，利用EUIPO新机制主动维权。建议关注马德里体系电子化趋势，优化国际注册策略以降低管理成本并提升响应速度。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating in Europe should implement robust monitoring mechanisms to ensure evidence chains cover the entire lifecycle from application to final decision, aligning with the new CJEU standard for prior rights validity. Compliance with the Digital Services Act is increasingly critical; companies should leverage the enhanced cooperation between the EUIPO and the European Commission to proactively address online infringement. Furthermore, market participants should adapt their international registration strategies to accommodate the growing digitization of the Madrid System, particularly with Brazil’s integration into e-Filing, to optimize management costs and improve response times across jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU裁定异议程序中的在先权利需在申请日及决定日均持续存在", "EUIPO与欧盟委员会签约强化DSA框架下在线打假执法力度", "多国推进数字化改革，如巴西接入马德里e-Filing及阿根廷简化流程"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["CJEU rules that prior rights in opposition proceedings must exist continuously from the application filing date through to the final decision date.", "EUIPO and the European Commission sign a five-year agreement under the Digital Services Act to strengthen actions against online counterfeiting and piracy.", "Brazil joins the Madrid e-Filing service as the first Latin American member, while Argentina introduces measures to simplify registration processes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["确保证据链覆盖全程以应对CJEU对在先权利存续时间的新要求", "利用DSA新机制强化在线维权并适应马德里体系电子化趋势"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure continuous proof of prior rights existence from filing to decision to satisfy CJEU opposition standards.", "Practice note: Leverage the new EUIPO-EC Digital Services Act framework to enhance proactive online anti-counterfeiting enforcement.", "What to monitor: Track the expansion of Madrid e-Filing adoption and evolving electronic filing rules in key non-European markets."]
core_points_en: CJEU rules that prior rights in opposition proceedings must exist continuously from the application filing date through to the final decision date.
EUIPO and the European Commission sign a five-year agreement under the Digital Services Act to strengthen actions against online counterfeiting and piracy.
Brazil joins the Madrid e-Filing service as the first Latin American member, while Argentina introduces measures to simplify registration processes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure continuous proof of prior rights existence from filing to decision to satisfy CJEU opposition standards.
Practice note: Leverage the new EUIPO-EC Digital Services Act framework to enhance proactive online anti-counterfeiting enforcement.
What to monitor: Track the expansion of Madrid e-Filing adoption and evolving electronic filing rules in key non-European markets.

items.title: How To Achieve Efficient Trademark Renewals Through Outsourcing
items.title_zh: 通过外包实现高效商标续展的策略分析
items.title_en: Optimizing EU Trademark Renewals: Leveraging Outsourcing for Efficiency and Cost Control
items.summary: If you’re looking to outsource your trademark renewals, you’ll need a provider you can trust to deliver the best possible service at a sensible cost.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨企业如何通过外包优化欧盟及法国的商标续展流程。文章指出，选择可靠的服务提供商需平衡内部干预与外部信任。以Questel为例，其提供单一联系人机制、无纸化网络平台及优化的代理网络，旨在降低行政负担、确保成本效益并提升服务质量。该模式强调通过API集成和严格的质量验证，为企业提供更省心、高效的知识产权管理服务。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines how companies can optimize trademark renewal processes in the European Union and France through strategic outsourcing. It highlights that selecting a reliable service provider requires balancing internal oversight with external trust. Using Questel as a case study, the text details a solution featuring a single point of contact, a paperless web platform, and an optimized agent network. These elements aim to reduce administrative burdens, ensure cost-effectiveness, and enhance service quality. The model emphasizes peace of mind through digital integration and strict quality verification, allowing rights holders to manage renewals efficiently while maintaining control over their intellectual property portfolios without excessive internal resource allocation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应重视商标续展的合规性与效率，利用专业外包服务规避因程序疏漏导致的权利丧失风险。建议评估服务商的数字化能力与本地代理网络质量，通过API集成实现全球IP资产统一管理，从而在控制成本的同时提升品牌保护的安全性与响应速度。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should prioritize compliance and efficiency in trademark renewals by leveraging specialized outsourcing services. This approach helps mitigate risks of rights loss due to procedural oversights. Companies are advised to evaluate providers based on their digital capabilities and the quality of local agent networks. Integrating these services via API facilitates unified global IP asset management. This strategy allows organizations to control costs effectively while enhancing the security and responsiveness of brand protection measures across multiple jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["建立单一联系点机制，简化客户与管理方沟通，显著降低行政负担", "采用无纸化平台与API集成，实现全天候监控及数据无缝对接", "持续优化代理网络并通过批量协议降低成本，同时严格验证文件质量"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Establishing a single point of contact simplifies communication between clients and providers, significantly reducing administrative burdens.", "Implementing a paperless platform with API integration enables twenty-four-hour monitoring and seamless data connectivity.", "Continuously optimizing the agent network and utilizing volume agreements lowers costs while strictly verifying document quality."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用专业外包规避程序风险，确保欧盟商标权利稳定延续", "评估服务商数字化能力，通过API集成实现全球IP资产高效管理"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilize professional outsourcing to avoid procedural risks and ensure the stable continuation of EU trademark rights.", "Practice note: Evaluate provider digital capabilities and leverage API integration for efficient global IP asset management.", "What to monitor: Assess the balance between internal intervention and provider trust to maintain optimal renewal workflows."]
core_points_en: Establishing a single point of contact simplifies communication between clients and providers, significantly reducing administrative burdens.
Implementing a paperless platform with API integration enables twenty-four-hour monitoring and seamless data connectivity.
Continuously optimizing the agent network and utilizing volume agreements lowers costs while strictly verifying document quality.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilize professional outsourcing to avoid procedural risks and ensure the stable continuation of EU trademark rights.
Practice note: Evaluate provider digital capabilities and leverage API integration for efficient global IP asset management.
What to monitor: Assess the balance between internal intervention and provider trust to maintain optimal renewal workflows.

items.title: Managing Intellectual Property Recordals—A Brainteaser For In-House Legal Teams
items.title_zh: 管理IP登记：企业内部法务团队的难题
items.title_en: Managing IP Recordals: Strategic Timing and Documentation for In-House Teams
items.summary: The process of managing an intellectual property (IP) recordal project can be complex for an in-house IP legal team since it requires detailed and accurate information specific to the applicable countries and jurisdictions.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出，在欧盟及成员国管理知识产权（IP）登记项目复杂且具挑战性。内部法务团队需处理特定法域的详细信息、文件准备及费用问题。建议分三步走：首先评估时机，权衡延期至续展以节省成本与即时登记以避免法律风险；其次组织文件，建立系统或委托外部代理以提高效率；最后分配预算与资源，确保项目顺利执行，避免因记录错误引发财务和法律损失。
items.ai_summary_en: Managing intellectual property recordal projects in the European Union and its member states presents significant complexity for in-house legal teams. These initiatives require precise handling of jurisdiction-specific details, meticulous document preparation, and careful fee management. The process involves balancing strategic timing to optimize costs while mitigating legal risks associated with delayed filings. Furthermore, the administrative burden of organizing varied documentation across different regions necessitates robust systems or external support. Effective execution demands accurate budgeting and resource allocation to prevent discrepancies in ownership records. Such errors can lead to substantial financial losses and legal vulnerabilities. This overview highlights the critical steps for planning and implementing successful IP recordals, ensuring compliance and protecting asset value across diverse European jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧扩张时，应建立统一的IP登记监控机制，避免仅依赖续展节点而忽视变更登记的时效性。针对多国差异，建议引入专业代理机构或数字化工具管理文档，确保权属清晰，降低因程序瑕疵导致的维权障碍及潜在罚款风险。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders operating across Europe, relying solely on renewal cycles for IP updates creates exposure to procedural gaps. A unified monitoring mechanism ensures that changes in ownership or licensing are recorded promptly, regardless of renewal dates. Given the divergent documentary requirements among EU member states, leveraging specialized external agencies or digital management tools is advisable. This approach minimizes the risk of procedural defects that could hinder enforcement actions or result in fines. Maintaining clear and current records is fundamental to securing robust legal protection and avoiding unnecessary administrative burdens during critical enforcement periods.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["IP登记需结合具体法域要求，平衡登记时机以优化成本并规避法律风险", "文档管理繁杂，应建立标准化流程或授权外部代理以提升跨国登记效率", "需精准规划预算与资源，防止因信息滞后导致权利归属错误及经济损失"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["IP recordals must align with specific jurisdictional requirements, balancing timing to optimize costs and avoid legal risks.", "Document management is complex; establishing standardized processes or engaging external agents improves cross-border efficiency.", "Precise budget and resource planning are essential to prevent ownership errors and subsequent financial or legal losses."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立统一监控机制，避免仅依赖续展节点，确保变更登记及时准确", "利用外部代理或数字化工具应对多国文档差异，降低程序瑕疵风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish a unified monitoring system to ensure timely and accurate recording of IP changes, rather than depending exclusively on renewal deadlines.", "Practice note: Utilize external specialists or digital solutions to manage varying national documentation standards and reduce procedural error risks.", "What to monitor: Track jurisdiction-specific filing requirements and deadlines to prevent ownership discrepancies that could compromise enforcement capabilities."]
core_points_en: IP recordals must align with specific jurisdictional requirements, balancing timing to optimize costs and avoid legal risks.
Document management is complex; establishing standardized processes or engaging external agents improves cross-border efficiency.
Precise budget and resource planning are essential to prevent ownership errors and subsequent financial or legal losses.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish a unified monitoring system to ensure timely and accurate recording of IP changes, rather than depending exclusively on renewal deadlines.
Practice note: Utilize external specialists or digital solutions to manage varying national documentation standards and reduce procedural error risks.
What to monitor: Track jurisdiction-specific filing requirements and deadlines to prevent ownership discrepancies that could compromise enforcement capabilities.

items.title: The UPC And License Agreements
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院与许可协议：关键考量
items.title_en: Unified Patent Court and License Agreements: Key Considerations for Rights Holders
items.summary: 2023 will most likely be the year the Unified Patent Court (UPC) will open its doors. With the imminent start of the new European court system, the underlying legal framework will also become applicable.
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着统一专利法院（UPC）即将启动，其法律框架将自动适用于传统欧洲专利。文章指出，需审查现有及未来许可协议以应对新系统风险。重点包括：确定谁有权决定“选择退出”UPC管辖，这通常取决于谁负责维持专利；明确谁有权决定是否申请具有单一效力的欧洲专利；以及界定在新旧系统中谁拥有专利执行权。建议在未来合同中明确这些权利归属，以避免争议并优化诉讼策略。
items.ai_summary_en: With the Unified Patent Court (UPC) set to commence operations, its legal framework will automatically apply to classic European patents unless an opt-out is declared. This shift introduces centralized enforcement benefits but also risks of centralized validity attacks. Consequently, rights holders must review existing and future license agreements to ensure compatibility with the new system. Critical issues include determining who holds the authority to decide on opting out of UPC jurisdiction, which often depends on maintenance obligations. Additionally, parties must clarify who decides whether to apply for a Unitary Patent and define enforcement rights across both traditional and new systems. Clear contractual provisions are essential to manage these strategic decisions effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应全面审查现有专利许可协议，明确UPC退出权及单一专利申请权的归属。建议在后续谈判中增加相关条款，以规避集中无效风险并掌握诉讼主动权。同时，需评估被许可人的执行权限，确保知识产权战略与新法院体系兼容，保护核心资产安全。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should comprehensively audit existing patent license agreements to clarify ownership of UPC opt-out rights and unitary patent application decisions. Future negotiations ought to include explicit clauses addressing these powers to mitigate risks associated with centralized invalidation actions. Furthermore, evaluating licensee enforcement permissions ensures that intellectual property strategies remain compatible with the new court structure. Proactively establishing clear rules helps secure core assets and maintains control over litigation strategies in the evolving European patent landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["审查许可协议以确定谁有权决定从UPC系统选择退出", "明确谁有权决定是否申请具有单一效力的欧洲专利", "界定在UPC及传统系统中谁拥有专利执行权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Review license agreements to determine who holds the authority to decide on opting out of UPC jurisdiction.", "Clarify which party has the right to decide whether to apply for a European patent with unitary effect.", "Define enforcement rights within both the UPC system and traditional national court frameworks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["审查协议明确UPC退出权及单一专利申请权归属", "增加条款明确执行权限，规避集中无效风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Audit existing licenses to clarify UPC opt-out and unitary patent decision rights.", "Practice note: Include explicit contractual clauses defining enforcement powers to mitigate central invalidation risks.", "What to monitor: Evolving interpretations of licensee enforcement authority under the new UPC framework."]
core_points_en: Review license agreements to determine who holds the authority to decide on opting out of UPC jurisdiction.
Clarify which party has the right to decide whether to apply for a European patent with unitary effect.
Define enforcement rights within both the UPC system and traditional national court frameworks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Audit existing licenses to clarify UPC opt-out and unitary patent decision rights.
Practice note: Include explicit contractual clauses defining enforcement powers to mitigate central invalidation risks.
What to monitor: Evolving interpretations of licensee enforcement authority under the new UPC framework.

items.title: Small town, big bite: local restaurant shows genuine use doesn’t need…
items.title_zh: 欧盟普通法院：单一餐厅本地使用可构成欧盟商标真实使用
items.title_en: General Court: Local Restaurant Use With Cross-Border Reach Qualifies As Genuine EU Trade Mark Use
items.summary: A single steakhouse, social media buzz and cross-border visibility: the General Court clarifies how “local” use in one member state can still amount to…
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在T-297/25案中裁定，德国一家位于小城的牛排馆“OX”虽仅在一国经营，但通过社交媒体、多语言在线广告及跨境客源，其商标影响力超越本地，构成欧盟范围内的真实使用。法院推翻了EUIPO以未使用为由撤销商标的决定，确认单一成员国的使用若具备跨境商业影响，即可维持欧盟商标权，强调服务类商标的使用地与宣传地可分离。
items.ai_summary_en: In Case T-297/25, the General Court ruled that a single steakhouse named “OX” in a small German town constituted genuine use of an EU trade mark. Although operating in only one member state, the business demonstrated cross-border influence through social media engagement, multilingual online advertising, and international clientele. The Court overturned the EUIPO’s initial revocation for non-use, confirming that local physical presence combined with digital visibility satisfies EU-wide requirements. This decision clarifies that genuine use does not require extensive geographical coverage across the Union. Instead, it emphasizes that service providers can maintain rights by proving their trade mark’s impact extends beyond immediate locality via digital channels and cross-border consumer interaction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧运营无需追求全境实体布局，应重视数字化营销与跨境流量转化。保留好在线广告、多语种用户反馈及国际订单等证据，可有效应对“不使用撤销”风险，以低成本维护欧盟商标权利。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating primarily in one EU member state should prioritize robust documentation of digital marketing efforts and cross-border customer interactions to defend against non-use cancellation actions. Maintaining records of online advertisements, user feedback in multiple languages, and international bookings provides critical evidence of genuine use. This approach allows businesses to sustain EU trade mark protection without establishing physical operations across all member states. By leveraging digital presence to demonstrate broader market influence, companies can effectively mitigate cancellation risks while optimizing resource allocation for brand maintenance.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["单一成员国内的局部使用若具跨境商业影响，可认定为欧盟商标真实使用", "服务类商标的使用地不仅限于物理经营场所，还包括在线广告及数字足迹覆盖区", "社交媒体粉丝量、多语言评论及跨境客源是证明商标超出本地范围的关键证据"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Local use within a single member state may constitute genuine EU trade mark use if it demonstrates significant cross-border commercial impact.", "For service marks, the place of use includes areas covered by online advertising and digital footprints, not just physical service locations.", "Social media metrics, multilingual reviews, and evidence of cross-border customers are key proofs of market reach beyond local boundaries."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化数字营销证据留存，以证明商标在欧盟市场的实际影响力", "利用跨境在线曝光弥补单一成员国实体经营的地理局限"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Documenting digital footprint and cross-border engagement helps validate genuine use for localized service businesses.", "Practice note: Preserve evidence of multilingual online ads, social media activity, and international customer data to counter non-use claims.", "What to monitor: Evolving judicial interpretations regarding the separation between physical service locations and digital trade mark exposure."]
core_points_en: Local use within a single member state may constitute genuine EU trade mark use if it demonstrates significant cross-border commercial impact.
For service marks, the place of use includes areas covered by online advertising and digital footprints, not just physical service locations.
Social media metrics, multilingual reviews, and evidence of cross-border customers are key proofs of market reach beyond local boundaries.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Documenting digital footprint and cross-border engagement helps validate genuine use for localized service businesses.
Practice note: Preserve evidence of multilingual online ads, social media activity, and international customer data to counter non-use claims.
What to monitor: Evolving judicial interpretations regarding the separation between physical service locations and digital trade mark exposure.
