items.title: Est-il important qu’un fonds d’investissement détienne des droits de marque ?
items.title_zh: 投资基金持有商标权利是否重要？
items.title_en: Strategic Importance of Investment Funds Holding Trademark Rights in Modern Valuation
items.summary: Introduction Dans un contexte économique où la valeur des entreprises repose de plus en plus sur des actifs immatériels, la question de la détention de droits de marque par les fonds d’investissement s’impose. Longtemps perçues comme de simples outils marketing, les marques constituent désormais des actifs juridiques et financiers structurants,
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出在经济日益依赖无形资产的背景下，投资基金持有商标权的问题愈发关键。商标已从单纯的营销工具转变为结构性的法律与金融资产。分析强调了在投资策略中正确归属和管理商标权利的重要性，以确保资产价值最大化并规避法律风险。
items.ai_summary_en: In an economic landscape increasingly driven by intangible assets, the ownership of trademark rights by investment funds has become a critical strategic consideration. Trademarks have evolved beyond mere marketing instruments to become foundational legal and financial assets that significantly influence corporate valuation. This analysis highlights the necessity for investors and rights holders to carefully structure trademark ownership within their investment strategies. Proper attribution and management of these rights are essential to maximize asset value, ensure legal compliance, and mitigate potential risks associated with intellectual property disputes or unclear ownership chains.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧并购或引入外资时，应提前规划商标权属架构。建议将核心商标独立于运营实体，由专门控股公司持有，以提升资产清晰度与估值，满足投资方尽职调查要求。
items.ai_insight_en: For market participants engaging in cross-border transactions or seeking European investment, proactive planning of trademark ownership architecture is essential. Separating core trademarks from operating entities by holding them within specialized holding companies can enhance asset clarity and valuation transparency. This structure facilitates smoother due diligence processes for investors and isolates operational risks. Furthermore, distinct ownership models improve asset liquidity, making the portfolio more attractive to institutional investors who prioritize clear intellectual property governance and risk mitigation strategies in their investment decisions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["商标已演变为结构性法律与金融资产而非仅营销工具", "投资基金需重视商标权属以优化无形资产投资组合", "明确商标持有主体有助于提升企业估值与融资能力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Trademarks have transformed from simple marketing tools into structural legal and financial assets central to business value.", "Investment funds must prioritize clear trademark ownership structures to optimize portfolios of intangible assets effectively.", "Defining the specific entity holding trademark rights enhances corporate valuation metrics and strengthens financing capabilities."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["优化商标权属架构可提升对欧洲投资方的吸引力", "分离持有商标有助于隔离风险并增强资产流动性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Optimizing trademark ownership structures increases attractiveness to European investors by demonstrating robust IP governance.", "Practice note: Consider separating trademark holdings from operating entities to isolate liability and enhance overall asset liquidity.", "What to monitor: Evolving investor expectations regarding intellectual property due diligence and the strategic separation of intangible assets."]
core_points_en: Trademarks have transformed from simple marketing tools into structural legal and financial assets central to business value.
Investment funds must prioritize clear trademark ownership structures to optimize portfolios of intangible assets effectively.
Defining the specific entity holding trademark rights enhances corporate valuation metrics and strengthens financing capabilities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Optimizing trademark ownership structures increases attractiveness to European investors by demonstrating robust IP governance.
Practice note: Consider separating trademark holdings from operating entities to isolate liability and enhance overall asset liquidity.
What to monitor: Evolving investor expectations regarding intellectual property due diligence and the strategic separation of intangible assets.

items.title: Case update (UK): Laboratorios Leon Farma SA v The Comptroller-General Of Patents - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: 英国高院驳回单成分 SPC 上诉，重申 Medeva 判例效力
items.title_en: UK High Court Dismisses Single-Ingredient SPC Appeal, Reaffirming Medeva Precedent
items.summary: Following our detailed report (here) of the UK IPO’s decision to refuse an SPC application for a single-ingredient product in light of earlier authorisations for combination products containing that ingredient (BL O/0705/25 of 31 July 2025), we now provide a brief update on the High Court’s dismissal of the applicant’s appeal ([2026] EWHC 663 (Ch) […]
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国高院驳回 Laboratorios Leon Farma 关于单成分产品补充保护证书（SPC）的上诉，维持知识产权局拒授决定。法院裁定，依据 CJEU Medeva 判例，含该活性成分的早期组合药授权即构成首次授权，阻碍后续单成分 SPC 申请。法官强调创新程度不影响法定资格，并指出匈牙利等国存在相反实践，凸显欧洲内部法律适用分歧。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK High Court has dismissed Laboratorios Leon Farma SA’s appeal against the UK IPO’s refusal of a supplementary protection certificate (SPC) for a single-ingredient product. The court upheld the decision that earlier marketing authorisations for combination products containing the same active ingredient constitute the first authorisation under Article 3(d). Recorder Douglas Campbell KC confirmed that the CJEU’s Medeva line of case law remains good law in the UK. Consequently, an early combination authorisation blocks subsequent single-ingredient SPC applications. The judgment clarifies that the level of innovation does not alter the strict statutory requirements. It also highlights divergent judicial practices across Europe, noting that jurisdictions like Hungary have adopted different approaches to similar factual scenarios.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企在英国布局单成分 SPC 时，若存在早期组合药上市记录，极可能面临拒授风险。需提前排查欧洲各国审查差异，利用匈牙利等宽松法域策略性布局，同时避免过度依赖技术创新理由突破法定形式要件限制。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking SPCs in the UK must carefully assess whether earlier marketing authorisations for combination products exist, as these may preclude protection for single-ingredient variants under the strict interpretation of Article 3(d). The High Court’s rejection of innovation-based arguments reinforces the importance of regulatory history over technical merit in eligibility determinations. Given the noted discrepancies with practices in other EU member states such as Hungary, applicants should consider jurisdiction-specific strategies. Monitoring ongoing developments and potential referrals to the CJEU will be essential to navigate this fragmented legal landscape effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["高院确认 Medeva 判例在英国持续有效，早期组合授权阻断单成分 SPC", "法官明确专利创新高度不改变 SPC 法规第 3(d) 条的严格适用标准", "判决揭示欧洲各国对类似事实图案的 SPC 资格审查存在显著司法分歧"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["High Court confirms Medeva precedent remains valid in UK, blocking single-ingredient SPCs via early combination authorisations.", "Judicial ruling establishes that patent innovation level does not override strict Article 3(d) eligibility standards for SPCs.", "Decision reveals significant judicial divergence across Europe regarding SPC examination for similar combination product fact patterns."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国严格遵循首次授权原则，早期组合药记录将致命打击单成分 SPC 申请", "应关注欧洲各国审查分歧，制定差异化国别策略以最大化专利保护期限"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Early combination product authorisations in the UK may fatally undermine subsequent single-ingredient SPC applications under current case law.", "Practice note: Do not rely on the inventive step or innovation level of the single-ingredient product to overcome Article 3(d) barriers; focus on regulatory timelines.", "What to monitor: Divergent national practices in jurisdictions like Hungary and any future CJEU references that may harmonise the interpretation of first authorisation."]
core_points_en: High Court confirms Medeva precedent remains valid in UK, blocking single-ingredient SPCs via early combination authorisations.
Judicial ruling establishes that patent innovation level does not override strict Article 3(d) eligibility standards for SPCs.
Decision reveals significant judicial divergence across Europe regarding SPC examination for similar combination product fact patterns.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Early combination product authorisations in the UK may fatally undermine subsequent single-ingredient SPC applications under current case law.
Practice note: Do not rely on the inventive step or innovation level of the single-ingredient product to overcome Article 3(d) barriers; focus on regulatory timelines.
What to monitor: Divergent national practices in jurisdictions like Hungary and any future CJEU references that may harmonise the interpretation of first authorisation.

items.title: De Koffiejongens averts PI filed by Ox Barrier in the Netherlands
items.title_zh: De Koffiejongens成功规避Ox Barrier在荷申请的临时禁令
items.title_en: De Koffiejongens averts PI filed by Ox Barrier in the Netherlands
items.summary: The case is reminiscent of the patent dispute over coffee capsules between manufacturers Nespresso, Krups, De’Longhi and Ethical Coffee Company (ECC), which broke out between 2016 and 2018 in Germany and Switzerland, as well as the more recent dispute between Koninklijke Douwe Egberts and Belmoca. Ox Barrier owns EP 3 145 838, which covers compostable […] The post De Koffiejongens averts PI filed by Ox Barrier in the Netherlands appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: Ox Barrier依据其拥有的欧洲专利EP 3 145 838（涵盖可堆肥技术），在荷兰对De Koffiejongens提起临时禁令申请，但被后者成功规避。该案背景类似于此前在德国和瑞士发生的咖啡胶囊专利纠纷，以及Koninklijke Douwe Egberts与Belmoca近期的争议。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案展示了欧洲专利在成员国层面的临时禁令执法风险。需关注荷兰法院在咖啡胶囊等技术密集领域对临时禁令的审查标准，以及权利人如何利用单一欧洲专利在不同法域发起并行或后续执法行动。

items.title: Current developments in the European Unitary Patent system | Grünecker
items.title_zh: 欧洲统一专利体系最新动态与战略考量
items.title_en: Strategic Implications of Recent UPC Case Law for European Patent Portfolios
items.summary: Recent practical experience and decisions are increasingly giving rise to strategic questions for companies, regarding the European Unitary Patent system.
items.ai_summary_zh: Grünecker律所分析指出，随着统一专利法院（UPC）案例法日益成熟，企业面临新的战略挑战。文章重点探讨了单一专利、传统欧洲专利与国家权利的组合策略，并深入剖析了UPC在权利要求解释、第二医疗用途侵权及 imminent infringement认定上的最新判例。建议医药及生命科学企业密切监控司法动态，制定灵活前瞻的专利布局以应对新机遇与风险。
items.ai_summary_en: As the Unified Patent Court (UPC) case law matures, rights holders face critical strategic decisions regarding patent enforcement and portfolio management. This analysis examines key judicial developments, including standards for claim interpretation, infringement determinations for second medical use claims, and thresholds for imminent infringement risks. The evolving jurisprudence highlights the necessity for companies to adapt their strategies within the new unitary patent framework. It emphasizes the importance of balancing the broad coverage of unitary patents with the flexibility of traditional European and national rights. By understanding these legal nuances, market participants can better navigate the opportunities and risks presented by the centralized litigation system, ensuring robust protection and effective enforcement across participating member states.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企应重新评估在欧洲的专利组合，利用UPC的一站式效力优化成本。需重点关注第二医疗用途等细分领域的侵权判定趋势，提前规避法律风险。同时，结合国家专利进行防御性布局，以应对UPC可能带来的禁令威胁，确保市场准入安全。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders in the pharmaceutical and life sciences sectors should closely track UPC rulings on second medical use claims to refine assertion strategies. Re-evaluating patent layouts to combine the wide territorial reach of unitary patents with the tactical flexibility of national rights creates a robust defensive posture. This approach helps mitigate risks associated with imminent infringement allegations while securing market access. Integrating these mechanisms allows for more resilient enforcement frameworks, enabling companies to respond effectively to litigation challenges and maintain competitive stability under the new European patent regime.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC案例法确立，需统筹单一专利与传统欧专的战略组合", "聚焦第二医疗用途侵权及紧迫侵权风险的司法认定标准", "医药企业需监控司法动态，构建灵活前瞻的专利保护策略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC jurisprudence is establishing clear standards for claim interpretation and patent infringement determinations.", "Judicial focus intensifies on second medical use claims and specific requirements for proving imminent infringement risks.", "Companies must dynamically adjust patent portfolios to balance unitary patent coverage with national right protections."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["优化单一专利与传统欧专组合，平衡成本与覆盖范围", "关注第二医疗用途侵权判定，提前规避UPC禁令风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Align claim drafting precision with UPC standards for second medical use to strengthen enforceability.", "Practice note: Establish dynamic monitoring systems to flexibly switch between unitary and national patent strategies during litigation.", "What to monitor: Evolving UPC precedents on imminent infringement thresholds and their impact on portfolio optimization."]
core_points_en: UPC jurisprudence is establishing clear standards for claim interpretation and patent infringement determinations.
Judicial focus intensifies on second medical use claims and specific requirements for proving imminent infringement risks.
Companies must dynamically adjust patent portfolios to balance unitary patent coverage with national right protections.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Align claim drafting precision with UPC standards for second medical use to strengthen enforceability.
Practice note: Establish dynamic monitoring systems to flexibly switch between unitary and national patent strategies during litigation.
What to monitor: Evolving UPC precedents on imminent infringement thresholds and their impact on portfolio optimization.

items.title: M&C in the news: UPC Court of Appeal makes inaugural CJEU referral on long-arm jurisdiction
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉法院就长臂管辖权首次向欧盟法院提请先决裁决
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Makes First CJEU Referral on Long-Arm Jurisdiction and Anchor Defendants
items.summary: The Unified Patent Court’s (UPC) Court of Appeal has made its inaugural referral to the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU), a...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院在戴森与追觅的卷发技术纠纷中，首次向欧盟法院（CJEU）提请先决裁决。核心争议在于 UPC 能否利用非欧盟实体在成员国的授权代表作为“锚点”，对其在非 UPC 成员国的侵权行为行使长臂管辖权并颁布泛欧禁令。此举旨在厘清管辖边界，虽引发短期不确定性，但有助于实现欧盟内判决一致性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Court of Appeal has issued its first referral to the Court of Justice of the European Union, marking a significant procedural milestone in European patent litigation. Arising from a dispute between Dyson and Dreame regarding hair curling technology, the referral seeks clarification on whether the UPC can exercise long-arm jurisdiction over non-EU entities. Specifically, it addresses whether a local authorized representative within a UPC member state serves as a sufficient anchor to bind a foreign parent company to pan-European injunctions for alleged infringements occurring outside the UPC’s territorial scope. This case aims to define critical jurisdictional boundaries. While the referral introduces a period of legal uncertainty for international businesses, resolving these questions is essential for achieving consistent, non-contradictory judgments across the European Union, which remains a primary objective of the UPC system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧需重新评估授权代表选址策略，避免在非 UPC 国家设立代表从而被纳入管辖。建议提前规划诉讼应对方案，关注欧盟法院最终裁定对长臂管辖权的界定，以防面临不可控的泛欧禁令风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This development presents significant implications for rights holders and market participants operating globally. Companies must carefully evaluate their choice of authorized representatives within Europe, as selecting entities in UPC member states may expose foreign parent companies to the court's long-arm jurisdiction. Strategic planning should include assessing litigation risks associated with such appointments. Furthermore, monitoring the CJEU’s eventual ruling is crucial, as the decision will clarify the reach of pan-European injunctions. Understanding these jurisdictional limits allows businesses to better manage enforcement strategies and mitigate the risk of facing broad, cross-border legal orders that could impact commercial activities beyond the immediate territory of the alleged infringement.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 上诉法院作出历史性首例欧盟法院先决裁决请求", "焦点在于能否通过本地代表管辖非欧盟母公司的境外行为", "裁决结果将决定泛欧禁令是否可延伸至非 UPC 成员国领域"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal has made its inaugural referral to the CJEU, establishing a historic precedent for interpreting jurisdictional rules within the new court system.", "The core legal issue concerns whether an authorized representative in a member state acts as an anchor defendant, allowing the UPC to claim jurisdiction over non-EU parent compani…", "The final ruling will determine if pan-European injunctions can legally extend to cover alleged patent infringements occurring in states that are not members of the Unified Patent…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["审慎选择欧盟境内授权代表所在地以规避管辖风险", "密切关注 CJEU 裁决，预判泛欧禁令对中国出口企业影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate the strategic selection of authorized representatives in Europe to avoid inadvertently establishing jurisdictional anchors for non-EU parent companies.", "Practice note: Develop proactive litigation response plans that account for potential long-arm jurisdiction claims based on local representative connections.", "What to monitor: Track the CJEU’s final ruling on this referral to understand how pan-European injunctions may apply to infringements in non-UPC member states."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal has made its inaugural referral to the CJEU, establishing a historic precedent for interpreting jurisdictional rules within the new court system.
The core legal issue concerns whether an authorized representative in a member state acts as an anchor defendant, allowing the UPC to claim jurisdiction over non-EU parent compani…
The final ruling will determine if pan-European injunctions can legally extend to cover alleged patent infringements occurring in states that are not members of the Unified Patent…
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate the strategic selection of authorized representatives in Europe to avoid inadvertently establishing jurisdictional anchors for non-EU parent companies.
Practice note: Develop proactive litigation response plans that account for potential long-arm jurisdiction claims based on local representative connections.
What to monitor: Track the CJEU’s final ruling on this referral to understand how pan-European injunctions may apply to infringements in non-UPC member states.

items.title: The Risks of Using (Too Much) Artificial Intelligence
items.title_zh: 过度使用人工智能的潜在风险分析
items.title_en: Analyzing the Risks of Over-Reliance on Artificial Intelligence in IP Practice
items.summary: I have long intended to write a piece about Artificial Intelligence (AI), but this seems to be one of these subjects where the world turns faster than my ability to process it. Nonetheless, here we go, finally.Let’s start with some general comments.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出全球科技巨头对 AI 基础设施投入巨大，但面临氦气短缺和内存价格飙升等供应链风险。作者质疑当前免费模式的可持续性，并重点探讨在知识产权领域使用 AI 时，数据保密性可能受到的威胁及商业泡沫风险，呼吁谨慎评估 AI 工具的使用边界。
items.ai_summary_en: The article examines the massive global investments by tech giants into AI infrastructure, highlighting significant supply chain vulnerabilities such as helium shortages and surging RAM prices. It questions the sustainability of current free AI service models, suggesting underlying commercial motives that may shift soon. For intellectual property professionals, the piece emphasizes critical risks regarding data confidentiality when using AI tools. There is a heightened concern about potential leaks of sensitive information and the formation of commercial bubbles. The author urges market participants to carefully evaluate the boundaries of AI adoption, balancing efficiency gains against the imperative of protecting trade secrets and ensuring robust data security protocols within their operations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧运用 AI 辅助 IP 工作时，应警惕数据跨境传输与保密风险，避免将核心机密输入公共模型。建议建立内部 AI 使用合规指南，平衡效率提升与信息安全的界限，防范潜在的商业秘密泄露。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants utilizing AI for intellectual property tasks should exercise caution regarding cross-border data transmission and confidentiality obligations. Relying on public AI models for sensitive matters poses significant risks of exposing core trade secrets or privileged client information. Organizations are advised to establish comprehensive internal compliance guidelines governing AI usage. These frameworks should clearly define acceptable use cases, ensuring a balance between operational efficiency and rigorous information security standards. By proactively managing these risks, rights holders can prevent inadvertent disclosures while leveraging technological advancements responsibly within their IP workflows.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["全球 AI 基础设施投资巨大但面临关键原材料短缺风险", "质疑当前免费服务模式背后的商业逻辑与可持续性", "强调 IP 领域使用 AI 需高度关注数据保密与泄露风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Huge AI infrastructure investments face supply chain risks like helium shortages and rising memory costs.", "Current free AI service models lack clear long-term business logic and raise sustainability concerns.", "IP practitioners must prioritize data confidentiality and mitigate leakage risks when adopting AI tools."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立严格的内部 AI 工具使用规范以保护商业秘密", "审慎评估公共 AI 模型在敏感 IP 业务中的适用性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish strict internal AI tool usage norms to protect trade secrets from unauthorized exposure.", "Practice note: Carefully assess the suitability of public AI models for handling sensitive intellectual property matters.", "What to monitor: Emerging regulatory requirements concerning data privacy and AI governance in IP contexts."]
core_points_en: Huge AI infrastructure investments face supply chain risks like helium shortages and rising memory costs.
Current free AI service models lack clear long-term business logic and raise sustainability concerns.
IP practitioners must prioritize data confidentiality and mitigate leakage risks when adopting AI tools.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish strict internal AI tool usage norms to protect trade secrets from unauthorized exposure.
Practice note: Carefully assess the suitability of public AI models for handling sensitive intellectual property matters.
What to monitor: Emerging regulatory requirements concerning data privacy and AI governance in IP contexts.

items.title: UK faces ‘systemic vulnerability’ in space technology gap
items.title_zh: 英国面临航天技术差距的系统性脆弱性
items.title_en: UK Space Sector Faces Systemic Vulnerability in Commercializing Academic Research
items.summary: As millions of people around the world watch the progress of Nasa’s Artemis II mission, an expert in the sector says it is vital for the UK to convert its academic ability into commercial success.
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文篇幅极短，仅包含标题与摘要。专家指出，随着NASA阿尔忒弥斯II号任务进展，英国亟需将学术界在航天领域的科研能力转化为商业成功，以应对该领域存在的系统性脆弱性与技术差距。
items.ai_summary_en: Amid global attention on NASA’s Artemis II mission, an industry expert highlights a critical gap in the United Kingdom’s space technology sector. The commentary identifies a systemic vulnerability stemming from the failure to effectively translate strong academic research capabilities into commercial success. While the UK possesses significant scholarly strength in space-related disciplines, this intellectual capital is not currently yielding proportional market outcomes or industrial leadership. The expert argues that bridging this divide is vital for the sector’s resilience and growth. This observation underscores the broader challenge of technology transfer within the UK innovation ecosystem, particularly in high-value, capital-intensive industries like space exploration and satellite technology.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This commentary highlights a structural challenge in the UK innovation landscape relevant to rights holders and investors. For European and US peers, it signals potential opportunities in licensing or partnering with UK academic institutions that hold under-monetized space technologies. It also suggests that policy interventions may target technology transfer mechanisms. Companies should assess the maturity of IP portfolios originating from UK universities, as these assets may be available for acquisition or collaboration at earlier stages than those in more commercially mature jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["专家称英国需将航天学术能力转化为商业成功", "标题显示英国在航天技术领域面临系统性脆弱性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Expert identifies systemic vulnerability in UK space technology due to poor commercialization of academic research.", "Converting scholarly capability into commercial success is deemed vital for the sector's future resilience."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注英国航天领域产学研转化的政策动向", "监测是否存在针对该技术领域的专利资助或快速审查机制", "等待更多关于“系统性脆弱性”的具体事实披露"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Identifies potential licensing or acquisition opportunities for under-commercialized space technologies held by UK academic institutions.", "Practice note: Highlights the importance of evaluating technology transfer efficiency when conducting due diligence on UK-based space sector IP portfolios.", "What to monitor: Watch for UK government or institutional initiatives aimed at improving the commercialization pipeline for academic space research outputs."]

Converting scholarly capability into commercial success is deemed vital for the sector's future resilience.

Practice note: Highlights the importance of evaluating technology transfer efficiency when conducting due diligence on UK-based space sector IP portfolios.

items.title: Innovations In Hair Removal: A Patent Perspective
items.title_zh: 脱毛技术创新：专利视角下的演进与布局
items.title_en: Hair Removal Innovations: Structural Patent Solutions for Lubrication Challenges
items.summary: In December 1901, King C. Gillette sent a patent application to the US Patent Office. This invention has changed the face of personal hygiene around the world.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文回顾吉列公司从早期刀片专利到现代脱毛技术的创新历程。重点剖析其针对润滑液输送受阻问题，通过结构设计而非化学改良获得的欧洲专利 EP3231564 B1。该专利实现了皮肤紧致与润滑剂输送功能的集成，展示了企业在成熟市场中利用专利解决具体技术瓶颈、构建高价值组合的策略。
items.ai_summary_en: This article reviews the evolution of hair removal technologies, tracing developments from early blade patents to modern systems. It focuses on European Patent EP3231564 B1, which addresses lubricant delivery obstruction through structural redesign rather than chemical modification. The patent integrates skin stretching and lubricant distribution functions using a flexible component with internal flow channels. This case illustrates how rights holders in mature markets leverage patents to resolve specific technical bottlenecks and build high-value intellectual property portfolios. The analysis highlights the strategic importance of mechanical innovations in overcoming limitations of traditional cartridge designs, ensuring effective fluid delivery to the cutting zone while maintaining skin tension for a closer shave.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国个护企业出海应重视欧洲专利布局，不仅关注化学成分，更需挖掘机械结构微创新。面对成熟市场巨头壁垒，建议针对特定痛点进行差异化研发并及时申请专利，以规避侵权风险并提升产品竞争力。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating in the personal care sector, this case underscores the value of pursuing structural patent protections alongside chemical formulations. In mature markets dominated by established players, focusing on mechanical micro-innovations can provide a viable pathway to differentiate products and navigate existing patent thickets. Rights holders should identify specific user pain points, such as inefficient lubricant delivery, and develop targeted structural solutions. Timely filing of such patents enhances product competitiveness and mitigates infringement risks. This approach demonstrates that even in saturated technology fields, incremental engineering improvements can yield significant intellectual property assets and commercial advantages.

items.title: Smarter Brand Protection: AI, Action And ROI
items.title_zh: 更智能的品牌保护：AI、行动与投资回报
items.title_en: Smarter Brand Protection: Leveraging AI, Enforcement Actions, and ROI Strategies
items.summary: Counterfeiting and online brand abuse are no longer isolated challenges — they are global, fast-moving, and increasingly sophisticated. From social media and marketplaces to offline distribution networks, brands today must navigate a complex and constantly evolving threat landscape.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文针对日益复杂的全球假冒与网络品牌滥用挑战，探讨如何利用 AI 技术转型品牌保护策略。内容涵盖从社交媒体到线下分销的全渠道监测，强调通过自动化工具提升侵权识别效率，并指导企业平衡线上线下执法资源以最大化投资回报，旨在将品牌保护转化为战略职能。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis addresses the escalating complexity of global counterfeiting and online brand abuse, exploring how artificial intelligence can transform brand protection strategies. It covers omnichannel monitoring spanning social media platforms to offline distribution networks, emphasizing the use of automated tools to enhance infringement detection efficiency. The content guides rights holders on balancing online and offline enforcement resources to maximize return on investment. Ultimately, it aims to reposition brand protection from a reactive cost center into a strategic, data-driven function capable of navigating sophisticated digital threats and protecting brand integrity across diverse markets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应加速部署 AI 驱动的品牌监测工具，应对高频网络侵权。建议建立 centralized 跨渠道响应机制，平衡法德等核心市场资源，将被动维权转为主动战略管理，降低声誉风险并提升执法效能。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should accelerate the deployment of AI-driven brand monitoring tools to address high-frequency online infringements effectively. Establishing a centralized, cross-channel response mechanism is critical for coordinating efforts across key jurisdictions such as France and Germany. By shifting from passive rights enforcement to active strategic management, companies can significantly reduce reputational risks while enhancing overall enforcement efficacy. This approach ensures that limited resources are directed toward high-impact actions, aligning brand protection activities with broader business objectives and improving measurable outcomes in complex digital ecosystems.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["利用 AI 技术实现大规模可疑列表自动检测与跨平台数据关联", "结合线上监控与线下调查及法律程序，构建全方位执法闭环", "通过精准定位高风险市场与重复侵权者，优化预算分配以提升 ROI"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Leverage AI technology for large-scale automatic detection of suspicious listings and cross-platform data correlation.", "Combine online monitoring with offline investigations and legal procedures to build a comprehensive enforcement loop.", "Optimize budget allocation by precisely targeting high-risk markets and repeat infringers to maximize ROI."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["引入 AI 自动化监测以应对海量电商及社交平台上的商标侵权行为", "统筹线上线下执法资源，针对重点法域制定高 ROI 的品牌保护预算"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Deploy AI automation to handle massive volumes of trademark infringements on e-commerce and social platforms.", "Practice note: Coordinate online and offline enforcement resources to formulate high-ROI brand protection budgets for key jurisdictions.", "What to monitor: Track the integration of AI detection tools and centralized response mechanisms for improved enforcement speed."]
core_points_en: Leverage AI technology for large-scale automatic detection of suspicious listings and cross-platform data correlation.
Combine online monitoring with offline investigations and legal procedures to build a comprehensive enforcement loop.
Optimize budget allocation by precisely targeting high-risk markets and repeat infringers to maximize ROI.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Deploy AI automation to handle massive volumes of trademark infringements on e-commerce and social platforms.
Practice note: Coordinate online and offline enforcement resources to formulate high-ROI brand protection budgets for key jurisdictions.
What to monitor: Track the integration of AI detection tools and centralized response mechanisms for improved enforcement speed.

items.title_en: EU Design Act: Reshaping the Future of Fashion and Furniture Protection
items.ai_summary_zh: 霍金路伟律所分析指出，即将实施的《欧盟外观设计法》将对时尚和家具行业产生深远影响。该法案旨在简化注册程序、增强设计保护力度并适应数字化趋势。文章强调了新法在提升确权效率及应对侵权挑战方面的关键作用，预示欧洲设计保护体系将迎来重大变革。
items.ai_summary_en: Hogan Lovells analyzes the imminent implementation of the EU Design Act, highlighting its profound impact on the fashion and furniture sectors. The legislation aims to streamline registration procedures, strengthen design protection, and adapt to digital trends. It emphasizes the new law's critical role in enhancing rights confirmation efficiency and addressing infringement challenges. This signals a major transformation for the European design protection system, offering improved legal certainty and faster processing times for rights holders operating within the EU market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国时尚与家具出口企业需密切关注新法过渡期安排，及时调整在欧知识产权布局策略。建议提前评估现有设计合规性，利用简化程序加快注册，并加强监测以防范潜在侵权风险，确保在欧洲市场的竞争优势。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor transitional arrangements under the new EU Design Act to adjust their intellectual property strategies accordingly. It is advisable to proactively assess the compliance of existing designs and utilize simplified procedures to accelerate registration processes. Strengthening monitoring mechanisms will help prevent potential infringement risks, ensuring sustained competitive advantages in the European market amidst these regulatory changes.

items.title: UKIPO fee rises from 1 April 2026 - GJE
items.title_zh: 英国知识产权局自 2026 年 4 月 1 日起上调官方费用
items.title_en: UKIPO Implements Significant Fee Increases Effective 1 April 2026
items.summary: The UKIPO’s official fees have increased from today and further guidance has now been provided on how the new fees will apply.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）自即日起将官方费用上调约 25%，旨在应对自上次调费以来累计约 32% 的通胀压力，并支持新系统投资。此次调整涵盖商标、外观设计及专利等领域，其中商标费率自 1998 年以来首次变动。律所 GJE 指出具体适用细则已发布，企业需关注新版收费标准以规划预算。
items.ai_summary_en: The United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) has implemented a substantial increase in official fees, rising by approximately 25% effective immediately. This adjustment aims to counteract the cumulative inflation of roughly 32% recorded since the previous fee revision and to fund ongoing investments in modernizing administrative systems. Notably, this marks the first change to trade mark fees since 1998, while design and patent fees are also affected. The UKIPO has published detailed guidance on how these new rates apply across various intellectual property procedures. Rights holders and applicants operating within the UK jurisdiction should review the updated fee schedule to ensure accurate financial planning for future filings, renewals, and maintenance of their IP portfolios.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英布局的中国企业需立即复核年度 IP 预算，特别是商标续展与新申请成本显著增加。建议提前评估存量资产价值，优化组合策略，避免因费用上涨导致非核心权利流失，同时关注后续程序性指引。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders with active UK portfolios should immediately reassess their annual intellectual property budgets to accommodate the higher costs associated with new applications and renewals. Given the magnitude of the increase, particularly for trade marks, it is prudent to conduct a strategic review of existing assets. Companies should evaluate the commercial value of each registered right to determine retention priorities, ensuring that non-core or underutilized rights do not incur unnecessary expenses. Proactive portfolio optimization will help mitigate budgetary impacts while maintaining protection for key brands and designs. Stakeholders must also stay alert for any further procedural updates from the office regarding implementation details.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO 官方费用整体上调约 25% 以抵消通胀影响", "商标费用自 1998 年来首次调整，涵盖多类知识产权", "新增收入将用于支持局方新系统建设与持续投资"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UKIPO official fees have increased by approximately 25% to offset significant inflationary pressures accumulated over recent years.", "Trade mark fees have been adjusted for the first time since 1998, alongside changes to design and patent application costs.", "Additional revenue generated will support the UKIPO's continued investment in upgrading its digital infrastructure and systems."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重新核算在英知识产权维护成本与预算分配", "评估存量商标与外观设计资产的保留优先级"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Recalculate UK IP maintenance costs and adjust annual budget allocations to reflect the 25% fee increase.", "Practice note: Evaluate the retention priority of existing trade mark and design assets to optimize portfolio efficiency.", "What to monitor: Watch for additional procedural guidance from the UKIPO regarding the specific application of new fee structures."]
core_points_en: UKIPO official fees have increased by approximately 25% to offset significant inflationary pressures accumulated over recent years.
Trade mark fees have been adjusted for the first time since 1998, alongside changes to design and patent application costs.
Additional revenue generated will support the UKIPO's continued investment in upgrading its digital infrastructure and systems.

items.title: Hoyng Rokh Monegier launches AI-powered UPC platform
items.title_zh: Hoyng Rokh Monegier推出AI驱动的UPC平台
items.title_en: Hoyng Rokh Monegier Launches AI-Driven Platform to Navigate Unified Patent Court Case Law
items.summary: IP boutique firm says its platform will help navigate ‘scattered’ decisions by bringing case law, commentary and research under one umbrella
items.ai_summary_zh: 知识产权精品律所Hoyng Rokh Monegier宣布推出人工智能驱动的统一专利法院（UPC）平台。该平台旨在整合判例法、评论与研究资源，帮助用户应对目前分散的UPC裁决信息，提供一站式导航服务。
items.ai_summary_en: The intellectual property boutique firm Hoyng Rokh Monegier has announced the launch of a specialized artificial intelligence-powered platform designed specifically for the Unified Patent Court. Addressing the significant challenge posed by fragmented and difficult-to-retrieve UPC decisions, this new digital tool aims to consolidate case law, expert commentary, and legal research into a single, accessible interface. By leveraging advanced AI technology, the platform provides a one-stop navigation service intended to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of legal practitioners handling cross-border patent disputes in Europe. This development responds directly to market needs for streamlined access to judicial precedents, offering users a comprehensive solution to manage the complexities associated with the emerging European patent litigation landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 随着UPC判例积累，法律科技工具成为提升检索效率的关键。需关注该平台对判例数据的覆盖范围及更新频率，以及AI技术在欧洲专利诉讼情报中的实际应用效果。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants involved in European patent litigation should evaluate such digital tools to potentially reduce compliance and research costs. Utilizing new platforms to track evolving UPC judicial tendencies can help optimize defense strategies and case preparation. However, rights holders must remain vigilant regarding opponents who may leverage these efficient AI tools to initiate more precise and targeted patent enforcement actions. Consequently, companies are advised to proactively strengthen their high-value patent portfolios and monitor technological advancements in legal research tools to maintain a competitive advantage in cross-border disputes.

items.title: Klaus Grabinski: “Economic logic suggests the UPC will continue to grow”
items.title_zh: Grabinski：经济逻辑表明UPC将持续增长
items.title_en: UPC President Grabinski: Economic Logic Indicates Continued Growth for the Unified Patent Court
items.summary: JUVE Patent: Mr Grabinski, you have now been President of the Unified Patent Court for over three years – initially during the preparatory phase and, since 1 June 2023, of the operational court. How satisfied are you with the current developement? Klaus Grabinski: I’m very pleased with how it has developed. It has undoubtedly been […] The post Klaus Grabinski: “Economic logic suggests the UPC will continue to grow” appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）主席Klaus Grabinski接受采访，回顾其任职三年来的工作。他表示对UPC自2023年6月1日正式运作以来的发展感到非常满意，并指出经济逻辑表明UPC将继续增长。原文仅提供访谈开头片段，未展示具体数据或详细论据。
items.ai_summary_en: In an interview with JUVE Patent, Klaus Grabinski, President of the Unified Patent Court (UPC), reflected on the court's development over its first three years. This period covers both the initial preparatory phase and the operational stage that began on June 1, 2023. Grabinski expressed strong satisfaction with the current trajectory, noting that the transition from preparation to full operation has been successful and aligns with expectations. He emphasized that underlying economic logic suggests the UPC will continue to expand in significance and caseload. The commentary confirms that the court has stabilized as a central forum for European patent litigation, moving beyond its initial setup challenges to become a functional and growing judicial institution within the European intellectual property landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文仅为访谈导语，缺乏具体案件数据或政策细节，目前仅能确认UPC高层对初期运营的积极态度。需等待完整访谈发布，以观察其关于案件数量、用户反馈或程序效率的具体论述，从而评估UPC的实际运行趋势。
items.ai_insight_en: The steady expansion of the Unified Patent Court signals an irreversible trend toward the centralization of European patent litigation. Market participants operating in Europe should reassess their patent portfolios and litigation strategies to account for this new jurisdictional reality. It is critical to familiarize teams with UPC procedural rules to mitigate risks associated with pan-European injunctions stemming from single proceedings. Conversely, rights holders can leverage the court’s efficient mechanisms for cross-border enforcement. Adapting to this centralized framework requires proactive compliance checks and strategic adjustments to manage potential exposure to broad injunctive relief across multiple member states simultaneously.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC主席Grabinski对法院自2023年6月运作以来的发展表示非常满意", "标题显示Grabinski认为经济逻辑表明UPC将持续增长"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC President expresses high satisfaction with the court's overall development during its first three years of existence.", "The institution has successfully transitioned from the preparatory phase to stable, full-scale operational status.", "Economic analysis predicts that the Unified Patent Court will experience continued growth in case volume and influence."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC高层对法院初期运营持积极肯定态度", "需关注后续完整内容中关于“经济增长”的具体依据", "暂无法从现有片段获取具体的诉讼统计或程序改进细节"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must adapt to the UPC's centralized jurisdiction by adjusting cross-border enforcement and defense strategies.", "Practice note: Conduct thorough pre-litigation compliance reviews to mitigate the risk of pan-European injunctions resulting from single actions.", "What to monitor: Track the evolution of UPC case law and procedural practices to anticipate shifts in litigation dynamics and enforcement efficiency."]

The institution has successfully transitioned from the preparatory phase to stable, full-scale operational status.

items.title: GlobalBlock adds world's two largest country domains—centralised brand protection in more than 750 web extensions
items.title_zh: GlobalBlock新增.cn与.de域名强化品牌保护
items.title_en: GlobalBlock Adds .cn and .de Domains, Expanding Centralized Brand Protection to Over 750 Extensions
items.summary: Addition of China's .cn and Germany's .de marks a major milestone in simplifying global IP protection, says Tony Kirsch of the Brand Safety Alliance.
items.ai_summary_zh: 品牌安全联盟（BSA）宣布将其GlobalBlock域名屏蔽服务扩展至中国.cn和德国.de顶级域名。此举旨在简化全球知识产权保护，帮助品牌以更低成本主动防御网络资产风险，覆盖超过750个网络扩展名。
items.ai_summary_en: The Brand Safety Alliance (BSA) has announced the integration of China’s .cn and Germany’s .de country code top-level domains into its GlobalBlock service. This expansion represents a significant milestone in simplifying global intellectual property protection strategies. By including the world’s two largest national domains, which collectively host over 30 million registered names, the service now covers more than 750 web extensions. This development allows rights holders to proactively defend their digital assets against infringement without incurring the substantial costs associated with defensive domain registrations or extensive litigation. The move aims to streamline online brand safety, enabling companies to manage protection across major digital markets efficiently and reliably.
items.ai_insight_zh: 随着.de等高价值国别域名纳入集中屏蔽体系，权利人可更高效地阻断侵权域名注册。需关注该商业保护机制在欧洲司法辖区对传统域名争议解决程序的补充作用及实际执行效力。
items.ai_insight_en: The inclusion of .de and .cn domains offers a cost-effective mechanism for blocking malicious cybersquatting in key European and Asian markets. Given the massive volume of .de registrations, rights holders with export operations should integrate this domain into their core monitoring frameworks. Utilizing centralized blocking services enhances proactive enforcement capabilities, allowing brands to secure their presence before disputes escalate. This approach minimizes exposure to passive litigation risks and reduces administrative burdens. Companies should evaluate how such consolidated tools fit within broader IP strategies to maintain robust defense against domain abuse across diverse jurisdictions.

items.title: Dior and L’Oréal fend off Chinese trademark challengers in Europe
items.title_zh: 迪奥与欧莱雅在欧洲成功阻击中国商标挑战者
items.title_en: Dior and L’Oréal Successfully Oppose Chinese Trademark Applications in the EU
items.summary: The French luxury and cosmetics groups both successfully opposed registrations that encroached on their signs in EU territories.
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国奢侈品集团迪奥与化妆品巨头欧莱雅在欧盟领土内，针对侵犯其标识的中国商标注册申请提出异议，并均获得成功。原文仅确认两家公司成功抵御了相关注册尝试，未披露具体案件细节、异议理由或涉及的具体商标图样。
items.ai_summary_en: French luxury group Dior and cosmetics giant L’Oréal have successfully opposed trademark registrations filed by Chinese applicants that encroached upon their established signs within EU territories. This development underscores the European market’s firm stance on protecting well-known brands, demonstrating that the European Union Intellectual Property Office maintains strict scrutiny during examination processes. These decisions effectively safeguard rights holders’ exclusive brand privileges across the pan-European region while curbing potential free-riding behaviors. The outcomes highlight a robust enforcement environment where established international brands can rely on rigorous administrative procedures to defend their intellectual property against unauthorized use or imitation by third parties seeking to capitalize on their reputation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息极简，仅能确认异议结果。需关注后续是否披露具体法律依据（如混淆可能性或声誉损害），以及EUIPO在此类跨境异议中的审查趋势。目前缺乏细节支持深入策略分析。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants seeking trademark protection in Europe must conduct comprehensive prior art searches to mitigate risks of conflict with existing well-known marks. When facing opposition proceedings, applicants should carefully evaluate the probability of success before committing substantial resources to litigation. It is advisable to enhance original design capabilities to reduce infringement disputes at the source. Establishing a compliant overseas intellectual property strategy is essential for navigating the strict regulatory environment. Rights holders should prioritize proactive monitoring and early intervention to protect their brand equity effectively against potential imitators in key jurisdictions.

The cases highlight strong protection for luxury and cosmetic brand signs within the European Union framework.
Chinese applicants face significant registration risks when encountering opposition from well-known rights holders.

items.title: How To Optimize Your Legal Department’s Work Thanks To External Trademark Search Solutions
items.title_zh: 借助外部商标检索方案优化法务部工作
items.title_en: Optimizing Legal Department Efficiency Through External Trademark Search Solutions
items.summary: If you are still conducting trademark searches in-house, you could be wasting your time and resources. Discover how external trademark search solutions can potentially save your business money while improving efficiency and search quality.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出企业内部进行商标检索存在效率低、专业性不足及缺乏国际视野等短板，可能导致注册被拒或权利受损。文章建议利用外部专业检索解决方案，以释放法务资源聚焦核心保护工作，提升检索质量与覆盖面，从而优化知识产权组合管理并降低长期成本。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis highlights the significant inefficiencies and professional limitations associated with conducting trademark searches entirely in-house. Relying on internal legal teams for such tasks often leads to incomplete results due to a lack of specialized tools and international expertise, potentially resulting in registration refusals or rights infringement. The article advocates for adopting external professional search solutions to enhance both the quality and coverage of trademark investigations. By outsourcing these labor-intensive tasks, companies can free up valuable legal resources to focus on core intellectual property protection strategies. This approach not only improves operational efficiency but also reduces long-term costs by minimizing the risks of portfolio damage caused by inadequate prior art checks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧拓展时，应避免依赖内部简易检索，需采购专业外部服务以规避跨国冲突风险。建议建立“内部初筛 + 外部深查”机制，确保商标在欧盟及成员国的注册安全，防止因检索疏漏导致品牌出海受阻或遭受侵权诉讼。
items.ai_insight_en: For market participants expanding into Europe, relying solely on basic internal screening is insufficient for mitigating cross-border conflict risks. Companies should adopt a hybrid model combining initial internal filtering with comprehensive external professional searches. This strategy ensures greater safety for trademark registrations across the European Union and its member states. It prevents brand expansion obstacles and potential infringement litigation arising from oversight. Establishing this collaborative mechanism helps secure intellectual property layouts in European markets, ensuring robust protection against complex local and regional trademark challenges.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["内部检索分散法务精力，阻碍其聚焦知识产权保护与演进核心职能", "内部团队常因缺乏专业工具与国际视野，导致检索不全引发注册风险", "外部专业方案能提升检索质量与效率，优化企业全球知识产权布局"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Internal trademark searches distract legal departments from their primary role of protecting and evolving the company's intellectual property assets.", "In-house teams often lack the necessary professional tools and global perspective, leading to incomplete searches that increase registration refusal risks.", "External professional solutions improve search quality and efficiency, optimizing global intellectual property layouts and reducing overall management costs."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["摒弃内部粗放检索，引入专业外部服务以规避欧盟商标注册驳回风险", "构建内外协同检索机制，确保中国品牌在欧洲市场的知识产权布局安全"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid reliance on rudimentary internal searches to prevent EU trademark registration rejections and protect brand integrity.", "Practice note: Implement a dual-layer system using internal preliminary screening followed by rigorous external professional verification.", "What to monitor: Ensure comprehensive coverage of EU and national databases to safeguard intellectual property layouts in European markets."]
core_points_en: Internal trademark searches distract legal departments from their primary role of protecting and evolving the company's intellectual property assets.
In-house teams often lack the necessary professional tools and global perspective, leading to incomplete searches that increase registration refusal risks.
External professional solutions improve search quality and efficiency, optimizing global intellectual property layouts and reducing overall management costs.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid reliance on rudimentary internal searches to prevent EU trademark registration rejections and protect brand integrity.
Practice note: Implement a dual-layer system using internal preliminary screening followed by rigorous external professional verification.
What to monitor: Ensure comprehensive coverage of EU and national databases to safeguard intellectual property layouts in European markets.

items.title: Intellectual Property Records: Avoid These 5 Common Irregular Requests For Recordals
items.title_zh: 知识产权备案：避免五种常见的不规范请求
items.title_en: EU Trademark Recordals: Avoiding Five Common Irregular Requests and Compliance Risks
items.summary: Intellectual property record updates are essential for ensuring that the details of IP are accurately maintained and that owners can freely exercise their rights. Consulting IP professionals for advice on a recordals project is the most effective way to ensure its successful completion. However, owners regularly request some irregular things for IP recordals.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出企业在进行欧盟知识产权备案更新时常犯的五类错误，包括推迟备案、等待续展时再处理、仅选择部分资产更新等。这些看似节省成本的做法实则可能导致权利行使受阻、费用增加甚至保护范围缩减。文章强调应及时、全面地完成备案变更，以确保持有人能自由行使权利并避免后续法律陷阱。
items.ai_summary_en: Maintaining accurate intellectual property records at the EUIPO is critical for rights holders to freely exercise their rights. This analysis highlights five common irregular requests that often lead to complications, including delaying recordals, waiting until renewal dates, and updating only selected assets. While these approaches may seem cost-effective initially, they frequently result in increased official and agent fees, procedural difficulties, and diminished legal protection. The article emphasizes that postponing necessary changes or selectively updating portfolios can jeopardize IP rights and create significant compliance issues. To avoid these avoidable traps, companies should prioritize timely and comprehensive updates rather than deferring actions. Engaging IP professionals early ensures successful completion of recordal projects, safeguarding the integrity of the IP portfolio and preventing future legal obstacles associated with incomplete or delayed administrative changes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧管理 IP 组合时，应摒弃“拖延省钱”思维，建立即时备案机制。针对并购或更名场景，需评估“全有或全无”的地域规则，避免因选择性申报导致权利瑕疵，建议聘请当地专业机构统筹全局。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders managing EU portfolios, particularly following mergers or corporate restructurings, relying on a 'delay to save' strategy creates significant exposure. Immediate and comprehensive recordal mechanisms are essential to prevent gaps in rights enforcement. Stakeholders must evaluate jurisdiction-specific requirements, such as 'all-or-nothing' territorial rules, to avoid partial filings that result in defective titles. Proactive coordination with local IP counsel ensures that global changes are accurately reflected across all relevant jurisdictions, mitigating the risk of unenforceable rights and costly remedial procedures later in the lifecycle of the trademark.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["推迟备案往往导致流程更复杂且官方与代理费用更高", "等待续展期再更新记录可能削弱知识产权保护效力", "部分资产更新在关联商标或全境要求下可能引发合规灾难"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Delaying recordals increases complexity and costs, leading to higher official and agent fees compared to immediate updates.", "Waiting until renewal periods to update records risks weakening IP protection and may jeopardize the validity of rights.", "Selective asset updates can cause compliance failures, especially where full territorial coverage or linked marks are required."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立即时备案机制，避免因拖延导致权利行使受阻", "跨境变更需统筹全量资产，防范选择性申报合规风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Immediate recordals prevent enforcement gaps and reduce long-term administrative costs for portfolio managers.", "Practice note: Coordinate cross-border changes holistically to avoid compliance risks from selective or partial asset declarations.", "What to monitor: Jurisdictional nuances regarding mandatory full-territory updates and strict deadlines for recording ownership transfers."]
core_points_en: Delaying recordals increases complexity and costs, leading to higher official and agent fees compared to immediate updates.
Waiting until renewal periods to update records risks weakening IP protection and may jeopardize the validity of rights.
Selective asset updates can cause compliance failures, especially where full territorial coverage or linked marks are required.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Immediate recordals prevent enforcement gaps and reduce long-term administrative costs for portfolio managers.
Practice note: Coordinate cross-border changes holistically to avoid compliance risks from selective or partial asset declarations.
What to monitor: Jurisdictional nuances regarding mandatory full-territory updates and strict deadlines for recording ownership transfers.

items.title: Patent Litigation in Practice Series: Spotlight on Belgium: Another Belgian Court Decision under the SPC Manufacturing Waiver
items.title_zh: 比利时法院系列判决：细化 SPC 制造豁免的通知义务与适用范围
items.title_en: Belgian Courts Clarify SPC Manufacturing Waiver: Notification Requirements and Stockpiling Scope
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析比利时企业法庭近期关于欧盟补充保护证书（SPC）制造豁免的三起判例。法院裁定，豁免通知无需在提交时包含出口国药品上市许可号或具体目的地，仅需在公开后补充；确认了为欧盟内上市而进行的合理期限库存行为合法；并初步涉及欧洲经济区（如冰岛）企业适用该豁免的资格争议，明确了立法避免披露商业敏感信息的意图。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines recent Belgian Enterprise Court decisions regarding the application of the EU Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) manufacturing waiver. The courts ruled that waiver notifications do not require immediate disclosure of foreign marketing authorization numbers or specific export destinations at the time of filing; these details need only be provided once publicly available. Furthermore, the judgments confirm the legality of stockpiling products within a reasonable period prior to SPC expiry for subsequent EU launch. The rulings also address preliminary questions concerning the eligibility of entities in European Economic Area (EEA) states, such as Iceland, to utilize the waiver. These decisions reinforce the legislative intent to balance IP rights with market access by avoiding mandatory disclosure of commercially sensitive information during the initial notification phase.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企利用欧盟 SPC 豁免机制出口或备货时，应确保提前三个月提交通知，但无需过度担忧初期无法提供境外许可号。需密切关注比利时等成员国对“合理库存期”及 EEA 非欧盟成员（如冰岛）主体资格的司法界定，优化合规策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and generic manufacturers utilizing the SPC manufacturing waiver must ensure notifications are submitted at least three months prior to manufacturing. However, parties should note that initial notifications need not contain foreign marketing authorization numbers, lowering early compliance barriers. It is critical to monitor how Belgian courts define 'reasonable' stockpiling periods and whether entities in non-EU EEA states, such as Iceland, qualify for the waiver. Litigation risks remain regarding the scope of eligible applicants and the precise timing for updating notification details, requiring careful strategic planning for both export and EU market entry strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["SPC 豁免通知无需即时提供出口国上市许可号或具体出口目的地", "法院允许为欧盟首发而在 SPC 到期前进行合理期限的库存储备", "明确立法本意是避免强制披露商业敏感信息以平衡各方利益"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Waiver notifications do not require immediate inclusion of foreign marketing authorization numbers or specific export destination countries.", "Stockpiling activities conducted for a reasonable period before SPC expiry are permitted for subsequent launch within the European Union.", "Legislative intent prioritizes avoiding forced disclosure of commercially sensitive information to balance competing stakeholder interests."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用豁免机制时，初期通知可暂缺境外许可号，降低合规门槛", "需警惕针对非欧盟 EEA 国家主体适用豁免权的潜在诉讼风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Initial waiver notifications may omit foreign marketing authorization numbers, reducing immediate administrative burdens while maintaining regulatory compliance.", "Practice note: Monitor judicial interpretations of 'reasonable' stockpiling durations and verify eligibility for entities based in non-EU EEA member states like Iceland.", "What to monitor: Potential litigation risks concerning the applicability of the waiver to non-EU EEA subjects and evolving standards for notification updates."]
core_points_en: Waiver notifications do not require immediate inclusion of foreign marketing authorization numbers or specific export destination countries.
Stockpiling activities conducted for a reasonable period before SPC expiry are permitted for subsequent launch within the European Union.
Legislative intent prioritizes avoiding forced disclosure of commercially sensitive information to balance competing stakeholder interests.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Initial waiver notifications may omit foreign marketing authorization numbers, reducing immediate administrative burdens while maintaining regulatory compliance.
Practice note: Monitor judicial interpretations of 'reasonable' stockpiling durations and verify eligibility for entities based in non-EU EEA member states like Iceland.
What to monitor: Potential litigation risks concerning the applicability of the waiver to non-EU EEA subjects and evolving standards for notification updates.

items.title: Everyday IP: Easter and the economics of commercial distinctiveness
items.title_zh: 日常 IP：复活节与商业显著性的经济学
items.title_en: Easter IP: Balancing Public Domain Heritage with Commercial Distinctiveness and Protection
items.summary: Explore how Intellectual Property law interacts with Easter folklore to support products in competitive markets, from egg-dispensers to iconic chocolate bunnies.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析复活节符号作为公共领域文化遗产与商业品牌之间的界限。虽然兔子、彩蛋等概念不可被垄断，但企业可通过专利保护独特的机械装置（如自动分发器），或利用商标区分具体产品表达。文章强调法律不保护节日概念本身，而是保护企业在共有文化空间中的差异化创新表达，以平衡商业利益与文化传承。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the legal boundaries between Easter symbols as public domain cultural heritage and commercial branding strategies. While fundamental concepts such as rabbits and eggs cannot be monopolized, companies can secure exclusive market positions by patenting unique mechanical structures, such as automatic dispensers, or using trademarks to distinguish specific product expressions. The article emphasizes that intellectual property law does not protect the holiday concept itself. Instead, it safeguards differentiated innovative expressions within shared cultural spaces. This approach helps consumers identify brands among similar goods while balancing commercial interests with the preservation of traditional cultural elements.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业在开发节日类产品时，应避免直接模仿通用符号，转而聚焦独特外观设计与功能结构的专利布局。同时需警惕将公共文化元素注册为商标的风险，确保在尊重传统的前提下实现商业差异化，规避侵权纠纷。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants developing seasonal products should avoid directly mimicking generic symbols. Instead, focus on patent layouts for unique aesthetic designs and functional structures. There is a significant risk when attempting to register public cultural elements as trademarks. Companies must ensure commercial differentiation respects traditional boundaries to avoid infringement disputes. By prioritizing distinct technical and design innovations over common cultural motifs, rights holders can build compliant competitive advantages while minimizing legal exposure in crowded seasonal markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["复活节符号属公共领域，任何组织无法垄断兔子或彩蛋等基础概念", "企业可利用专利保护独特的节日产品机械结构，确立市场独占权", "知识产权法旨在保护具体表达形式，帮助消费者在同类商品中识别品牌"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Easter symbols belong to the public domain, meaning no organization can monopolize basic concepts like rabbits or eggs.", "Companies can utilize patents to protect unique mechanical structures in festive products, establishing market exclusivity.", "Intellectual property law protects specific expression forms, helping consumers identify brands among similar commodity offerings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["聚焦产品独特结构与外观设计申请专利，避免陷入通用符号争议", "尊重公共文化元素边界，通过差异化表达构建合规的商业竞争优势"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Focus on patenting unique product structures and aesthetics to avoid disputes over generic symbols.", "Practice note: Respect public cultural element boundaries by building compliant commercial advantages through differentiated expression.", "What to monitor: Risks associated with registering public cultural elements as trademarks and potential infringement liabilities."]
core_points_en: Easter symbols belong to the public domain, meaning no organization can monopolize basic concepts like rabbits or eggs.
Companies can utilize patents to protect unique mechanical structures in festive products, establishing market exclusivity.
Intellectual property law protects specific expression forms, helping consumers identify brands among similar commodity offerings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Focus on patenting unique product structures and aesthetics to avoid disputes over generic symbols.
Practice note: Respect public cultural element boundaries by building compliant commercial advantages through differentiated expression.
What to monitor: Risks associated with registering public cultural elements as trademarks and potential infringement liabilities.

items.title: The UPC Intelligence Platform
items.title_zh: HOYNG 推出统一专利法院智能情报平台
items.title_en: HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER Launches Free UPC Intelligence Platform for Structured Case Law Search
items.summary: HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER has launched the UPC Intelligence Platform, a free and publicly accessible search tool for Unified Patent Court case law.
items.ai_summary_zh: HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER 律所推出免费公开的“统一专利法院（UPC）情报平台”。该工具由资深律师开发，旨在解决 UPC 判决虽公开但难以结构化检索的痛点。平台集成可搜索的案例数据库、AI 辅助研究工具及专业评论，为从业者提供高效的判例法访问途径，并承诺持续更新以服务于更广泛的 UPC 社区。
items.ai_summary_en: HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER has introduced the UPC Intelligence Platform, a free and publicly accessible search tool designed to streamline access to Unified Patent Court case law. Developed by senior associate Akiva Friedmann, the platform addresses the practical challenge that while UPC decisions are public, they lack structured and efficient search capabilities. Originally an internal project to enhance accessibility of Prof. Willem Hoyng’s commentaries, it has evolved into a comprehensive resource featuring a searchable database of decisions, AI-supported research tools, and direct links to expert commentary. This initiative aims to facilitate easier access to legal materials for practitioners, clients, and stakeholders interested in the Court’s evolving jurisprudence. The platform is offered at no cost and is intended as a dynamic resource for the broader UPC community, with plans for continuous development and updates over time.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临专利诉讼风险增加，此工具降低了跟踪 UPC 判例的门槛。建议利用该平台监控竞争对手诉讼策略及法院裁判倾向，优化海外专利布局与应诉准备，提升在欧洲市场的知识产权合规与防御能力。
items.ai_insight_en: This new resource lowers the barrier for monitoring UPC jurisprudence, which is increasingly critical given the rising volume of patent litigation in Europe. Rights holders and corporate counsel can leverage this platform to track competitor litigation strategies and identify emerging judicial trends without incurring additional costs. By utilizing these data insights, companies can refine their European patent portfolios and strengthen their defense preparations. The availability of structured case law supports more informed decision-making regarding enforcement actions and compliance measures, ultimately enhancing intellectual property resilience and strategic positioning within the European market.

items.title: De Super Bowl, De Olympische Spelen En Het WK: Hoe Scoor Je Zonder Inbreuk Te Maken Op Handelsmerken?
items.title_zh: 超级碗与奥运会：如何在不侵犯商标权下进行营销
items.title_en: Super Bowl, Olympics, and World Cup: Marketing Without Trademark Infringement
items.summary: De grootste sportevenementen, waaronder de Super Bowl, de Olympische Spelen en het WK Voetbal zijn enorme aandachtstrekkers. Slechts een selecte groep bedrijven mag zich ‘official partner’ of ‘sponsor’ van zo’n evenement noemen.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析企业在超级碗、奥运会等大型赛事期间进行“伏击营销”的商标侵权风险。依据比荷卢知识产权公约及欧盟商标条例，赛事名称多为知名注册商标。若商业使用导致混淆或不当利用声誉（如 L'Oréal 案确立原则），即便无直接竞争亦构成侵权。仅纯信息性引用通常被允许，但促销性使用风险极高。
items.ai_summary_en: Major sporting events like the Super Bowl, Olympic Games, and FIFA World Cup attract significant public attention. Only a select group of companies holds official sponsorship rights. Other market participants often face temptation to leverage this exposure through thematic products or social media campaigns, risking ambush marketing claims. This analysis examines trademark infringement risks under the Benelux Convention on Intellectual Property and the EU Trade Mark Regulation. Event names are registered trademarks with extensive licensing programs. Rights holders can oppose use that causes confusion or unfairly exploits the reputation of well-known marks, even without direct competition. While purely informational references, such as stating business hours during an event, are generally permissible, commercial or promotional use carries high legal risk. Companies must carefully distinguish between factual information and unauthorized commercial exploitation to avoid liability.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧借势大型赛事营销时，须严守商标边界。避免在广告、产品名中直接使用赛事标识或暗示官方关联，以防因“搭便车”被诉侵权。建议仅做事实性营业说明，并提前开展商标合规排查。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants engaging in comparative advertising or event-related promotions must strictly adhere to trademark boundaries. Direct use of event identifiers or implications of official association in advertisements and product names creates substantial liability for unfair advantage. Legal practice suggests limiting communications to factual operational statements, such as opening hours, rather than leveraging event prestige for brand enhancement. Prior to launching campaigns, comprehensive trademark clearance searches are essential. This approach mitigates the risk of enforcement actions by rights holders who actively protect their brands against ambush marketing strategies across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["赛事名称属知名商标，受欧盟及比荷卢法律严格保护", "非赞助商若不当利用赛事声誉获利，即便无混淆也构成侵权", "纯信息性引用合法，但带有促销性质的商业使用风险巨大"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Event names are well-known registered trademarks strictly protected under EU and Benelux intellectual property laws.", "Non-sponsors exploiting event reputation for commercial gain may infringe trademarks even without consumer confusion.", "Purely informational references are legally permissible, but promotional commercial use entails significant infringement risks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严禁在非授权产品中直接使用赛事名称或标识进行促销", "营销活动需区分信息告知与商业蹭热点，避免不当得利指控"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid using event names or logos in non-authorized products to prevent unfair advantage allegations.", "Practice note: Clearly distinguish between factual information provision and commercial exploitation of event popularity.", "What to monitor: Active enforcement trends by major sports organizations regarding ambush marketing and trademark misuse."]
core_points_en: Event names are well-known registered trademarks strictly protected under EU and Benelux intellectual property laws.
Non-sponsors exploiting event reputation for commercial gain may infringe trademarks even without consumer confusion.
Purely informational references are legally permissible, but promotional commercial use entails significant infringement risks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid using event names or logos in non-authorized products to prevent unfair advantage allegations.
Practice note: Clearly distinguish between factual information provision and commercial exploitation of event popularity.
What to monitor: Active enforcement trends by major sports organizations regarding ambush marketing and trademark misuse.

items.title: Cut Intellectual Project Cost And Reduce Inefficiency: What Can In-House Legal Teams Do?
items.title_zh: 降低知识产权项目成本与提升内部法务效率的策略
items.title_en: Strategies for Reducing IP Project Costs and Enhancing In-House Legal Efficiency
items.summary: There is always pressure on intellectual property costs. Indeed, an in-house legal team is often considered solely as a cost center, which can make budgeting discussions challenging. However, there are several ways a legal team can demonstrate its value to a business—and, potentially, some easy ways to cut costs and improve efficiency within the legal department.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文针对欧洲企业内部法务团队面临的预算压力，提出三步降本增效策略：首先通过基准测试评估外部律师费用与内部人力配置；其次采取行动，如重新招标或引入专业服务商替代不必要的外部 counsel；最后建立关键绩效指标以量化团队表现，优化资源配置，从而证明法务部门的商业价值。
items.ai_summary_en: European in-house legal teams frequently face significant budgetary pressures, often being viewed strictly as cost centers. This article outlines a three-step strategy to demonstrate value while reducing inefficiencies. First, conduct benchmarking assessments to identify excessive external counsel fees and internal operational bottlenecks. Second, take decisive action by renegotiating rates or issuing requests for proposals to secure more cost-effective legal services. Third, establish key performance indicators to quantify team performance, ensuring high-value staff focus on strategic tasks rather than routine administration. These measures help optimize resource allocation, control operational expenditures, and clearly articulate the commercial contribution of the legal department to broader business objectives.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧设立法务部时，应摒弃单纯视其为成本中心的观念。建议定期审查外部律师账单，对常规 IP 管理事务采用专业化外包，并建立内部 KPI 考核机制，以提升整体合规效率并控制运营成本。
items.ai_insight_en: Legal departments should move beyond the perception of being mere cost centers by actively managing expenditure structures. Regular audits of external lawyer billing are essential to identify savings opportunities. Routine intellectual property management tasks should be outsourced to specialized providers offering better cost-efficiency. Furthermore, implementing robust internal key performance indicator mechanisms allows teams to measure compliance efficiency accurately. This approach ensures that senior legal talent is reserved for complex, high-value strategic matters, thereby controlling overall operational costs while enhancing the department's strategic impact within the organization.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["开展知识产权成本管理评估，识别外部服务溢价与内部低效环节", "通过重新招标或引入专业供应商，替代高成本的外部法律顾问服务", "建立关键绩效指标体系，量化内部团队表现以优化高阶任务分配"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Conduct intellectual property cost management assessments to identify external service premiums and internal inefficiencies.", "Replace high-cost external counsel with specialized vendors or through competitive re-tendering processes.", "Establish key performance indicators to quantify internal team performance and optimize high-value task allocation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["定期审查外部律师费用结构，对基础 IP 事务寻求更具性价比的专业替代方案", "建立内部法务绩效考核指标，将高薪人力聚焦于高价值战略任务而非基础操作"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Regularly review external lawyer fee structures to seek cost-effective professional alternatives for basic IP matters.", "Practice note: Establish internal legal performance assessment metrics to focus high-salary personnel on high-value strategic tasks.", "What to monitor: Monitor the balance between external counsel spending and internal process automation to maintain budget efficiency."]
core_points_en: Conduct intellectual property cost management assessments to identify external service premiums and internal inefficiencies.
Replace high-cost external counsel with specialized vendors or through competitive re-tendering processes.
Establish key performance indicators to quantify internal team performance and optimize high-value task allocation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Regularly review external lawyer fee structures to seek cost-effective professional alternatives for basic IP matters.
Practice note: Establish internal legal performance assessment metrics to focus high-salary personnel on high-value strategic tasks.
What to monitor: Monitor the balance between external counsel spending and internal process automation to maintain budget efficiency.

items.title: Mewburn Ellis continues litigation and prosecution push with partnership additions
items.title_zh: Mewburn Ellis借合伙人增补推进诉讼与申请业务
items.title_en: Mewburn Ellis Expands Partnership to Strengthen Litigation and UPC Capabilities
items.summary: The latest round of promotions has contributed to a 21% rise in partner headcount in the past two years, with business leaders eyeing litigation and the UPC
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，英国律所Mewburn Ellis通过最新一轮晋升，在过去两年内将合伙人人数增加了21%。企业管理层明确表示，此次人员扩张旨在加强诉讼业务能力，并重点关注统一专利法院（UPC）相关的法律事务机会。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK-based intellectual property firm Mewburn Ellis has achieved a 21 percent increase in its partner headcount over the past two years through a series of recent promotions. This strategic expansion is explicitly designed to enhance the firm's service capabilities in patent litigation and before the Unified Patent Court (UPC). The move reflects heightened market attention toward this emerging judicial jurisdiction and underscores the firm's proactive strategic positioning. By bolstering its leadership team, Mewburn Ellis aims to address the growing demand for specialized legal services in the evolving European patent landscape, signaling a robust response to the operational changes introduced by the new court system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息有限，仅能确认该律所正通过增加合伙人资源来布局UPC诉讼市场。需持续观察其后续在UPC的具体案件参与情况及团队专业领域分布，以评估其对欧洲专利诉讼格局的实际影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe face an increasingly professional and competitive litigation environment following the establishment of the Unified Patent Court. Rights holders should proactively assess potential risks associated with UPC proceedings and secure necessary resources for effective case management. It is advisable to monitor the activities of law firms specializing in this domain to optimize both defensive and offensive intellectual property strategies. Aligning legal support with the specific procedural nuances of the new court will be critical for navigating cross-border disputes efficiently.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["过去两年间，该律所合伙人总数实现了21%的增长", "业务领导层将诉讼业务及统一专利法院（UPC）作为重点发展方向"]

Strategic focus clearly targets patent litigation and Unified Patent Court related business operations.
