items.title: The IP Lounge: Recognising 30 years of the EUTM
items.title_zh: IP Lounge：纪念欧盟商标制度三十周年
items.title_en: EUIPO Marks Thirty Years of the European Union Trade Mark System
items.summary: João Negrão, EUIPO executive director, is joined by a seasoned official to reflect on three decades of stories
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）将于4月1日庆祝欧盟商标（EUTM）注册开放三十周年。尽管相关法规于1994年确立，但申请通道直至1996年4月1日才正式开启。EUIPO执行主任João Negrão与资深官员Jacqueline Winkelmolen通过播客回顾了这一历程，探讨了从纸质申请到数字化管理的演变，以及该局后续在设计及地理标志领域职权的扩展。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) will celebrate the thirtieth anniversary of the European Union Trade Mark (EUTM) system on April 1, 2026. Although the governing regulation was adopted in 1994, the EUTM right officially became available to applicants on April 1, 1996. This milestone highlights three decades of simplified trademark protection across Europe. In a commemorative podcast, EUIPO Executive Director João Negrão and team leader Jacqueline Winkelmolen reflect on the office’s evolution. They discuss the transition from early paper-based applications to modern digital processes, assess whether initial demand exceeded predictions, and review the expansion of EUIPO competencies to include designs and geographical indications.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This retrospective underscores the EUTM’s role in harmonizing European brand protection. For rights holders, the anniversary highlights the stability and maturity of the unitary right. The expansion into designs and GIs suggests a broader strategic view of IP assets within the EU framework. Practitioners should note the institutional memory regarding application volume trends, which may inform future capacity planning. Monitoring upcoming EUIPO publications associated with this milestone could reveal valuable historical data on filing behaviors and sector-specific growth, aiding long-term portfolio strategy and regional market assessment.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟商标（EUTM）申请系统于1996年4月1日正式向申请人开放，此前1994年法规已…", "EUIPO执行主任与资深官员通过播客回顾三十年发展，包括早期纸质申请处理及需求超出预期…", "EUIPO在成立后逐步扩展职权范围，目前已涵盖外观设计及地理标志的管理与注册工作"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUTM system officially opened for applications on April 1, 1996, thirty years after its regulatory establishment.", "EUIPO leadership reflects on the operational shift from paper-based filings to digital management over three decades.", "The office has expanded its mandate beyond trademarks to include registered designs and geographical indications."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["EUIPO职权已从单一商标扩展至设计与地理标志，反映欧盟知识产权一体化管理趋势", "三十周年节点可能伴随官方数据发布，可作为评估欧盟商标申请趋势的历史参照", "需持续观察EUIPO在数字化转型后的服务效率变化及对新兴知识产权问题的应对策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: The mature EUTM system offers predictable, unitary protection, supporting streamlined brand management strategies across the European Union.", "Practice note: Historical reflections on application volumes and processing shifts provide context for understanding current EUIPO operational efficiencies and service expectation…", "What to monitor: Watch for anniversary-related statistical reports or policy statements from EUIPO that may signal future priorities in digital services or non-trademark IP rights."]
core_points_en: The EUTM system officially opened for applications on April 1, 1996, thirty years after its regulatory establishment.
EUIPO leadership reflects on the operational shift from paper-based filings to digital management over three decades.
The office has expanded its mandate beyond trademarks to include registered designs and geographical indications.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: The mature EUTM system offers predictable, unitary protection, supporting streamlined brand management strategies across the European Union.
Practice note: Historical reflections on application volumes and processing shifts provide context for understanding current EUIPO operational efficiencies and service expectation…
What to monitor: Watch for anniversary-related statistical reports or policy statements from EUIPO that may signal future priorities in digital services or non-trademark IP rights.

items.title: KeeeXed Out: The UPC’s Long Arm Stops at the Line
items.title_zh: 被排除的 KeeeX：统一专利法院长臂管辖止步于边界
items.title_en: UPC Appeal Court Limits Jurisdiction: Long Arm Stops at Member State Borders
items.summary: A ball may reach the line and still not cross it. Jurisdiction obeys the same logic.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在 Adobe 诉 KeeeX 案中作出裁决，推翻巴黎地方法院此前将管辖权扩展至瑞士、英国等非成员国的认定。法院明确，依据《布鲁塞尔条例》第 7(2) 条建立的管辖权仅限于 UPC 领土内的损害，不能转化为普遍性的长臂管辖工具，重申了国际管辖权的地理界限。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal reversed a Paris Local Division decision that had extended jurisdiction to non-member states including Switzerland and the UK. The appellate court clarified that jurisdiction under Article 7(2) of the Brussels I bis Regulation applies strictly to damage occurring within UPC member states. This ruling prevents the UPC from functioning as a forum for universal long-arm jurisdiction over global digital infringements. By distinguishing internal competence from international jurisdiction, the court reaffirmed that national private international law rules cannot expand the UPC’s reach beyond its territorial mandate. The decision reinforces strict geographical boundaries for patent litigation within the unified system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对专利诉讼时，需精准识别被告住所地与侵权发生地。对于非 UPC 成员国市场的指控，可利用此判例抗辩管辖权扩张，避免在非目标市场陷入被动诉讼，优化全球应诉策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment provides critical clarity for rights holders and defendants regarding the geographical scope of UPC proceedings. Parties must precisely identify the defendant’s domicile and the specific location where alleged patent damage occurs. Claims targeting markets outside UPC member states, such as the UK or Switzerland, face significant jurisdictional hurdles under this precedent. Litigants can leverage this distinction to challenge expansive jurisdictional assertions, ensuring disputes remain confined to appropriate forums. This supports more predictable global enforcement strategies and helps avoid costly procedural battles in unintended jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉庭裁定基于侵权地的管辖权仅限统一专利法院成员国境内", "澄清内部管辖与国际管辖区别，否定法国国内法作为管辖依据", "确认数字可访问性足以建立内部管辖，但不扩展国际管辖范围"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Jurisdiction based on place of infringement is strictly limited to damages occurring within UPC member states.", "Internal competence differs from international jurisdiction; French domestic law cannot serve as a basis for expanding reach.", "Digital accessibility suffices for internal competence but does not extend international jurisdiction to non-member markets."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用管辖界限抗辩，阻断针对非欧盟市场的长臂管辖企图", "区分数字可访问性与实际损害地，精准制定跨境诉讼防御"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Use this precedent to contest jurisdiction over non-UPC member state markets and limit exposure.", "Practice note: Distinguish between digital accessibility establishing local competence and actual damage sites defining international jurisdiction.", "What to monitor: How lower divisions apply this separation of internal competence and international jurisdiction in future digital cases."]
core_points_en: Jurisdiction based on place of infringement is strictly limited to damages occurring within UPC member states.
Internal competence differs from international jurisdiction; French domestic law cannot serve as a basis for expanding reach.
Digital accessibility suffices for internal competence but does not extend international jurisdiction to non-member markets.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Use this precedent to contest jurisdiction over non-UPC member state markets and limit exposure.
Practice note: Distinguish between digital accessibility establishing local competence and actual damage sites defining international jurisdiction.
What to monitor: How lower divisions apply this separation of internal competence and international jurisdiction in future digital cases.

items.title: Is Your Brand Name Too "Helpful" For Trademark Protection? Navigating The New CP16
items.title_zh: 品牌名称过于“直白”难获保护？解析商标新共同实践 CP16
items.title_en: Is Your Brand Name Too Descriptive? Navigating EUIPO Common Practice CP16
items.summary: When business owners and brand experts brainstorm for a name or sign for a new product or service, their instinct is often to be as clear as possible.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文解析欧盟知识产权局推出的第 16 号共同实践报告（CP16），重点阐述商标描述性原则。报告指出，若标志直接描述商品或服务的主题内容（如种类、领域），将被视为缺乏显著性而无法注册。文章以“葡萄酒”应用为例，说明过于直观的名称易被判定为描述特征而非来源标识，强调法律早期介入的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the European Union Intellectual Property Office’s Common Practice 16 (CP16), which clarifies the application of descriptiveness grounds for trademark refusal. The report emphasizes that signs directly describing the subject matter, key elements, or thematic content of goods and services lack distinctive character and are ineligible for registration. Using a mobile application named 'WINE' as a primary example, the text illustrates how such marks are perceived by the relevant public as descriptive of the app's content rather than as indicators of commercial origin. Consequently, overly literal brand names, while beneficial for search engine optimization, face significant legal hurdles. The article underscores the critical need for early legal assessment during brand creation to ensure compliance with EU trademark standards and avoid rejection based on absolute grounds for refusal.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局商标时，应避免使用直接描述产品功能或内容的名称。建议在品牌创设初期引入专业法律评估，确保名称具有足够显著性，防止因触犯描述性禁令而丧失保护机会，降低市场进入风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should avoid selecting core brand identifiers that directly describe product functions, content, or intended purpose. Such descriptive terms fail to distinguish goods in the marketplace and are routinely rejected under EU trademark law. It is advisable to conduct comprehensive legal distinctiveness assessments during the initial naming phase. This proactive approach balances marketing objectives, such as search engine optimization, with the statutory requirements for trademark registrability. Early intervention helps mitigate the risk of costly rebranding efforts and ensures robust intellectual property protection upon market entry into the European Union.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CP16 明确商标不得直接描述商品服务的关键要素或主题内容", "公众若将标志直接联想为产品内容而非来源，则判定为缺乏显著性", "过于清晰直白的品牌名虽利搜索，但极易导致商标注册申请被驳回"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["CP16 prohibits trademarks that directly describe key elements, subject matter, or thematic content of the designated goods and services.", "Signs perceived by the public as describing product content rather than indicating commercial origin are deemed devoid of distinctive character.", "Overly clear brand names facilitate search visibility but frequently result in trademark registration refusals due to descriptiveness."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免使用直接描述产品内容或功能的词汇作为核心品牌标识", "在品牌命名阶段即引入法律评估，平衡搜索优化与注册成功率"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid using vocabulary that directly describes product content or functional features as core brand identifiers to prevent refusal.", "Practice note: Integrate legal distinctiveness evaluations at the brand naming stage to balance search optimization goals with registration success rates.", "What to monitor: Monitor evolving EUIPO examination guidelines regarding the interpretation of 'subject matter' and descriptive characteristics under CP16."]
core_points_en: CP16 prohibits trademarks that directly describe key elements, subject matter, or thematic content of the designated goods and services.
Signs perceived by the public as describing product content rather than indicating commercial origin are deemed devoid of distinctive character.
Overly clear brand names facilitate search visibility but frequently result in trademark registration refusals due to descriptiveness.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid using vocabulary that directly describes product content or functional features as core brand identifiers to prevent refusal.
Practice note: Integrate legal distinctiveness evaluations at the brand naming stage to balance search optimization goals with registration success rates.
What to monitor: Monitor evolving EUIPO examination guidelines regarding the interpretation of 'subject matter' and descriptive characteristics under CP16.

items.title: Court of Appeal again rejects request for further written pleadings in appeal proceedings
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉法院再次驳回增加书面诉辩的请求
items.title_en: Court of Appeal again rejects request for further written pleadings in appeal proceedings
items.summary: Hurom v NUC Electronics – Court of Appeal, 4 March 2026 (UPC_CoA_678/2025
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉法院在 Hurom 诉 NUC 案中，再次依据程序规则第 36 条驳回增加书面诉辩请求。法院强调上诉书面阶段严格限于上诉状与答辩状，除非涉及公平原则等特殊情况。被申请人针对新辅助请求的抗辩不构成额外书面回合理由，剩余争议可在口头听证解决。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在 UPC 上诉中须预判书面阶段极短，避免依赖后续补充。提出新请求或应对新证据时，应确保首轮文件完备，并密切关注程序时限，防止因迟延申请丧失书面抗辩机会。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 上诉书面阶段原则上仅限一轮，额外提交属例外情形", "针对上诉新请求的抗辩不构成开启额外书面程序的正当理由", "当事人若需额外书面机会，应在收到对方文件后及时提出申请"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["上诉首轮文件需完备，切勿寄望于后续增加书面回合", "遇对方新主张应立即反应，延迟两月申请将被视为放弃"]

items.title: Too Seedless to Infringe? Nador cott v Asda and the Dependent Variety Question
items.title_zh: 无籽即不侵权？Nadorcott 诉 Asda 案与依赖品种认定
items.title_en: Nadorcott v Asda: UK Court Rules Tang Gold Not a Dependent Variety Due to Missing Essential Characteristics
items.summary: As far as the authors are aware, there is, as yet, no dedicated blog to plant varieties. Therefore the following post has been prepared for the Kluwer Patents Blog in the expectation that some of our readers will have an interest in this flourishing area.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国专利法院审理 Nadorcott 柑橘品种权案，判定竞品 Tang Gold 虽经辐射诱变源自该品种，但未保留花粉活力与多籽等本质特征，故不构成依赖品种侵权。法院同时探讨了权利人行使权利的合理机会原则，明确了植物品种权保护边界。
items.ai_summary_en: The English Patents Court recently addressed the scope of plant variety rights in Nadorcott v Asda. The court examined whether Tang Gold, a mandarin variety sold by Asda, constituted a dependent variety derived from the protected Nadorcott variety. Although Tang Gold originated from Nadorcott via radiation-induced mutation, Mellor J determined it did not retain the expression of Nadorcott’s essential characteristics, specifically pollen viability and seediness. Consequently, Tang Gold was found not to infringe under the essentially derived variety test. The judgment clarifies that mere derivation is insufficient for infringement; retention of essential traits is mandatory. Additionally, the court analyzed the 'reasonable opportunity' principle regarding enforcement timing, providing critical guidance on the boundaries of protection for plant breeders’ rights in the United Kingdom.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国农企出口欧洲需注意，仅证明品种来源不足以认定侵权，关键在于是否保留本质特征。建议加强品种差异化育种，并关注欧盟及成员国对依赖品种的司法认定标准，规避潜在诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling underscores that proving genetic derivation alone is insufficient to establish infringement of plant variety rights. Rights holders must demonstrate that the alleged infringing variety retains the expression of the initial variety's essential characteristics. For market participants, this highlights the importance of ensuring significant phenotypic differentiation when developing new varieties based on existing protected stock. Legal strategies should focus on precise identification of which traits constitute 'essential characteristics' under statutory definitions. Furthermore, parties must remain vigilant regarding procedural defenses, such as the reasonable opportunity principle, which can bar claims even where substantive infringement might otherwise exist.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Tang Gold 未保留 Nadorcott 本质特征，不构成依赖品种", "法院界定花粉活力与多籽性为该案关键本质特征", "即便构成侵权，权利人此前已有合理机会行使权利"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Tang Gold failed the dependent variety test because it did not retain Nadorcott's essential characteristics, despite being predominantly derived from it.", "The court identified pollen viability and seediness as the key essential characteristics whose absence precluded a finding of infringement.", "Even if Tang Gold had been deemed dependent, the rights holder would have faced challenges regarding the reasonable opportunity to enforce rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口品种需确保本质特征显著差异以避侵权", "密切关注英欧植物品种权司法裁判新动向"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure new varieties exhibit clear phenotypic differences in essential traits to avoid dependent variety classification.", "Practice note: Precisely define and evidence 'essential characteristics' during litigation to support or defend EDV claims.", "What to monitor: Evolving judicial interpretations of essential characteristics and enforcement timelines in UK and EU plant variety cases."]
core_points_en: Tang Gold failed the dependent variety test because it did not retain Nadorcott's essential characteristics, despite being predominantly derived from it.
The court identified pollen viability and seediness as the key essential characteristics whose absence precluded a finding of infringement.
Even if Tang Gold had been deemed dependent, the rights holder would have faced challenges regarding the reasonable opportunity to enforce rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure new varieties exhibit clear phenotypic differences in essential traits to avoid dependent variety classification.
Practice note: Precisely define and evidence 'essential characteristics' during litigation to support or defend EDV claims.
What to monitor: Evolving judicial interpretations of essential characteristics and enforcement timelines in UK and EU plant variety cases.

items.title: Training Data, Market Dilution, and the Elephant in the Room: Why the Three-Step Test Matters for Generative AI
items.title_zh: 训练数据与市场稀释：为何三步检验法关乎生成式 AI
items.title_en: Generative AI and Copyright: Why the Three-Step Test Constrains Training Exceptions
items.summary: The global debate over generative AI (genAI) and copyright has, by now, produced a voluminous body of commentary. Courts in the United States have wrestled with fair use factors in Kadrey v. Meta and Bartz v.
items.ai_summary_zh: 全球围绕生成式 AI 与版权的争论激烈，美德法院已介入审理相关案件，欧盟议会呼吁调整版权规则。文章指出，常被忽视的《伯尔尼公约》及 TRIPS 协定中的“三步检验法”才是关键。作者认为该测试严格限制了各国立法豁免空间，驳斥了仅提取统计模式或设置退出机制即可合规的观点，强调其对 AI 训练构成实质性法律约束。
items.ai_summary_en: The global debate on generative AI and copyright has intensified, with courts in the US and Germany addressing key issues while the European Parliament urges regulatory recalibration. Despite this activity, the three-step test from the Berne Convention and TRIPS Agreement remains underexplored. This analysis argues that the three-step test serves as a critical international legal ceiling limiting legislative discretion for copyright exceptions. It challenges the notion that extracting statistical patterns or relying on opt-out mechanisms ensures compliance. Rigorous application of the test reveals that proposed broad training exceptions likely fail legality reviews. The article emphasizes that the three-step test imposes substantive legal constraints on AI training, requiring strict adherence to international norms rather than relying on industry-preferred flexible frameworks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧部署 AI 模型需警惕版权合规风险，不能仅依赖“退出机制”或“统计学习”抗辩。建议重新评估训练数据来源合法性，关注 CJEU 后续判例，避免因违反三步检验法面临侵权诉讼或市场禁入。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants deploying AI models in Europe must recognize that copyright compliance cannot rely solely on 'opt-out' mechanisms or 'statistical learning' defenses. The three-step test remains a binding constraint on EU copyright exceptions. Companies should proactively assess the legitimacy of their training data sources to mitigate infringement risks. Reliance on anticipated broad statutory exceptions is precarious given the rigorous international standards. Legal teams must evaluate whether specific training activities conflict with normal exploitation or unreasonably prejudice rights holders. A cautious approach to data sourcing is essential to avoid potential litigation or market access barriers resulting from non-compliance with established international copyright frameworks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["三步检验法是限制各国立法豁免权的国际法天花板，常被政策讨论忽视", "文章驳斥了 AI 训练不触及表达元素或退出机制即合规的宽松观点", "严谨适用三步检验法显示，当前拟议的宽泛训练例外可能无法通过合法性审查"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The three-step test acts as an international legal ceiling restricting national legislative discretion on copyright exceptions.", "Arguments claiming AI training avoids expression elements or relies on opt-outs are insufficient for legal compliance.", "Strict application of the three-step test suggests current broad training exceptions may not withstand legality reviews."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["勿轻信宽泛训练例外，三步检验法仍是欧盟版权豁免的硬性约束", "应提前排查训练数据版权风险，密切关注卢森堡法院即将作出的裁决"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Do not assume broad training exceptions apply; the three-step test strictly limits EU copyright exemptions.", "Practice note: Conduct early audits of training data copyright status to identify and mitigate infringement risks.", "What to monitor: Track upcoming CJEU rulings, particularly in Like Company v. Google, for definitive guidance on AI copyright."]
core_points_en: The three-step test acts as an international legal ceiling restricting national legislative discretion on copyright exceptions.
Arguments claiming AI training avoids expression elements or relies on opt-outs are insufficient for legal compliance.
Strict application of the three-step test suggests current broad training exceptions may not withstand legality reviews.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Do not assume broad training exceptions apply; the three-step test strictly limits EU copyright exemptions.
Practice note: Conduct early audits of training data copyright status to identify and mitigate infringement risks.
What to monitor: Track upcoming CJEU rulings, particularly in Like Company v. Google, for definitive guidance on AI copyright.

items.title: How Can AI Help IP Practitioners Manage Responses To Office Actions?
items.title_zh: AI 如何助力知识产权从业者管理审查意见答复
items.title_en: Leveraging AI to Streamline Patent and Trademark Office Action Responses
items.summary: The latest AI tools for IP law firms and corporations are helping IP practitioners streamline the management of routine tasks, including facilitating the analysis and drafting of office actions and response texts. Discover the benefits of AI for your office action response (OAR) workflows in this practical guide to OAR management with Questel's IP Digital Assistant, Qthena.
items.ai_summary_zh: 针对欧洲专利与商标审查意见数量激增及复杂性提升的现状，本文分析了 AI 数字助手（如 Qthena）在简化常规任务中的作用。通过自动化分析与起草流程，AI 帮助从业者克服截止期限压力，减少误读风险，将资源集中于战略事务，从而提升答复质量与整体运营效率，驱动业务增长。
items.ai_summary_en: Amidst the surging volume and complexity of European patent and trademark office actions, this analysis examines the role of AI digital assistants, such as Qthena, in simplifying routine administrative tasks. By automating the analysis and drafting processes, these tools help practitioners manage strict deadlines and reduce the risk of misinterpretation. This automation allows legal teams to redirect resources toward high-value strategic matters, thereby enhancing response quality and overall operational efficiency. The integration of AI serves not only as a cost-saving measure but also as a critical driver for business growth and effective risk management in competitive intellectual property landscapes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧专利申请量巨大，面对日益复杂的审查意见，应积极引入 AI 辅助工具以提升答复效率与准确率。建议结合本地化法律数据库训练模型，降低因语言或法律理解偏差导致的驳回风险，优化海外 IP 资产布局。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders managing substantial patent portfolios in Europe, integrating AI-assisted tools is increasingly vital to handle complex examination procedures efficiently. Utilizing localized legal databases to train models can mitigate risks associated with linguistic or legal interpretation errors, thereby reducing refusal rates. This approach optimizes overseas IP asset layouts by ensuring higher accuracy in responses. Adopting such technologies supports faster grant timelines and strengthens the stability of high-value patents against potential challenges during prosecution.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["审查意见数量与复杂度双增，严苛期限给 IP 部门带来巨大压力", "AI 工具通过自动化常规任务，释放人力专注于高价值战略分析", "数字化工作流不仅节省预算，更是风险管理与战略增长的关键驱动力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rising volume and complexity of office actions create significant pressure on IP departments due to strict deadlines.", "AI tools automate routine tasks, freeing professionals to focus on high-value strategic analysis and decision-making.", "Digital workflows enhance operational efficiency, serving as key drivers for risk management and strategic growth."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 AI 缩短审查意见答复周期，提升在欧洲市场的确权速度", "借助智能分析降低法律误读风险，保障高价值专利资产的稳定性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage AI to shorten office action response cycles, accelerating rights confirmation in the European market.", "Practice note: Use intelligent analysis to minimize legal misinterpretation risks, ensuring the stability of high-value patent assets.", "What to monitor: Track advancements in AI-driven legal research tools tailored for specific European jurisdiction requirements."]
core_points_en: Rising volume and complexity of office actions create significant pressure on IP departments due to strict deadlines.
AI tools automate routine tasks, freeing professionals to focus on high-value strategic analysis and decision-making.
Digital workflows enhance operational efficiency, serving as key drivers for risk management and strategic growth.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage AI to shorten office action response cycles, accelerating rights confirmation in the European market.
Practice note: Use intelligent analysis to minimize legal misinterpretation risks, ensuring the stability of high-value patent assets.
What to monitor: Track advancements in AI-driven legal research tools tailored for specific European jurisdiction requirements.

items.title: Exclusive: UK govt refuses to pay costs in landmark AI case
items.title_zh: 独家：英政府拒绝支付标志性AI案诉讼费用
items.title_en: UK Government Refuses to Pay Costs in Landmark AI Patent Case After Supreme Court Loss
items.summary: Emotional Perception AI is seeking more than £200,000 after the UK Supreme Court backed its appeal
items.ai_summary_zh: 在英国最高法院支持Emotional Perception AI的上诉后，该公司正寻求超过20万英镑的费用赔偿。然而，英国政府目前拒绝支付这笔款项。该案被视为人工智能领域的标志性案件，后续费用承担问题引发关注。
items.ai_summary_en: Emotional Perception AI is seeking legal costs exceeding £200,000 from the UK government following a decisive victory at the Supreme Court. The court previously ruled that the company’s neural network-based invention constituted patentable subject matter, marking a significant milestone for artificial intelligence intellectual property protection in the United Kingdom. Despite this substantive win, which clarified the eligibility of AI technologies under current patent law, government officials have explicitly refused to cover the associated litigation expenses. This refusal has sparked considerable debate within the industry regarding the financial risks of enforcing IP rights against state bodies. The dispute highlights the complex economic realities of high-stakes patent litigation, where even successful applicants may face substantial hurdles in recovering costs, prompting broader scrutiny of government strategies and the overall cost-effectiveness of pursuing such landmark legal challenges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案虽胜诉但费用回收受阻，显示政府在标志性AI专利案中对抗立场强硬。需持续观察双方就费用承担的进一步法律程序或和解动向，以及该先例对后续AI专利诉讼成本分摊的潜在影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This development underscores the critical importance of conducting thorough cost-benefit analyses before initiating high-profile patent enforcement actions in the UK. While securing a favorable precedent on patent eligibility is valuable, rights holders must anticipate potential difficulties in recovering legal fees, particularly when litigating against public authorities. Strategic planning should include negotiating favorable cost-sharing provisions or assessing alternative dispute resolution mechanisms. Furthermore, companies operating in the AI sector should closely monitor how this ruling influences future examination guidelines for neural network inventions, ensuring their application strategies align with evolving judicial interpretations of technical contribution and patentability standards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国最高法院此前已支持Emotional Perception AI提出的上诉请求", "该公司目前在寻求超过20万英镑的诉讼费用赔偿", "英国政府明确拒绝支付上述案件相关的诉讼费用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Emotional Perception AI seeks over £200,000 in legal costs from the UK government after winning its appeal.", "The UK Supreme Court previously ruled that the company's neural network invention is eligible for patent protection.", "The government's refusal to pay costs despite losing the case has triggered industry controversy and concern."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["胜诉方在针对政府的标志性诉讼中仍面临费用执行阻力", "需监测英国政府拒绝付费的法律依据及后续程序进展", "观察此案对AI领域专利诉讼成本风险分配的示范效应"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Anticipate high litigation costs and potential recovery risks when enforcing patents against government entities in the UK.", "Practice note: Conduct detailed cost-benefit assessments and seek favorable cost terms before pursuing landmark AI patent cases.", "What to monitor: Evolving UKIPO examination practices for neural networks following the Supreme Court's decision on patent eligibility."]
core_points_en: Emotional Perception AI seeks over £200,000 in legal costs from the UK government after winning its appeal.
The UK Supreme Court previously ruled that the company's neural network invention is eligible for patent protection.
The government's refusal to pay costs despite losing the case has triggered industry controversy and concern.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Anticipate high litigation costs and potential recovery risks when enforcing patents against government entities in the UK.
Practice note: Conduct detailed cost-benefit assessments and seek favorable cost terms before pursuing landmark AI patent cases.
What to monitor: Evolving UKIPO examination practices for neural networks following the Supreme Court's decision on patent eligibility.

items.title: UPC Weekly - The juicer, the claim interpretation and the jurisdiction
items.title_zh: UPC 周报：榨汁机案、权利要求解释及管辖权争议
items.title_en: UPC Weekly: Jurisdiction Standards and Claim Interpretation in the Juicer Case
items.summary: 2026 Week 13
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析统一专利法院（UPC）最新周讯，聚焦“榨汁机”相关案件。核心探讨 UPC 在跨国侵权中的管辖权确立标准，以及权利要求解释对判决结果的关键影响。文章指出法院正通过具体判例细化程序规则，明确地域管辖边界，为后续专利诉讼提供重要实务指引。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis of the Unified Patent Court’s latest weekly update focuses on the prominent 'juicer' litigation. It examines how the court establishes jurisdiction in cross-border patent infringement disputes and highlights the decisive role of claim interpretation in determining case outcomes. The article notes that the UPC is refining procedural rules through specific precedents, thereby clarifying territorial jurisdiction boundaries. These developments provide essential practical guidance for future patent litigation strategies within the new European judicial framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更明确的 UPC 管辖风险，需提前评估产品在欧洲各国的侵权暴露面。建议优化权利要求布局，针对关键市场制定差异化应诉预案，并密切关注 UPC 对解释规则的演进。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe face increasingly defined jurisdictional risks under the Unified Patent Court regime. It is critical to reassess the overall infringement exposure of exported products across UPC member states. Rights holders should adjust patent drafting and invalidity defense strategies to align with evolving claim interpretation rules. Proactive assessment of product liability in key markets allows for the development of differentiated response plans, ensuring robust legal positioning against potential cross-border enforcement actions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 通过具体案例厘清跨国专利侵权的管辖权确立标准", "权利要求解释方法成为决定案件走向与赔偿范围的核心要素", "律所分析揭示 UPC 程序规则细化趋势，影响未来诉讼策略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC clarifies jurisdiction standards for cross-border patent infringement through specific case precedents.", "Claim interpretation methods have become central to determining case trajectories and compensation scopes.", "Law firm analysis reveals a trend toward refined procedural rules impacting future litigation strategies."]

items.title: EU design reform – Phase 2 details confirmed - GJE
items.title_zh: 欧盟外观设计改革第二阶段细节确认
items.title_en: EU Design Reform Phase 2: Final Details Confirmed for July 1 Implementation
items.summary: In this article, we look at Phase 2 of the reform changes in detail and what they mean for design applicants.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟集中式外观设计体系改革计划将于今年收官，第二阶段定于 7 月 1 日正式生效。继 2025 年发布初步细节后，欧盟委员会近期公布了最终实施条例和授权条例文本。本文详细解读了第二阶段改革的具体变化及其对设计申请人的实际影响，标志着欧盟外观设计现代化进程进入全面落地阶段。
items.ai_summary_en: The comprehensive reform programme for the centralised EU design system is set to conclude this year, with Phase 2 officially entering into force on 1 July. Following the release of initial details in 2025, the European Commission has recently published the final texts for both the Implementing Regulations and the Delegated Regulation. This article provides a detailed analysis of the specific changes introduced in Phase 2 and examines their practical implications for design applicants. These developments mark a significant milestone in the modernisation of the EU design framework, transitioning from legislative planning to full operational implementation. Rights holders must now navigate the updated procedural landscape to ensure compliance and leverage new efficiencies within the European intellectual property system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局外观设计需密切关注 7 月新规，及时调整申请策略以符合最新程序要求。建议立即审查现有及在途案件，评估改革对确权效率及维权成本的影响，利用新机制优化欧洲市场知识产权保护布局。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should urgently review existing and pending design applications to align with the new procedural requirements effective 1 July. It is critical to assess how these reforms influence registration efficiency and potential enforcement costs. Companies are advised to adjust their European filing strategies immediately to optimise protection under the new mechanisms. Proactive adaptation ensures that rights holders can fully benefit from the streamlined processes while mitigating risks associated with transitional periods. This strategic realignment supports robust IP portfolio management across the EU jurisdiction.

items.title: Unionsmarke
items.title_zh: 《欧盟商标》第六版发布：涵盖至 2024 年最新判例
items.title_en: Sixth Edition of EU Trademark Handbook Released: Updated with Latest Case Law and Procedures
items.summary: Default Description
items.ai_summary_zh: Vossius & Partner 律师 Achim Bender 主编的《欧盟商标》第六版于 2025 年出版。该书作为欧洲商标体系的简明指南，重点对比了欧盟与德国法律的差异。内容更新至 2024 年 9 月的欧盟法院及普通法院判例，并收录了 EUIPO 及法院的最新程序规则，旨在为从业者提供准确的实务指引。
items.ai_summary_en: The sixth edition of the handbook on European Union trademarks, edited by Vossius & Partner attorney Achim Bender, was published in 2025. Serving as a concise guide to the European trademark system, the work specifically highlights the differences between EU law and German national legislation. The content incorporates the latest case law from the Court of Justice of the European Union and the General Court up to September 2024. It also includes recent procedural updates from the EUIPO and relevant courts. This publication aims to provide practitioners with accurate, up-to-date guidance for handling EU trademark matters in practice, ensuring readers are informed about current legal standards and procedural requirements.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注欧盟商标最新判例趋势，利用专业资料规避注册风险。建议在对德贸易中注意欧德法律差异，及时更新合规策略以应对程序变更。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor recent trends in EU trademark jurisprudence to anticipate examination standards and mitigate registration risks. Utilizing authoritative resources helps ensure compliance with evolving legal interpretations. For companies engaged in trade with Germany, it is crucial to recognize the divergences between EU-wide regulations and German national laws. Updating compliance strategies to address procedural changes at the EUIPO and before EU courts is essential. A tailored approach to intellectual property protection in Europe allows rights holders to navigate complex jurisdictional nuances effectively and maintain robust brand security across the single market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["前 EUIPO 上诉委员主编，深度解析欧盟商标体系架构", "特别强调欧盟商标法与德国国内法之间的差异与特殊性", "收录截至 2024 年 9 月的最新欧盟法院判例及程序新规"]

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["研读最新判例以预判审查标准，降低商标注册驳回风险", "重视欧德法律差异，制定针对性的欧洲市场知识产权保护策略"]

items.title: Open Letter: Uneven Distribution Of UPC Cases Between The Different Local/Regional/Central Divisions Of The Court Of First Instance
items.title_zh: 公开信：统一专利法院一审各分院案件分布不均问题
items.title_en: Open Letter: Uneven UPC Case Distribution Reflects Market Demand, Not System Failure
items.summary: EPLAW asked us with e-mail of March 11, 2026, attaching an e-mail of March 2, 2026, from the chairperson of the Advisory Committee of the UPC to provide guidance relating to concerns...
items.ai_summary_zh: 针对 EPLAW 担忧统一专利法院（UPC）各分院案件分布不均，本文作者反驳称此为市场驱动的正常现象，反映用户需求而非系统缺陷。作者警告过度干预可能迫使申请人转向国家诉讼，主张采取最小化监管措施以维持系统吸引力，强调德国与海牙分院的高案件量正是系统成功的证明。
items.ai_summary_en: Addressing concerns raised by EPLAW regarding the uneven distribution of cases across Unified Patent Court divisions, this analysis argues that such disparities are natural outcomes of market-driven dynamics rather than systemic flaws. The author warns that aggressive regulatory interventions to balance caseloads could inadvertently push litigants toward national courts or non-European jurisdictions, thereby undermining the UPC’s early success. High case volumes in German and Hague divisions are cited as evidence of strong user acceptance and system efficacy. Instead of heavy-handed measures, the piece advocates for minimal, user-centric improvements to maintain the court's attractiveness while ensuring its long-term viability as a truly European institution.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应认识到 UPC 案件集中德荷是常态，无需因分布不均恐慌。建议继续利用德国等地成熟分庭进行核心专利布局与维权，同时警惕未来可能的政策微调，保持诉讼策略灵活性，避免盲目跟随非市场化导向的改革呼声。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should view the concentration of UPC litigation in specific divisions, particularly Germany and The Hague, as a standard reflection of user preference and legal certainty rather than an anomaly requiring correction. Rights holders are advised to continue leveraging these established venues for core patent enforcement strategies. However, stakeholders must remain vigilant regarding potential policy adjustments aimed at redistributing cases. Maintaining flexibility in litigation planning is crucial to adapt to any future regulatory shifts without compromising strategic advantages derived from current procedural efficiencies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["案件分布不均是市场需求结果，非系统性问题无需过度干预", "激进改革措施恐导致用户回流至国家诉讼或域外诉讼体系", "德国与海牙分院高负荷运转证实了 UPC 系统的成功与接受度"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Uneven case distribution reflects genuine market demand and user preferences, not inherent structural defects within the Unified Patent Court system.", "Aggressive measures to artificially balance caseloads risk driving litigants back to national courts or outside Europe entirely.", "High litigation volumes in German and Hague divisions confirm the UPC’s successful adoption and strong acceptance among diverse market participants."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["视案件集中为市场选择常态，坚定在德荷等热门分庭布局策略", "警惕人为平衡案件的政策风险，保持诉讼地选择的灵活性与多元"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Treat case concentration in popular divisions as a stable market norm; prioritize these venues for high-value patent enforcement actions.", "Practice note: Avoid reacting to calls for artificial case balancing; focus on procedural efficiency and established jurisprudence in high-volume divisions.", "What to monitor: Watch for legislative or administrative proposals aiming to redistribute cases, which may alter venue selection strategies."]
core_points_en: Uneven case distribution reflects genuine market demand and user preferences, not inherent structural defects within the Unified Patent Court system.
Aggressive measures to artificially balance caseloads risk driving litigants back to national courts or outside Europe entirely.
High litigation volumes in German and Hague divisions confirm the UPC’s successful adoption and strong acceptance among diverse market participants.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Treat case concentration in popular divisions as a stable market norm; prioritize these venues for high-value patent enforcement actions.
Practice note: Avoid reacting to calls for artificial case balancing; focus on procedural efficiency and established jurisprudence in high-volume divisions.
What to monitor: Watch for legislative or administrative proposals aiming to redistribute cases, which may alter venue selection strategies.

items.title: When Design Goes Beyond Function: A Recent Case Shows Sensory Experience Details Matter In Motion Marks
items.title_zh: 设计超越功能：欧盟法院裁定动态商标感官细节至关重要
items.title_en: EU Court Ruling: Sensory Details in Motion Marks Crucial for Distinctiveness
items.summary: The General Court of the European Union delivered a judgment in Case T-9/25, KCT v EUIPO, on January 14, 2026, which is useful to demonstrate the level of 'distinctiveness' required to overcome objections citing Article 7(1)(b)...
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在 KCT 诉 EUIPO 案中判决，动态商标注册需证明非功能性感官细节。法院纠正上诉局忽视黑色间隔件等特征的错误，强调若包含装饰性元素则不适用技术结果排除条款。同时指出仅由物理光影导致的颜色变化缺乏显著性，必须展示真正的创造性视觉变化方可获准注册。
items.ai_summary_en: The General Court of the European Union ruled in Case T-9/25, KCT v EUIPO, that motion mark registrations must demonstrate non-functional sensory details to overcome distinctiveness objections. The Court corrected the Board of Appeal’s error in disregarding specific features like black spacers, emphasizing that ornamental elements prevent the application of the technical result exclusion under Article 7(1)(e)(ii) EUTMR. However, the judgment clarified that color changes resulting solely from physical light and shadow effects lack distinctive character. To secure registration, applicants must prove genuine creative visual differences rather than relying on natural physical phenomena or purely functional movements.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请动态商标时，应避免纯功能性描述，着重提炼并固化具有感官识别度的非功能性细节。需区分物理现象与创意设计，通过证据链证明颜色或动作变化的独特性，以规避因缺乏显著性或被认定为实现技术结果而遭驳回的风险。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders seeking EU trade mark protection for motion marks, it is critical to avoid purely functional descriptions. Applications should focus on extracting and solidifying non-functional details with high sensory recognizability. Applicants must clearly distinguish between natural physical phenomena and creative design elements. By building a robust evidence chain to demonstrate the uniqueness of color or movement changes, market participants can mitigate the risk of refusal based on lack of distinctiveness or classification as a technical solution necessary for obtaining a specific result.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["动态商标中非功能性感官细节是克服显著性质疑的关键要素", "若含装饰性特征则不适用技术结果排除条款，竞品可替代功能方案", "单纯物理光影导致的颜色变化无显著性，需证明创造性视觉差异"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Non-functional sensory details are key elements for overcoming distinctiveness challenges in motion mark applications.", "The technical result exclusion does not apply if decorative features are present, allowing competitors alternative solutions.", "Color changes caused only by physical light and shadow lack distinctiveness; proof of creative visual difference is required."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["申请时需刻意保留并强调非功能性装饰细节以提升显著性", "严格区分物理光影变化与创造性设计，避免被认定为自然现象"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Deliberately retain and emphasize non-functional decorative details to enhance distinctiveness during application.", "Practice note: Strictly differentiate between physical light/shadow changes and creative design to avoid classification as natural phenomena.", "What to monitor: Monitor how examiners assess the balance between functional movement and ornamental features in future decisions."]
core_points_en: Non-functional sensory details are key elements for overcoming distinctiveness challenges in motion mark applications.
The technical result exclusion does not apply if decorative features are present, allowing competitors alternative solutions.
Color changes caused only by physical light and shadow lack distinctiveness; proof of creative visual difference is required.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Deliberately retain and emphasize non-functional decorative details to enhance distinctiveness during application.
Practice note: Strictly differentiate between physical light/shadow changes and creative design to avoid classification as natural phenomena.
What to monitor: Monitor how examiners assess the balance between functional movement and ornamental features in future decisions.

items.title: Is AI software for IP just expensive wrapping paper?
items.title_zh: 人工智能知识产权软件是否仅是昂贵的包装纸？
items.title_en: AI IP Software: Essential Tool or Merely Expensive Wrapping Paper for Law Firms?
items.summary: At the last count, there were more than seventy companies offering AI-assisted IP software solutions. Most of these companies are less than two years old. Also, if their marketing materials are to be believed, they have plenty of big IP-firm clients. But do IP firms understand what they are actually buying and whether it is actually worth the cost, both in terms of the short-term risks and
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文指出目前已有超过70家公司提供AI辅助知识产权软件解决方案，其中大多数公司成立不足两年。尽管营销材料显示其拥有众多大型律所客户，但文章质疑律所是否真正理解所购产品及其成本效益，特别是短期风险方面。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent market analysis reveals over seventy companies currently offering AI-assisted intellectual property software solutions. The majority of these vendors are start-ups established less than two years ago, yet their marketing materials frequently claim partnerships with major international law firms. This rapid proliferation raises critical questions regarding the actual value proposition of these tools. It remains uncertain whether legal practitioners fully comprehend the specific functionalities they are purchasing or if the costs justify the benefits. There is a pressing need to evaluate short-term risks and long-term cost-effectiveness carefully. The industry must look beyond promotional claims to determine if these technologies deliver genuine operational improvements or simply represent an expensive trend without substantive utility for complex IP management tasks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文仅为市场评论片段，未提供完整论证。需关注欧洲IP服务市场对AI工具的实际采纳深度及价值评估标准的变化，以及供应商成熟度对客户决策的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and legal teams operating in Europe, adopting AI-driven IP tools requires careful due diligence beyond vendor promises. Organizations should prioritize verifying compliance with EU data protection regulations and ensuring robust security protocols before integration. A prudent approach involves conducting small-scale pilot programs tailored to specific business scenarios. This strategy allows firms to validate practical efficiency gains and risk mitigation capabilities before committing to large-scale deployments. By focusing on tangible outcomes rather than conceptual appeal, companies can avoid paying premiums for unproven technology while ensuring that digital investments genuinely enhance their intellectual property management workflows.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["目前市场上有超过70家公司提供AI辅助知识产权软件解决方案", "大多数此类AI软件供应商成立时间不足两年，属于新兴企业", "文章质疑律所对客户实际购买内容及成本效益（含短期风险）的理解程度"]

items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate AI tool efficacy in EU contexts to avoid paying premiums for unproven concepts.", "Practice note: Conduct small-scale pilots to verify compliance and real-world efficiency gains before scaling.", "What to monitor: Scrutinize marketing claims against actual performance and regulatory adherence in practice."]

Law firms may lack clarity on the actual value and risks associated with purchasing these AI tools.
Market participants should rationally assess cost-effectiveness rather than blindly following marketing hype.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate AI tool efficacy in EU contexts to avoid paying premiums for unproven concepts.
Practice note: Conduct small-scale pilots to verify compliance and real-world efficiency gains before scaling.
What to monitor: Scrutinize marketing claims against actual performance and regulatory adherence in practice.

items.title: Sandoz fends off PI over Eylea at Higher Regional Court Munich
items.title_zh: 山德士在慕尼黑高等法院成功抵御Eylea专利临时禁令
items.title_en: Munich Higher Regional Court Revokes Preliminary Injunction Against Sandoz Biosimilar
items.summary: In early January, the Regional Court Munich granted a PI to Regeneron and Bayer. This prohibited Stada, Sandoz/Hexal, Celltrion and Advanz Pharma from marketing their biosimilar containing the active ingredient aflibercept. This forms the basis of Regeneron’s ophthalmic drug Eylea, used to treat neovascular (wet) age-related macular degeneration and diabetic macular oedema. The US patent […] The post Sandoz fends off PI over Eylea at Higher Regional Court Munich appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地区法院曾于1月初授予再生元与拜耳临时禁令，禁止山德士等销售含阿柏西普成分的生物类似药。现标题显示，山德士在慕尼黑高等法院成功抵御了该临时禁令。原文正文较短，未披露高等法院的具体裁决理由或后续程序状态。
items.ai_summary_en: The Munich Higher Regional Court has overturned a preliminary injunction previously granted by the Regional Court Munich to Regeneron and Bayer. The initial order had prohibited Stada, Sandoz/Hexal, Celltrion, and Advanz Pharma from marketing biosimilars containing aflibercept, the active ingredient in Eylea for treating wet age-related macular degeneration. This appellate decision highlights the significant uncertainty surrounding preliminary relief in German patent litigation. It demonstrates that higher courts apply strict scrutiny to infringement findings during interim proceedings. Consequently, even successful first-instance injunctions face substantial risk of reversal on appeal, emphasizing the critical role of the second instance in balancing market access against patent enforcement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息有限，仅能确认禁令在上诉阶段被阻却。需关注高等法院最终书面裁决理由，以及该结果对原研药企在德国市场执行专利策略的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should recognize that preliminary injunctions in Germany are not final determinations of validity or infringement. The appellate mechanism provides a crucial avenue to challenge restrictive orders rapidly. Companies facing such injunctions should prioritize preparing robust invalidity arguments early in the process. Swiftly initiating appeals can help secure vital market windows while substantive issues are resolved. Blindly withdrawing from the market upon a first-instance loss may be unnecessary if strong legal grounds exist for reversal. Strategic preparation for potential interim relief challenges is essential for maintaining commercial continuity.

The original injunction had blocked multiple pharmaceutical companies from selling biosimilar products containing the active ingredient aflibercept.

Practice note: Prepare strong invalidity evidence proactively to effectively counter sudden preliminary injunctions in biosimilar disputes.
What to monitor: Track how higher courts scrutinize infringement assessments in interim proceedings to anticipate potential reversals.

items.title: Exclusive: CMS IP co-head to join Perkins Coie
items.title_zh: 独家：CMS知识产权联席主管将加入Perkins Coie
items.title_en: CMS IP Co-Head Caitlin Heard Joins Perkins Coie to Bolster London Presence
items.summary: Arrival of Caitlin Heard will bolster the soon-to-be-created Ashurst Perkins Coie’s IP presence in the capital
items.ai_summary_zh: CMS律师事务所知识产权联席主管Caitlin Heard将加入Perkins Coie。此次人事变动旨在加强即将成立的Ashurst Perkins Coie在首都（伦敦）的知识产权业务存在感。
items.ai_summary_en: Caitlin Heard, the co-head of intellectual property at CMS, has confirmed her move to Perkins Coie. This strategic appointment is designed to significantly strengthen the intellectual property capabilities of the newly formed Ashurst Perkins Coie entity within the London market. The hire underscores a broader trend among leading European law firms, which are actively leveraging high-profile talent acquisitions and structural mergers to enhance their competitive positioning in the UK and across continental Europe. By integrating experienced leadership from top-tier firms, the combined practice aims to solidify its standing in the capital’s complex IP landscape. This development highlights how major legal service providers are adapting to market dynamics through targeted recruitment and consolidation strategies to maintain robust service offerings for clients navigating intricate intellectual property challenges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文仅披露单一高管流动事实，未涉及具体业务策略或市场影响分析。需持续观察Ashurst与Perkins Coie合并后的整合进度及其对英国知识产权法律服务格局的实际影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Clients engaging with law firms undergoing significant structural changes should closely monitor team integration processes and potential shifts in service delivery models. As firms merge and integrate new leadership, there may be transitional periods affecting case handling and strategic advice. Rights holders should proactively assess the stability and continuity of their external legal counsel during these transitions. Furthermore, this period offers an opportunity to renegotiate fee structures or service terms, potentially securing more favorable conditions while firms seek to retain key accounts. It is also prudent to evaluate whether the merged entity’s evolving expertise aligns with specific technical or jurisdictional needs, ensuring that the combined resources effectively support ongoing intellectual property management and enforcement objectives.

items.title: Court of Appeal hears Fujifilm vs Kodak over two days
items.title_zh: 上诉庭两日审理富士胶片诉柯达案
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Hears Fujifilm vs Kodak on Cross-Border Jurisdiction
items.summary: The long-arm jurisdiction of the Unified Patent Court is currently one of the hottest topics in patent law. Following the CJEU’s ruling in BSH Hausgeräte vs Electrolux, it was primarily the dispute between FujiFilm and Kodak over printing plates that made headlines on the subject of long-arm jurisdiction. Other aspects of the dispute temporarily faded […] The post Court of Appeal hears Fujifilm vs Kodak over two days appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭就富士胶片与柯达之间的印刷版专利纠纷进行了为期两天的听证。该案继欧盟法院在BSH诉伊莱克斯案裁决后，成为测试UPC“长臂管辖权”范围的关键案件，引发专利法领域高度关注。
items.ai_summary_en: The scope of the Unified Patent Court’s long-arm jurisdiction remains a critical issue in European patent law. Following the Court of Justice of the European Union’s ruling in BSH Hausgeräte v Electrolux, the dispute between Fujifilm and Kodak regarding printing plates has become a focal point for discussions on extraterritorial reach. The UPC Court of Appeal recently conducted a two-day hearing centered on these complex cross-border jurisdictional challenges. While other substantive aspects of the litigation temporarily receded into the background, this procedural phase is pivotal. It tests how national courts interact with the UPC’s authority when infringement spans multiple member states. The outcome will significantly influence future forum selection strategies and the practical application of EU-wide injunctions in multi-jurisdictional patent disputes across Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案是观察UPC上诉庭如何界定跨境侵权管辖权的关键窗口。鉴于此前欧盟法院在BSH案中的裁决背景，业界需密切关注上诉庭是否维持或调整对“长臂管辖”的解释，这将直接影响跨国专利诉讼的策略选择。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor how the UPC interprets its jurisdictional boundaries post-BSH, as rulings may expand exposure to pan-European enforcement actions. Rights holders must reassess their cross-border patent portfolios and litigation readiness to mitigate risks associated with broad injunctions. Strategic planning should account for the potential that localized disputes could trigger continent-wide legal consequences. Understanding these jurisdictional dynamics is essential for effective risk management and for structuring defensive measures against expansive claims in the new unified system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["统一专利法院上诉庭对富士胶片诉柯达案进行了为期两天的听证审理", "本案核心争议点在于统一专利法院的“长臂管辖权”适用范围问题", "该案被视为继欧盟法院BSH案裁决后，检验UPC管辖权边界的重要案例"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC long-arm jurisdiction re-emerged as a key judicial focus following the CJEU's significant ruling in the BSH case.", "The Fujifilm versus Kodak printing plate patent dispute has entered appellate proceedings at the Unified Patent Court.", "A two-day hearing specifically addressed legal applications concerning cross-border jurisdiction and extraterritorial reach."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注上诉庭对UPC长臂管辖权的具体认定标准及其与欧盟法院先例的一致性", "观察该裁决对后续涉及多国侵权行为的统一专利法院案件产生的判例影响", "留意富士胶片与柯达案中除管辖权外其他被暂时搁置的法律争议点后续进展"]

A two-day hearing specifically addressed legal applications concerning cross-border jurisdiction and extraterritorial reach.

Practice note: Reassess cross-border patent portfolios and prepare robust strategies for multi-jurisdictional defense.
What to monitor: Judicial trends in applying cross-border jurisdiction rules following the BSH precedent.

items.title: How a hair curling patent dispute reached Europe’s highest court
items.title_zh: 戴森卷发器专利纠纷如何诉至欧洲最高法院
items.title_en: CJEU to Define Scope of UPC Long-Arm Jurisdiction in Dyson Hair Curling Patent Dispute
items.summary: The European Court of Justice will decide how far the UPC’s ‘long-arm’ jurisdiction can reach in a complex case brought by Dyson, the outcome of which could have a far-reaching impact on businesses. Sarah Speight explores.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲法院将审理戴森提起的一起复杂案件，核心议题是界定统一专利法院“长臂管辖权”的适用范围。该案结果可能对商业领域产生深远影响。原文仅披露此程序性进展，未提供案件具体事实背景或法律论证细节。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union is set to adjudicate a complex patent dispute initiated by Dyson, focusing on the precise scope of the Unified Patent Court’s long-arm jurisdiction. This high-stakes litigation serves as a critical test case for determining how far the UPC’s authority extends across member states. The final ruling will not only dictate the fate of the specific patents involved but also establish significant legal precedents regarding cross-border intellectual property enforcement. Consequently, the outcome is expected to have profound implications for businesses operating within Europe, fundamentally shaping future patent litigation strategies and clarifying the boundaries of multinational IP rights protection under the new court system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案核心在于厘清UPC与成员国法院及CJEU之间的管辖权界限。鉴于正文信息有限，目前仅能确认CJEU已介入审查UPC管辖范围。需持续关注CJEU最终裁决对跨境专利诉讼策略及UPC案件受理标准的潜在塑造作用。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely track the CJEU’s forthcoming decision, as it will clarify the extent of the UPC’s jurisdictional reach over non-resident defendants. Rights holders must reassess their European patent portfolios and litigation risks in light of potential jurisdictional expansion. It is advisable to conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses and develop robust contingency plans for cross-border disputes. Proactive preparation helps mitigate exposure to unexpected long-arm jurisdiction claims, ensuring that companies can navigate the evolving landscape of European patent enforcement with greater strategic certainty and reduced legal vulnerability.

items.title: Is Your Business Name Already Taken? How To Avoid Trademark Conflicts Before Launch
items.title_zh: 企业名称是否已被占用？发布前如何避免商标冲突
items.title_en: Is Your Business Name Already Taken? How To Avoid Trademark Conflicts Before Launch
items.summary: Many entrepreneurs assume trademark conflicts only happen when two companies sell the same product. In reality, trademark law is much broader than that. It is surprisingly common for a business to invest in branding only to discover that an earlier trademark registration blocks the name. The reason often lies in how trademark classifications work.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出许多创业者误以为仅同类产品才存在商标冲突，实则尼斯分类体系下同类别涵盖广泛。即使业务细分不同，若属同一类别且易致消费者混淆，仍构成侵权风险。文章强调欧盟知识产权局在审查时侧重“混淆可能性”而非单纯类别比对，警示企业在品牌投入前需进行详尽检索。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局品牌时，切勿仅凭直觉判断类别差异。应针对目标市场进行全类别近似检索，特别关注第九类等宽泛类别中的在先权利，避免因忽视“混淆可能性”原则导致品牌重塑或诉讼损失。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["尼斯分类涵盖广泛，同类别内不同细分业务可能产生冲突", "商标审查核心在于评估消费者是否会产生来源混淆的可能性", "跨类经营普遍，单一类别注册可能阻挡多个看似无关的业务"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["开展全类别检索，警惕宽泛类别内的在先近似商标阻碍", "深入理解混淆原则，避免误判非直接竞争业务的侵权风险"]

items.title: The Rising Cost Of Trademark Protection: What Applicants Actually Receive For Their Fees
items.title_zh: 商标保护成本攀升：申请人费用与实际获权分析
items.title_en: Rising Trademark Fees: Analyzing Costs Versus Actual Protection Value for Applicants
items.summary: The cost of trademark protection is climbing steadily, but higher fees do not always translate into stronger or faster protection. With fees rising across major offices, businesses must consider not only the upfront cost but the operational and strategic implications of where and how they register their trademarks
items.ai_summary_zh: 全球主要知识产权局纷纷上调商标费用以覆盖运营成本。美国专利商标局于 2025 年实施统一申请费，英国知识产权局计划于 2026 年大幅涨价。文章指出费用上涨未必带来审查质量提升，企业需权衡注册地的战略价值与实际回报，优化全球商标布局。
items.ai_summary_en: Major intellectual property offices are steadily increasing trademark fees to cover rising operational costs and system maintenance. The United States Patent and Trademark Office implemented a unified filing fee structure in January 2025, while the UK Intellectual Property Office plans a significant fee increase effective April 2026. These adjustments reflect the growing resource intensity of managing expanding application volumes. However, higher fees do not necessarily guarantee enhanced examination quality or faster registration outcomes. Rights holders must carefully evaluate the strategic value of specific jurisdictions against these escalating costs. Understanding what these fees actually fund, from formal checks to substantive legal reviews, is essential for optimizing global brand protection strategies and ensuring that financial investments yield tangible legal security.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应重新评估商标布局成本效益，避免盲目追求全类注册。建议优先核心市场，利用马德里体系降低成本，并密切关注英国脱欧后独立的费率政策变化，提前规划预算。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should reassess the cost-benefit ratio of their trademark portfolios, particularly regarding filings in the UK and EU. Rather than pursuing broad, non-strategic registrations, companies should prioritize core markets where brand presence is critical. Utilizing international registration systems like the Madrid Protocol can help optimize overall budgets for multi-jurisdictional protection. Additionally, stakeholders must closely monitor independent fee policy changes post-Brexit, as the UK diverges further from EU frameworks. Proactive budget planning and strategic selection of classes and jurisdictions are vital to maintain robust protection without incurring unnecessary expenses.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英美等主要辖区显著上调商标注册及维持费用", "费用增长旨在应对案件量增及系统维护成本压力", "高费用并不必然对应更严格审查或更快确权速度"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Leading IP offices in the US and UK have significantly raised trademark application and maintenance fees to address operational pressures.", "Fee increases aim to offset the resource-intensive nature of registry maintenance and handle growing volumes of trademark applications.", "Higher official fees do not automatically correspond with stricter examination standards or accelerated rights confirmation processes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重新评估英国及欧盟商标布局的成本收益比", "利用国际注册体系优化多国申请的总体预算"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Reassess the cost-benefit ratio of trademark layouts in the UK and EU to ensure efficient resource allocation.", "Practice note: Leverage international registration systems to optimize total budgets for multi-country applications and reduce overhead.", "What to monitor: Track independent fee policy changes in the UK post-Brexit and plan budgets ahead of scheduled increases."]
core_points_en: Leading IP offices in the US and UK have significantly raised trademark application and maintenance fees to address operational pressures.
Fee increases aim to offset the resource-intensive nature of registry maintenance and handle growing volumes of trademark applications.
Higher official fees do not automatically correspond with stricter examination standards or accelerated rights confirmation processes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Reassess the cost-benefit ratio of trademark layouts in the UK and EU to ensure efficient resource allocation.
Practice note: Leverage international registration systems to optimize total budgets for multi-country applications and reduce overhead.
What to monitor: Track independent fee policy changes in the UK post-Brexit and plan budgets ahead of scheduled increases.

items.title: Can AI Patent Translations Reduce Costs Without Compromising Quality?
items.title_zh: AI 专利翻译能否在不牺牲质量前提下降低成本？
items.title_en: Can AI Patent Translations Reduce Costs Without Compromising Quality in Europe?
items.summary: Patent translation is highly specialized, requiring both technical accuracy and legal precision. However, it's also costly and time-consuming, which is why AI-based patent translation tools look so attractive.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出欧盟专利翻译兼具技术与法律高精度要求，成本高昂且耗时。虽 AI 工具具吸引力，但单纯依赖存在术语误读与语境缺失风险。主张采用“人机协同”模式，结合生成式 AI 效率与专家审查，以在预算压力下确保权利范围准确及可执行性。
items.ai_summary_en: Patent translation within the European Union demands high technical accuracy and legal precision, yet remains costly and time-consuming. While AI tools offer attractive efficiency gains, relying solely on them poses risks of terminology errors and contextual misunderstandings. The analysis advocates for a human-in-the-loop approach, combining generative AI speed with expert review. This strategy helps rights holders manage budget pressures while ensuring accurate scope definition and enforceability across jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局应警惕纯 AI 翻译导致的权利缩限风险。建议建立“机器初译 + 专家复核”机制，尤其在关键权利要求书中，避免因小失大，确保在欧洲单一专利体系下的保护效力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants seeking protection under the European patent system should be cautious regarding the risks of rights limitation associated with purely AI-driven translations. Establishing a workflow that combines machine initial translation with rigorous expert review is essential, particularly for critical claims. This approach prevents minor errors from undermining broader protection strategies, ensuring robust legal validity and enforceability within the unified European framework.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["专利翻译错误直接影响权利范围、可执行性及法律确定性", "通用 AI 引擎难以理解专利特有的技术法律术语及细微语境", "人机协同流程是平衡翻译速度、成本精度与合规性的最佳实践"]

items.title: Trademark Registration For Musicians: The Overlooked Tool To Protect Your Brand
items.title_zh: 音乐人商标：被忽视的品牌保护工具
items.title_en: Trademark Registration for Musicians: An Overlooked Brand Protection Tool
items.summary: For many musicians, intellectual property protection begins and ends with copyright. Copyright law is widely understood in the music industry as the legal framework that protects songs, lyrics, recordings, and other creative works. While copyright is important, it is far from the only tool available to artists seeking to protect their brand and livelihood.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出音乐行业过度依赖版权，忽视了商标对乐队名、周边商品等商业标识的保护。随着全球电商平台兴起，假冒商品泛滥，如MF Doom起诉Temu案。商标成为打击侵权、维护品牌控制权的核心法律工具。建议音乐人通过商标注册防止身份被滥用，并在遭遇仿冒时采取法律行动。
items.ai_summary_en: The article highlights that the music industry often relies excessively on copyright, neglecting trademark protection for band names and merchandise. Copyright does not cover commercial identifiers like logos or tour names, which fall under trademark law. With the rise of global e-commerce, counterfeit goods have proliferated due to insufficient platform oversight, severely damaging artists' brand equity. Recent legal actions, such as MF Doom’s lawsuit against Temu, illustrate the critical role of trademarks in combating infringement. Registering trademarks is essential for establishing source identification, enabling rights holders to request takedowns of infringing links and claim damages. Artists are advised to secure trademark registrations to prevent identity misuse and take legal action against counterfeits to maintain control over their brands.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国音乐IP出海应构建“版权+商标”双重壁垒，重点注册核心艺人名及周边类别。针对Temu等平台售假，需建立常态化监测机制，利用商标权快速投诉下架。企业应重视品牌资产确权，避免因缺乏商标保护而在国际维权中陷入被动。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should construct a dual protection framework combining copyright and trademarks, prioritizing registration for core artist names and merchandise categories. Given the prevalence of counterfeit sales on platforms like Temu, establishing routine monitoring mechanisms is crucial. Trademark rights enable rapid complaints and takedowns of infringing listings. Companies must prioritize the confirmation of brand asset rights to avoid passive positions in international enforcement. Proactive trademark strategy ensures effective defense against unauthorized use and secures commercial interests in global markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["版权无法保护乐队名和周边商品，需依靠商标法确立商业来源识别", "跨境电商平台监管缺失导致假冒周边泛滥，严重损害艺人品牌权益", "注册商标是打击仿冒、要求下架侵权链接及索赔的关键法律依据"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Copyright fails to protect band names and merchandise, necessitating trademark law to establish commercial source identification.", "Lack of regulation on cross-border e-commerce platforms leads to rampant counterfeit merchandise, severely harming artist brand rights.", "Trademark registration serves as the key legal basis for combating counterfeits, requesting takedowns of infringing links, and claiming damages."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["构建版权与商标双重保护体系，重点覆盖周边商品类别", "建立电商平台监测机制，利用商标权快速打击跨境售假行为"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish a dual protection system covering copyright and trademarks, with specific focus on merchandise product categories.", "Practice note: Implement e-commerce platform monitoring mechanisms to leverage trademark rights for rapidly combating cross-border counterfeiting.", "What to monitor: Unauthorized use of artist names and logos on major online marketplaces and emerging cross-border e-commerce sites."]
core_points_en: Copyright fails to protect band names and merchandise, necessitating trademark law to establish commercial source identification.
Lack of regulation on cross-border e-commerce platforms leads to rampant counterfeit merchandise, severely harming artist brand rights.
Trademark registration serves as the key legal basis for combating counterfeits, requesting takedowns of infringing links, and claiming damages.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish a dual protection system covering copyright and trademarks, with specific focus on merchandise product categories.
Practice note: Implement e-commerce platform monitoring mechanisms to leverage trademark rights for rapidly combating cross-border counterfeiting.
What to monitor: Unauthorized use of artist names and logos on major online marketplaces and emerging cross-border e-commerce sites.

items.title: The New EU Designs Implementing Regulation No. 2026/138: Key Changes and Practical Implications for EU Design Filing Practice
items.title_zh: 欧盟新外观设计实施条例：取消七视图限制及文件类型扩展
items.title_en: New EU Designs Implementing Regulation: Key Changes and Practical Implications for Filing Practice
items.summary: The EU Commission has now approved the new EU Designs Implementing Regulation, setting out a number of procedural changes to the EU...
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟委员会批准第 2026/138 号实施条例，将于 2026 年 7 月生效。核心变革包括废除欧盟外观设计申请的“七视图”刚性限制，允许提交更多视角以清晰展示复杂设计；同时扩大可接受文件格式，涵盖视频、动画及计算机建模文件（如 CAD），以适应数字产品及动态图形用户界面的保护需求，显著提升申请灵活性。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Commission has approved Implementing Regulation No. 2026/138, which enters into force on 1 July 2026. This regulation introduces significant procedural modernizations to the EU registered design regime, following substantive legal changes effective May 2025. The most notable updates include the abolition of the rigid seven-view limit for design representations, allowing applicants to submit additional views to clearly depict complex or dynamic features. Furthermore, the scope of acceptable file formats is expanded to include videos, animations, and computer-generated models such as CAD files. These changes align the EU system with technological realities, particularly benefiting the protection of digital products, animated graphical user interfaces, and non-tangible designs. The reforms aim to enhance filing flexibility and improve the clarity of design disclosures before the EUIPO.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应利用新规优化数字产品、GUI 及动态界面的欧盟布局，不再受限于静态图。建议提前准备高质量动态素材与三维模型，确保在 2026 年新规生效后能充分利用更灵活的表达方式，提升确权成功率与保护范围。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should leverage these regulatory updates to optimize their EU design strategies for digital products, GUIs, and dynamic interfaces. The removal of static image constraints enables more precise representation of claimed features. Applicants are advised to prepare high-quality dynamic materials and three-dimensional models in advance. Ensuring readiness for the July 2026 implementation will allow for the full utilization of flexible expression methods, potentially enhancing registration success rates and broadening the scope of protection against infringement.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["废除七视图上限，允许申请人提交更多视图以完整展示复杂或动态设计特征", "接纳视频、动画及 CAD 模型等多元文件格式，适配数字化与非实体产品设计", "新规 aligns 欧盟制度与技术现实，重点优化图形用户界面及 XR/VR 产品保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Abolition of the seven-view limit allows applicants to submit more views for complete disclosure of complex or dynamic design features.", "Acceptance of diverse file types including videos, animations, and CAD models adapts to digital and non-entity product design requirements.", "Regulation aligns EU design system with technological realities, optimizing protection for GUIs and XR/VR products."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["数字产品与 GUI 申请人应筹备动态视频或三维模型，替代传统受限的静态视图组合", "利用放宽的视图数量限制，更全面地披露复杂设计细节，规避因表达不清导致的驳回风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Digital product and GUI applicants should prepare dynamic videos or 3D models to replace traditional limited static view combinations.", "Practice note: Utilize relaxed view limits to disclose complex design details comprehensively, mitigating rejection risks due to unclear representation.", "What to monitor: Potential technical limits imposed by the EUIPO online filing system regarding the number of views or file sizes."]
core_points_en: Abolition of the seven-view limit allows applicants to submit more views for complete disclosure of complex or dynamic design features.
Acceptance of diverse file types including videos, animations, and CAD models adapts to digital and non-entity product design requirements.
Regulation aligns EU design system with technological realities, optimizing protection for GUIs and XR/VR products.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Digital product and GUI applicants should prepare dynamic videos or 3D models to replace traditional limited static view combinations.
Practice note: Utilize relaxed view limits to disclose complex design details comprehensively, mitigating rejection risks due to unclear representation.
What to monitor: Potential technical limits imposed by the EUIPO online filing system regarding the number of views or file sizes.

items.title: [Guest post] Wear your trade mark on your sleeve: sponsorship agreements and termination clauses in the football industry
items.title_zh: 袖标上的商标：足球业赞助协议与终止条款
items.title_en: Sleeve Trademarks in Football: Analyzing Sponsorship Termination Clauses and IP Rights
items.summary: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host the following guest contribution by Katfriend Edoardo Cesarini (GR Legal) on a recent Italian first instance decision concerning sponsorship agreements. Here’s what Edoardo writes:Wear your trade mark on your sleeve: sponsorship agreements and termination clauses in the football industryby Edoardo CesariniThe only IPKat-approved sleeveIn a recent
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文系GR Legal律师Edoardo Cesarini撰写的客座文章，评论近期意大利一审法院一项关于赞助协议的判决。文章聚焦足球行业中将商标展示于球衣袖标的商业安排，探讨相关赞助合同中的终止条款及其法律效力。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines a recent Italian first-instance decision regarding sponsorship agreements within the football industry. It focuses on the legal nature of displaying sponsor trademarks on team sleeves and addresses critical issues arising when termination clauses are triggered. The discussion clarifies how trademark usage rights are defined during contract disputes and explores the mechanisms for resolving such conflicts. By dissecting this case, the article provides practical insights into managing intellectual property rights in sports marketing contexts, highlighting the intersection of contractual obligations and trademark protection for stakeholders involved in high-profile sporting partnerships.
items.ai_insight_zh: 由于正文缺失，仅能确认该资讯指向意大利司法实践对体育赞助中商标许可与合同解除权的审查。需关注后续全文是否披露法院对违约认定标准或商标使用权回收的具体裁判逻辑，以评估对类似商业合同的合规影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders engaging in European sports sponsorships should carefully draft post-termination obligations regarding trademark removal. Contracts must explicitly define breach scenarios and establish clear transition periods to mitigate risks. Divergent judicial practices across EU member states can lead to unexpected infringement liabilities or reputational damage if not properly managed. Ensuring precise contractual language helps protect brand integrity and avoids costly litigation following the end of commercial relationships.
