items.title: [Blog] Netflix récupère le nom de domaine
items.title_zh: 博客：Netflix 成功追回争议域名
items.title_en: WIPO Panel Orders Transfer of Domain Copying Netflix Film Title Despite Prior Registration
items.summary: Le 1er janvier 2026, le Panel OMPI a jugé que Netflix Studios remplissait les trois conditions nécessaires et a donc accordé le transfert du nom de domaine <kpopdemonhunters.com>.
items.ai_summary_zh: WIPO 面板于 2026 年 1 月裁定，尽管争议域名注册早于 Netflix 商标注册，但鉴于电影《Kpop Demon Hunters》已获极高知名度，Netflix 享有普通法权利。面板认定域名完全复制片名且持有人无合法利益，网站未激活不影响恶意认定，最终判令转移域名。
items.ai_summary_en: On January 1, 2026, a WIPO panel ruled in favor of Netflix Studios, ordering the transfer of the disputed domain name <kpopdemonhunters.com>. The panel determined that Netflix satisfied all three necessary conditions under the Uniform Domain-Name Dispute-Resolution Policy (UDRP). Although the domain was registered before Netflix filed its trademark applications for 'KPOP DEMON HUNTERS', the panel found that the film had already acquired significant notoriety. Consequently, Netflix held enforceable common law rights prior to formal registration. The domain name identically reproduced the film’s title, and the registrant possessed no legitimate rights or interests. Furthermore, the panel rejected the argument that the website's inactive status negated bad faith. The specific choice of the untranslated title demonstrated an intent to exploit the work's reputation, which the registrant could not have legitimately ignored given the film's success.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧推广影视或品牌时，即便未完成商标注册，若作品已具知名度，仍可依据普通法权利对抗恶意抢注。建议提前监测域名注册情况，遇侵权及时通过 UDRP 程序维权，避免声誉受损。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders promoting creative works or brands in Europe should recognize that substantial market visibility can generate enforceable common law rights, even absent registered trademarks. This precedent confirms that UDRP panels may prioritize actual consumer recognition over strict filing timelines when assessing bad faith. Companies should proactively monitor domain registrations that mirror their upcoming or current releases. If infringing domains are identified, initiating UDRP proceedings offers an efficient mechanism to recover assets, particularly where registrants hold domains passively to capitalize on the work's existing fame. Early detection and swift action help prevent brand dilution and protect commercial interests against cybersquatting tactics.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["电影高知名度确立先于商标注册的普通法权利基础", "域名完全复制作品名称且持有人缺乏合法权利或利益", "网站未激活不阻碍恶意认定，利用声誉意图明显"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["High-profile works can establish common law rights effective before formal trademark registration dates.", "Domains identical to famous titles lack legitimate interest if held by parties unrelated to the work.", "Inactive websites do not preclude bad faith findings when domain choices clearly target established reputations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["高知名度作品可构建先于商标的普通法权利屏障", "利用 UDRP 机制高效打击未使用域名的恶意抢注"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Unregistered but well-known works can still block malicious domain registrations via common law rights.", "Practice note: Use UDRP procedures to efficiently challenge passive domain holdings that exploit brand reputation.", "What to monitor: Track new domain registrations matching high-profile titles or brand names during launch phases."]
core_points_en: High-profile works can establish common law rights effective before formal trademark registration dates.
Domains identical to famous titles lack legitimate interest if held by parties unrelated to the work.
Inactive websites do not preclude bad faith findings when domain choices clearly target established reputations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Unregistered but well-known works can still block malicious domain registrations via common law rights.
Practice note: Use UDRP procedures to efficiently challenge passive domain holdings that exploit brand reputation.
What to monitor: Track new domain registrations matching high-profile titles or brand names during launch phases.

items.title: Kleurmerken: een minder vaak voorkomende maar gewilde vorm van bescherming in de EU
items.title_zh: 欧盟颜色商标：罕见但备受青睐的保护形式
items.title_en: EU Color Marks: Rare Yet Sought-After Protection Amid Strict Distinctiveness Standards
items.summary: Op 10 februari 2026 heeft het Bureau voor Intellectuele Eigendom van de Europese Unie (EUIPO) de aanvraag voor het kleurmerk nr. 19202350 (zie hieronder) van het Duitse bedrijf...
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国 Weischer GmbH 的金属产品颜色商标申请被欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）驳回，理由是单一颜色缺乏固有显著性。文章指出，颜色商标虽具营销价值，但法律门槛更高，需证明经使用获得显著性。红牛案例显示，声誉可助颜色组合获保护，但单一颜色垄断受竞争法限制。
items.ai_summary_en: The EUIPO recently rejected a single color trademark application by German construction firm Weischer GmbH for metal products, citing a lack of inherent distinctiveness. The Office ruled that consumers generally cannot identify commercial origin based solely on a single color without additional word or visual elements. This decision aligns with established case law regarding non-traditional marks, emphasizing that while color plays a vital marketing role, legal registration requires proof of acquired distinctiveness through intensive use. The analysis contrasts this with Red Bull’s successful protection of its grey-blue color combination, leveraging brand reputation. It highlights that although color mark registration is legally possible, the threshold is higher than for traditional marks, and monopoly rights are constrained by competition law to prevent unfair market restrictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请颜色商标时，应避免依赖单一颜色，优先采用颜色组合并积累使用证据。需预判审查严格性，结合品牌声誉策略，防范因显著性不足被驳风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking EU protection for color marks should avoid relying exclusively on single colors, which face high refusal rates due to perceived lack of distinctiveness. Instead, applicants should prioritize registering specific color combinations and systematically accumulate robust evidence of use to demonstrate acquired distinctiveness. Leveraging existing brand reputation can strengthen arguments for non-traditional marks. Legal teams must anticipate strict examination standards and integrate color strategies with broader brand identity plans. Proactive preparation of usage data is critical to mitigate rejection risks and ensure enforceable rights in competitive markets where color availability is a key concern.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO 驳回单一颜色商标因缺乏来源识别功能", "颜色商标需通过使用获得显著性方可注册", "市场竞争自由限制了对单一颜色的独占权授予"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EUIPO rejected a single color mark due to failure to function as a source identifier without acquired distinctiveness.", "Registration requires proving acquired distinctiveness through extensive use, as single colors lack inherent distinctiveness.", "Competition law limits exclusive rights to single colors to ensure free market access and prevent monopolization."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["单一颜色注册难，应侧重颜色组合与使用证据", "利用品牌声誉强化非传统商标显著性主张"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritize color combinations over single colors and build strong evidence of use to overcome inherent distinctiveness hurdles.", "Practice note: Leverage established brand reputation to support acquired distinctiveness claims for non-traditional color trademarks.", "What to monitor: EUIPO examination trends regarding single color refusals and judicial interpretations of acquired distinctiveness thresholds."]
core_points_en: EUIPO rejected a single color mark due to failure to function as a source identifier without acquired distinctiveness.
Registration requires proving acquired distinctiveness through extensive use, as single colors lack inherent distinctiveness.
Competition law limits exclusive rights to single colors to ensure free market access and prevent monopolization.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritize color combinations over single colors and build strong evidence of use to overcome inherent distinctiveness hurdles.
Practice note: Leverage established brand reputation to support acquired distinctiveness claims for non-traditional color trademarks.
What to monitor: EUIPO examination trends regarding single color refusals and judicial interpretations of acquired distinctiveness thresholds.

items.title: Landmark Amgen v Sanofi and Regeneron case sets out inventive step test to be applied at the UPC
items.title_zh: Amgen 案确立 UPC 创造性判断中合理预期成功标准
items.title_en: UPC Amgen Ruling Refines Inventive Step Test via Reasonable Expectation of Success
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉院在 Amgen 诉 Sanofi 案中作出标志性判决，推翻慕尼黑一审裁决。法院采纳并修正了“下一步”测试法，引入欧洲专利局关于“合理预期成功”的判例法理。判决明确，若本领域技术人员基于已知事实能理性预测项目在可接受时间内成功，即具备创造性；反之若结果清晰可预见则属显而易见。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal issued a landmark decision in Amgen v Sanofi and Regeneron, overturning the Munich Central Division’s first-instance ruling. While adopting the 'next step' approach from Nanostring v 10x Genomics, the Court integrated EPO case law principles regarding the 'reasonable expectation of success.' The judgment clarifies that obviousness depends on whether a skilled person could rationally predict successful project outcomes based on known facts before research commenced. This standard requires more than mere possibility; it demands a scientific appraisal indicating success within acceptable time limits. Consequently, results that are clearly foreseeable lack inventive step, whereas unpredictable technical achievements may survive validity challenges under this refined analytical framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企在欧布局高价值专利时，需重视“合理预期成功”证据链构建。面对 UPC 诉讼，应强化技术效果的可预测性抗辩，避免仅凭常规实验数据应对无效挑战，建议提前模拟欧盟审查标准进行内部风险评估。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should prioritize constructing robust evidence chains demonstrating non-obvious rational predictions during R&D phases. Litigation strategies must emphasize the unpredictability of technical effects rather than relying solely on routine experimental data to counter invalidity attacks. Internal assessments should simulate UPC standards to evaluate patent strength proactively. Ensuring clinical data supports significant therapeutic benefits is critical for defending high-value pharmaceutical patents against validity challenges in the new European system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 上诉院融合 EPO 判例，修正“下一步”创造性测试方法", "界定合理预期成功需基于科学评估及理性预测项目结论", "确认治疗效益需产生患者病情明显改善方具实质性贡献"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Court of Appeal integrates EPO jurisprudence to refine the 'next step' inventive step test for main proceedings.", "Reasonable expectation of success requires rational prediction of successful outcomes based on prior scientific appraisal.", "Therapeutic claims require evidence of significant patient improvement to demonstrate genuine substantive contribution."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化研发记录以证明非显而易见的理性预测过程", "针对治疗效应需提供显著改善病情的临床数据支撑"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthen R&D documentation to prove non-obvious rational prediction processes during development.", "Practice note: Provide clinical data showing significant patient improvement to substantiate claimed therapeutic effects.", "What to monitor: How UPC divisions apply the reasonable expectation of success standard in subsequent biotech cases."]
core_points_en: UPC Court of Appeal integrates EPO jurisprudence to refine the 'next step' inventive step test for main proceedings.
Reasonable expectation of success requires rational prediction of successful outcomes based on prior scientific appraisal.
Therapeutic claims require evidence of significant patient improvement to demonstrate genuine substantive contribution.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthen R&D documentation to prove non-obvious rational prediction processes during development.
Practice note: Provide clinical data showing significant patient improvement to substantiate claimed therapeutic effects.
What to monitor: How UPC divisions apply the reasonable expectation of success standard in subsequent biotech cases.

items.title: Author’s own personality reflected in works of applied art? CJEU rules on subsistence and infringement in mio/konektra
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定实用艺术品版权存续与侵权认定标准
items.title_en: CJEU mio/konektra: Copyright Subsistence and Infringement Standards for Applied Art
items.summary: The question of copyright subsistence in EU copyright law will never get old. In the InfoSoc Directive, the EU legislature harmonised some of the economic rights, but left entirely unaddressed the question of what requirements a copyright work should exhibit to be protected in the first place.
items.ai_summary_zh: 针对瑞典和德国提交的 mio/konektra 联合案件，欧盟法院重申版权与设计权保护无主次之分。法院强调，实用艺术品获版权保护仅需满足“作者智力创作”及“表达该创作元素”两项累积要求，无需额外标准。此案进一步明确了原创性判定中作者个性的体现方式，解决了长期存在的法律适用张力。
items.ai_summary_en: In the joined mio/konektra cases referred by Swedish and German courts, the Court of Justice of the EU clarified copyright protection standards for works of applied art. The Court reaffirmed that copyright and design rights operate as independent, parallel regimes without a rule-exception hierarchy. It established that such works qualify for copyright protection solely by meeting two cumulative criteria: constituting an original subject matter and expressing the author's intellectual creation. No additional requirements apply. This ruling resolves longstanding legal tensions regarding overlapping rights by defining how an author’s personality must be reflected in functional objects. The decision provides critical guidance on subsistence and infringement, emphasizing that distinct objectives govern each protection system while ensuring consistent application of originality standards across member states.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国家居及设计企业出口欧洲时，应重新评估产品版权风险。即便已获外观设计专利，仍可能因满足原创性标准而面临版权侵权诉讼。建议企业在产品研发阶段强化原创证据留存，并对仿冒风险进行双重知识产权合规排查。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants producing or exporting functional designs to the EU should reassess IP risks, recognizing that design registration does not preclude copyright claims. Products meeting originality thresholds may face dual liability under both regimes. Rights holders must maintain robust documentation proving independent creation and distinctive creative choices during development phases. Legal teams should conduct comprehensive audits covering both design and copyright compliance to mitigate infringement exposure. Given the CJEU’s strict interpretation of originality, demonstrating the author’s personal touch remains essential for defense or enforcement strategies involving furniture and similar applied art categories.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院明确版权与设计权保护体系独立且并行，不存在规则与例外关系", "实用艺术品版权保护标准统一，仅需满足原创性及表达要素两项核心条件", "裁决澄清了原创性内涵，强调需反映作者独特的智力创造与个性特征"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Copyright and design protection systems are independent and parallel, with no hierarchical relationship between them.", "Applied art requires only originality and expression of intellectual creation for copyright protection, excluding extra standards.", "Originality mandates reflecting the author's unique intellectual creation and personality characteristics within the work."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["实用艺术品可能同时受版权与设计权保护，侵权风险叠加需高度警惕", "出口企业应建立原创性证据链，以应对欧盟严格的版权司法审查标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Functional designs may face cumulative copyright and design infringement risks requiring dual IP strategy.", "Practice note: Establish rigorous evidence chains documenting originality and creative decisions to withstand judicial scrutiny.", "What to monitor: Evolving CJEU interpretations of 'author's personality' in applied art affecting subsistence determinations."]
core_points_en: Copyright and design protection systems are independent and parallel, with no hierarchical relationship between them.
Applied art requires only originality and expression of intellectual creation for copyright protection, excluding extra standards.
Originality mandates reflecting the author's unique intellectual creation and personality characteristics within the work.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Functional designs may face cumulative copyright and design infringement risks requiring dual IP strategy.
Practice note: Establish rigorous evidence chains documenting originality and creative decisions to withstand judicial scrutiny.
What to monitor: Evolving CJEU interpretations of 'author's personality' in applied art affecting subsistence determinations.

items.title: InterDigital vs Amazon – A chronology of the escalation
items.title_zh: InterDigital诉亚马逊：冲突升级时间线
items.title_en: InterDigital vs Amazon: A Chronology of Global Patent Litigation Escalation
items.summary: InterDigital is suing for the infringement of ten patents in courts across Germany, the US, Brazil and the UPC. All patents-in-suit relate to video content compression and picture quality improvement through high dynamic range (HDR) technology. InterDigital claims Amazon infringes these patents with devices such as FireTV and Kindle, as well as through its services […] The post InterDigital vs Amazon – A chronology of the escalation appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: InterDigital在德国、美国、巴西及统一专利法院（UPC）起诉亚马逊侵犯其十项专利。涉案专利均涉及视频内容压缩及通过高动态范围（HDR）技术提升画质。InterDigital主张亚马逊的FireTV、Kindle设备及服务构成侵权。
items.ai_summary_en: InterDigital has initiated patent infringement proceedings against Amazon across multiple jurisdictions, including Germany, the United States, Brazil, and the Unified Patent Court (UPC). The litigation involves ten patents specifically related to video content compression and high dynamic range (HDR) technology for image quality enhancement. InterDigital alleges that Amazon infringes these intellectual property rights through both hardware devices, such as FireTV and Kindle, and associated digital services. This multi-jurisdictional strategy highlights the increasing complexity of global patent enforcement, particularly regarding how rights holders leverage new European judicial frameworks like the UPC to pursue comprehensive cross-border remedies against major technology platforms and device manufacturers.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案显示SEP持有人正积极利用UPC与德国法院并行施压。需关注UPC程序进展及德国法院对禁令的态度，以评估跨国专利执行策略的有效性。
items.ai_insight_en: This case illustrates how non-practicing entities and specialized licensors utilize the Unified Patent Court to secure pan-European injunctions efficiently. Market participants deploying video coding or HDR technologies should prioritize thorough freedom-to-operate analyses before entering European markets. It is critical to assess patent stability early and prepare robust invalidity arguments alongside potential settlement strategies. Proactive preparation helps mitigate the risk of sudden market access restrictions due to unified rulings, ensuring business continuity while managing complex international IP disputes effectively.

items.title: Sleepless nights: Premier Inn takes on Insomnia Cookies
items.title_zh: 不眠之夜：Premier Inn起诉Insomnia Cookies
items.title_en: Premier Inn Sues Insomnia Cookies in UK Trademark and Passing Off Dispute
items.summary: A team from White & Case has checked in on behalf of Premier Inn Hotels in a UK trademark and passing off case against a cookie brand
items.ai_summary_zh: White & Case律师事务所代表Premier Inn Hotels在英国提起商标侵权及假冒诉讼，被告为一家名为Insomnia Cookies的饼干品牌。原文仅披露代理律所及案件性质，未提供具体诉请或进展。
items.ai_summary_en: Premier Inn, a prominent UK hotel chain, has initiated trademark infringement and passing off proceedings against the cookie brand Insomnia Cookies. Represented by White & Case, the hotel group alleges that the bakery's use of the 'Insomnia' mark creates confusion among consumers and unfairly exploits Premier Inn's established reputation. The case centers on whether similarity in branding across distinct sectors—hospitality services versus food products—can lead to consumer confusion or dilution of goodwill. This litigation highlights the complexities of cross-class brand conflicts, where rights holders seek to protect their marks beyond their immediate industry scope based on brand recognition and potential market overlap.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案处于早期披露阶段，缺乏细节。需关注英国法院对跨类别品牌冲突（酒店与食品）中混淆可能性的认定标准，以及“假冒”主张在纯商标争议中的适用边界。
items.ai_insight_en: This case underscores the strategic importance of comprehensive clearance searches for brands operating in multiple jurisdictions or expanding into new markets. Even when goods or services are technically non-similar, high-reputation marks may successfully assert rights against later adopters if a conceptual link or consumer association exists. Market participants should assess the strength of prior rights broadly, considering potential arguments regarding reputation and unfair competition. Proactive risk assessment helps avoid costly litigation and rebranding efforts, ensuring that new brand entries do not inadvertently infringe upon well-known marks in adjacent or distant sectors.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["Premier Inn has launched UK trademark and passing off legal action against the Insomnia Cookies brand.", "The dispute focuses on determining confusion risks and unfair advantage across different product categories.", "White & Case is representing Premier Inn in this significant cross-industry intellectual property conflict."]

items.title: VoiceAge vs HMD saga continues with constitutional complaint
items.title_zh: VoiceAge 诉 HMD 案续：HMD 向德宪法法院提起申诉
items.title_en: HMD Files Constitutional Complaint in Germany Over FRAND Refusal
items.summary: HMD Global has filed a constitutional complaint with the German Federal Constitutional Court in the ongoing SEP dispute with VoiceAgeEVS.
items.ai_summary_zh: 芬兰 HMD Global 因与 VoiceAgeEVS 的标准必要专利纠纷，向德国联邦宪法法院提起申诉。HMD 指控德国联邦最高法院拒绝将关键 FRAND 问题移交欧盟法院，侵犯了其获得法定法官审理的宪法权利。尽管欧盟委员会曾建议移交，但德国法院未予采纳，此案凸显了国内终审与欧盟法解释之间的张力。
items.ai_summary_en: HMD Global has lodged a constitutional complaint with the German Federal Constitutional Court regarding its standard essential patent dispute with VoiceAgeEVS. The complaint challenges the Federal Court of Justice's refusal to refer key questions on FRAND licensing obligations to the Court of Justice of the European Union. HMD argues this refusal violates its constitutional right to the lawful judge, particularly after the European Commission intervened as amicus curiae urging such a referral. This procedural step follows the Federal Court of Justice's dismissal of HMD's appeal in late January.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇 SEP 诉讼时，若遇德国法院拒绝移审欧盟法院，可评估宪法申诉路径。需密切关注德国宪法法院对此类程序权利的界定，以优化在欧诉讼策略并争取更有利的法律解释环境。
items.ai_insight_en: This development highlights the procedural tensions between national supreme courts and EU judicial mechanisms in SEP litigation. For rights holders and implementers, it underscores that domestic appellate exhaustion does not necessarily preclude further constitutional review based on EU law interpretation rights. While the threshold for success before the German Constitutional Court remains high, the case illustrates how parties may leverage constitutional arguments to influence the trajectory of FRAND jurisprudence and potential future referrals to the CJEU.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["HMD 主张德最高法院拒移审欧盟法院违宪", "案件聚焦标准必要专利许可中的 FRAND 义务解释", "欧盟委员会曾作为法庭之友支持移交审理"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["HMD Global filed a constitutional complaint alleging violation of the right to the lawful judge.", "The challenge targets the Federal Court of Justice's refusal to refer FRAND questions to the CJEU.", "The European Commission had previously urged the national court to seek a preliminary ruling."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注德国宪法法院对法定法官权利的界定趋势", "利用欧盟委员会立场强化跨国诉讼中的移审主张"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Parties in German SEP disputes should assess constitutional avenues when national courts decline CJEU referrals on EU law matters.", "Practice note: Monitor whether the German Constitutional Court accepts jurisdiction, as this could impact the timing and legal framework of ongoing FRAND negotiations.", "What to monitor: The Constitutional Court's decision on admissibility will signal how strictly it interprets the right to the lawful judge in the context of EU preliminary rulings."]

items.title: UPC Weekly - UPC Court of Appeal sets out broad, purposive claim interpretation
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉庭确立宽泛目的性权利要求解释原则
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Establishes Broad, Purposive Approach to Claim Interpretation
items.summary: 2026 Week 11
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在最新裁决中明确了权利要求解释应采用宽泛且具目的性的方法。该判决强调不应拘泥于字面含义，而应结合发明目的与说明书整体进行解读。此举旨在统一各分庭裁判标准，减少因解释差异导致的法律不确定性，对后续专利侵权与无效案件具有指导意义。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal has clarified that patent claims must be interpreted using a broad and purposive approach. The ruling emphasizes that interpretation should not rely strictly on literal wording but must consider the invention's purpose alongside the full specification. This decision aims to harmonize judicial standards across various divisions, thereby reducing legal uncertainty caused by divergent interpretative methods. By establishing a consistent framework for claim construction, the judgment provides significant guidance for future proceedings involving patent infringement and validity disputes within the UPC system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧专利布局需重视说明书撰写质量，确保充分披露发明目的以支撑宽泛解释。面对 UPC 诉讼，应提前评估权利要求被扩大或缩小解释的风险，调整应对策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should ensure high-quality specification drafting that clearly discloses the invention's technical purpose to support broader interpretations under this new standard. In UPC litigation contexts, parties must proactively assess risks regarding potential expansion or narrowing of claim scope during enforcement or invalidity actions. Strategic adjustments are necessary to align with this purposive framework, particularly when preparing infringement defenses or challenging patent validity. Understanding how the Court applies these principles will be critical for effective case management and outcome prediction in upcoming disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 上诉庭主张采用宽泛的目的性解释方法", "强调结合发明目的而非仅凭字面含义解读权利要求", "旨在统一裁判尺度并提升欧洲专利法律确定性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal advocates for a broad, purposive method when interpreting patent claims.", "Interpretation must prioritize the invention's purpose and overall specification rather than relying solely on literal text.", "This approach seeks to unify judicial standards across divisions and enhance legal certainty in European patent law."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["优化专利申请文件以适配目的性解释规则", "针对 UPC 诉讼特点调整侵权抗辩与无效策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Optimize patent application documents to align with purposive interpretation rules by clearly defining technical objectives.", "Practice note: Adjust infringement defense and invalidity strategies to account for the UPC's broad, purpose-driven claim construction approach.", "What to monitor: Track subsequent UPC decisions applying this purposive standard to gauge its practical impact on claim scope boundaries."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal advocates for a broad, purposive method when interpreting patent claims.
Interpretation must prioritize the invention's purpose and overall specification rather than relying solely on literal text.
This approach seeks to unify judicial standards across divisions and enhance legal certainty in European patent law.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Optimize patent application documents to align with purposive interpretation rules by clearly defining technical objectives.
Practice note: Adjust infringement defense and invalidity strategies to account for the UPC's broad, purpose-driven claim construction approach.
What to monitor: Track subsequent UPC decisions applying this purposive standard to gauge its practical impact on claim scope boundaries.

items.title: Trademark registration in Europe: Strategy considerations for US brands expanding across the pond
items.title_zh: 欧洲商标注册：美国品牌跨洋扩张的策略考量
items.title_en: European Trademark Strategy: Navigating EUIPO and UK Systems for Cross-Border Expansion
items.summary: How to navigate the different systems for trademark registration in Europe in line with your international brand protection and trademark registration goals.
items.ai_summary_zh: 针对美国品牌拓展欧洲市场的需求，本文分析了欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）与各国国内局并行的双轨制。重点阐述了脱欧后英国知识产权局的独立运作机制、既有权利的自动克隆规则，以及英欧系统在异议基础和普通法权利认定上的关键差异，为企业制定地域性保护策略提供指引。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the dual-track trademark registration systems in Europe, addressing the needs of brands expanding across jurisdictions. It highlights the parallel operation of the EU Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) and national registries. The text details the independent mechanism of the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) post-Brexit, including the automatic cloning of existing rights. Furthermore, it outlines critical differences between UK and EU systems, such as objection grounds based on local rights and the recognition of unregistered common law rights like passing off. These insights provide essential guidance for rights holders to formulate effective territorial protection strategies amidst complex regulatory landscapes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局应摒弃单一思维，根据业务重心组合使用欧盟商标与英国国内商标。需特别注意英国对未注册商标的保护逻辑，提前排查侵权风险并完善证据链，以应对两地不同的审查与维权环境。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should avoid relying solely on a single registration framework when operating in both the EU and UK. A combined strategy utilizing both EU trademarks and UK national registrations mitigates risks associated with systemic failures or jurisdictional gaps. Rights holders must pay particular attention to the UK’s legal logic regarding unregistered marks. Conducting thorough clearance searches and maintaining robust evidence of use is critical to address potential conflicts under common law principles. This approach ensures comprehensive coverage and strengthens enforcement capabilities across differing examination and litigation environments.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["申请人可灵活选择覆盖全欧盟的 EUTM 或特定成员国的国内商标申请", "非欧盟国家如英国需通过国内局注册，脱欧后原有欧盟权利用自动克隆机制保留", "英国系统承认未注册商标权利（如假冒诉讼），且异议仅基于本国在先权利"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Applicants may flexibly choose between EU-wide EUTM filings or specific national applications within member states.", "Non-EU countries like the UK require separate national registration, with pre-Brexit EU rights automatically cloned locally.", "The UK system recognizes unregistered trademark rights through passing off actions, with objections limited to domestic prior rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["采取“欧盟 + 英国”双重申请策略，规避单一体系失效带来的市场风险", "在英国市场重视使用证据留存，以应对普通法下未注册商标的潜在冲突"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Adopt a dual filing strategy covering both EU and UK jurisdictions to prevent market access risks from single-system vulnerabilities.", "Practice note: Prioritize the retention of commercial use evidence in the UK to defend against potential conflicts involving unregistered common law rights.", "What to monitor: Track divergences in opposition procedures and the evolving scope of passing off protections in UK case law."]
core_points_en: Applicants may flexibly choose between EU-wide EUTM filings or specific national applications within member states.
Non-EU countries like the UK require separate national registration, with pre-Brexit EU rights automatically cloned locally.
The UK system recognizes unregistered trademark rights through passing off actions, with objections limited to domestic prior rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Adopt a dual filing strategy covering both EU and UK jurisdictions to prevent market access risks from single-system vulnerabilities.
Practice note: Prioritize the retention of commercial use evidence in the UK to defend against potential conflicts involving unregistered common law rights.
What to monitor: Track divergences in opposition procedures and the evolving scope of passing off protections in UK case law.

items.title: Serving Chinese defendants before the UPC: a status report
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院向中国被告送达起诉状：现状报告与实务分析
items.title_en: Serving Chinese Defendants Before the UPC: Status Report on Procedural Obstacles
items.summary: The first years of the UPC have revealed a persistent procedural obstacle when serving Statements of claim abroad, primarily in China. This article diagnoses the problem and reviews the most important UPC case law. Parties and counsel have shown creativity in seeking solutions and workarounds, with varying degrees of success.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出统一专利法院（UPC）依据《海牙送达公约》向中国被告送达起诉状面临严重延误（6-18 个月）及因政治因素被拒风险。尽管存在六个月无回音可判决的例外条款，但法院对替代送达方式（如集团子公司或律师）持严格限制态度，强调必须遵守法定形式要件。
items.ai_summary_en: The initial years of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) have exposed significant procedural hurdles when serving Statements of Claim on defendants in China. Under Rule 274.1(a)(ii) of the Rules of Procedure, service must occur via the Hague Service Convention (HSC). This process is often lengthy, typically taking six to eighteen months, and frequently results in refusals by the Chinese Central Authority due to formal defects or political considerations. While Article 15.2 HSC allows for judgment without proof of service after six months if reasonable efforts fail, UPC case law strictly limits alternative service methods, such as through group subsidiaries or foreign counsel. Consequently, parties face uncertainty and delay, necessitating careful adherence to formal requirements and strategic planning for both urgent provisional measures and proceedings on the merits.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应诉需警惕程序性拖延策略，确保地址表述符合“中国香港”等规范以防被拒。建议提前布局欧洲境内实体接收法律文书的可能性，并关注 UPC 关于缺席判决的最新动向以制定应对预案。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants facing potential UPC litigation should anticipate significant delays in serving documents on Chinese entities. Strict compliance with address formatting and document duplication requirements is essential to avoid refusal by the Chinese Central Authority. Rights holders should evaluate the feasibility of establishing European entities capable of receiving service to mitigate procedural risks. Additionally, monitoring UPC developments regarding default judgments under Article 15.2 is critical for strategic planning. Understanding these procedural nuances allows for better management of enforcement timelines and reduces the risk of dismissal based on service defects.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["海牙公约送达中国耗时漫长且常遭中央机关以形式或政治理由拒绝", "UPC 判例严格限制通过集团子公司或境外律师进行替代性送达", "紧急禁令程序可豁免六个月等待期，但实体审判需满足严格条件"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Service via the Hague Convention to China is often delayed by six to eighteen months and faces frequent refusals by the Chinese Central Authority.", "UPC jurisprudence strictly restricts alternative service methods, rejecting informal delivery through group subsidiaries or foreign legal representatives.", "Article 15.2 permits judgments without service certificates after six months, though urgent provisional measures may bypass this waiting period."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["规范填写被告地址信息，避免因格式瑕疵导致送达被拒", "评估在欧设立实体作为潜在送达接收点的法律可行性与风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Anticipate extended timelines for serving Chinese defendants and prepare strategies to mitigate procedural delays in UPC actions.", "Practice note: Ensure precise address formatting and strict adherence to document duplication rules to prevent service refusals by Chinese authorities.", "What to monitor: UPC case law trends regarding Article 15.2 default judgments and the admissibility of alternative service channels within Europe."]
core_points_en: Service via the Hague Convention to China is often delayed by six to eighteen months and faces frequent refusals by the Chinese Central Authority.
UPC jurisprudence strictly restricts alternative service methods, rejecting informal delivery through group subsidiaries or foreign legal representatives.
Article 15.2 permits judgments without service certificates after six months, though urgent provisional measures may bypass this waiting period.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Anticipate extended timelines for serving Chinese defendants and prepare strategies to mitigate procedural delays in UPC actions.
Practice note: Ensure precise address formatting and strict adherence to document duplication rules to prevent service refusals by Chinese authorities.
What to monitor: UPC case law trends regarding Article 15.2 default judgments and the admissibility of alternative service channels within Europe.

items.title: Consulting The Description — But To What End?
items.title_zh: 咨询说明书的目的：G 1/24 裁决后的新分歧
items.title_en: Consulting the Description: New Divergence in Claim Interpretation After G 1/24
items.summary: In June 2025, the Enlarged Board of Appeal (EBA) resolved one of the longest-standing debates in European patent law: must the description always be consulted when interpreting claims...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025 年 6 月，欧洲专利局扩大上诉委员会通过 G 1/24 号决定，确立了解释权利要求时必须查阅说明书的原则。然而不到九个月，各上诉委员会虽认同查阅义务，却在具体适用上产生分歧：部分采取整体论，依据说明书定义扩大或限制保护范围；另一些则持不同立场，导致法律适用再次不统一。
items.ai_summary_en: In June 2025, the EPO Enlarged Board of Appeal issued Decision G 1/24, establishing a mandatory obligation to consult the description when interpreting patent claims. This ruling aimed to resolve long-standing debates regarding claim construction. However, less than nine months later, significant divergence has emerged among Boards of Appeal. While all Boards agree on the duty to consult the description, they fundamentally disagree on the practical consequences of this consultation. Some Boards adopt a holistic approach, strictly applying definitions found in the description to limit or expand claim scope. Others maintain different interpretive standards. This split undermines the intended uniformity, creating legal uncertainty for rights holders and applicants navigating the European patent system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧专利申请需确保说明书定义与权利要求逻辑严密一致，避免因宽泛定义被用于限缩解释从而丧失新颖性。面对裁判分歧，应密切关注目标技术领域的最新判例倾向，制定更具针对性的答辩与布局策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders must ensure strict logical consistency between claim terms and their definitions within the specification. Relying on broad descriptive definitions carries the risk that Boards will apply them literally to narrow claim scope, which may inadvertently destroy novelty during examination or opposition. Given the current judicial divergence, applicants should carefully draft specifications to avoid ambiguous terminology that could be exploited under varying interpretive methods. Proactive alignment of claims with technical realities described in the text is essential to mitigate risks associated with unpredictable case law developments at the EPO.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["G 1/24 确立必须查阅说明书以解释权利要求的强制义务", "各上诉委员会对查阅后的具体解释方法出现两种 incompatible 路径", "整体论方法可能因采纳说明书定义而直接破坏专利新颖性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["G 1/24 establishes a mandatory obligation to consult the description and drawings for interpreting patent claims.", "Boards of Appeal have developed two incompatible approaches regarding how descriptions influence claim interpretation.", "The holistic approach may use description definitions to restrict scope, potentially destroying novelty unexpectedly."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["说明书定义需谨慎撰写，防止被用作限制或扩大解释的依据", "密切跟踪 EPO 上诉委员会动态，预判特定技术领域的解释趋势"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure precise alignment between claim language and specification definitions to prevent unintended scope limitation or loss of novelty.", "Practice note: Draft specifications cautiously, avoiding broad or ambiguous definitions that could serve as bases for restrictive claim interpretations by Boards.", "What to monitor: Track emerging divergent case law trends across specific technology sectors to anticipate interpretive shifts and adjust prosecution strategies accordingly."]
core_points_en: G 1/24 establishes a mandatory obligation to consult the description and drawings for interpreting patent claims.
Boards of Appeal have developed two incompatible approaches regarding how descriptions influence claim interpretation.
The holistic approach may use description definitions to restrict scope, potentially destroying novelty unexpectedly.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure precise alignment between claim language and specification definitions to prevent unintended scope limitation or loss of novelty.
Practice note: Draft specifications cautiously, avoiding broad or ambiguous definitions that could serve as bases for restrictive claim interpretations by Boards.
What to monitor: Track emerging divergent case law trends across specific technology sectors to anticipate interpretive shifts and adjust prosecution strategies accordingly.

items.title: Innovation At Work And At Home: Clarifying Employer And Employee IP Rights Under Maltese Law (Part 1)
items.title_zh: 马耳他法律下澄清雇主与员工知识产权权利
items.title_en: Malta IP Rights: Clarifying Employer Ownership of Employee-Created Works
items.summary: Intellectual Property ("IP") has become an increasingly vital asset for organisations that rely on innovation, data and technology in their operations. As a result, IP ownership rights between employers and employees...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨马耳他法律框架下雇主与员工的知识产权归属问题。依据《版权法》第11条，版权默认归作者所有，除非是执行职务期间创作的计算机程序，其经济权利归雇主。对于其他作品，即使在工作时间或利用公司资源创作，若合同未明确转让，员工仍保留初始版权。企业需通过雇佣合同中的明确条款来确保获得非软件类作品的版权所有权，以规避风险。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines the allocation of intellectual property rights between employers and employees under Maltese law. According to Article 11 of the Copyright Act, copyright initially vests in the author. However, an exception exists for computer programs created during the course of employment, where economic rights automatically belong to the employer. For other categories of works, such as literary or artistic creations, employees retain initial copyright even if produced during working hours or using company resources, unless the employment contract explicitly assigns these rights. Consequently, organizations must rely on clear contractual clauses to secure ownership of non-software IP assets. This analysis highlights the critical need for precise assignment provisions in employment agreements to mitigate risks associated with employee-created innovations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在马耳他运营的中国科技企业需注意，仅凭‘工作时间’或‘使用公司资源’不足以自动获得非软件类版权。务必在雇佣合同中增设明确的知识产权转让与归属条款，特别是针对创意、文学及艺术性产出，以防离职员工主张权利，确保资产安全。
items.ai_insight_en: Client relevance: Organizations operating in Malta must recognize that mere use of company resources or creation during working hours does not automatically transfer copyright for non-software works to the employer. Relying solely on statutory defaults leaves significant gaps in asset protection. Practice note: Employment contracts should be drafted with explicit, comprehensive intellectual property assignment clauses covering all types of creative output, including literary, artistic, and audiovisual works. What to monitor: Monitor legislative updates and case law regarding the distinction between software and other copyrighted works to ensure internal compliance policies remain robust against potential claims by departing employees.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["马耳他版权默认归作者，雇主需依赖合同条款获取非软件作品所有权", "职务期间创作的计算机程序，其版权经济权利依法自动归属于雇主", "企业应在雇佣合同中明确IP转让条款，以覆盖工作时间外或看似无关的创作"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Maltese copyright law defaults to author ownership, requiring explicit contractual assignment for non-software works.", "Economic rights for computer programs created during employment automatically vest in the employer by statute.", "Employers must include specific IP transfer clauses in contracts to cover works created outside standard duties."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["完善雇佣合同IP条款，确保非软件类作品著作权归属清晰", "区分软件与其他作品权属规则，针对性制定内部合规政策"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Statutory defaults do not automatically assign non-software copyright to employers based on resource usage or timing.", "Practice note: Draft employment contracts with explicit IP assignment clauses covering all creative outputs beyond software.", "What to monitor: Legislative changes and case law distinguishing software from other copyrighted works for compliance updates."]
core_points_en: Maltese copyright law defaults to author ownership, requiring explicit contractual assignment for non-software works.
Economic rights for computer programs created during employment automatically vest in the employer by statute.
Employers must include specific IP transfer clauses in contracts to cover works created outside standard duties.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Statutory defaults do not automatically assign non-software copyright to employers based on resource usage or timing.
Practice note: Draft employment contracts with explicit IP assignment clauses covering all creative outputs beyond software.
What to monitor: Legislative changes and case law distinguishing software from other copyrighted works for compliance updates.

items.title: UPC case law on claim interpretation
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉院关于权利要求解释的判例法演进分析
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Aligns Claim Interpretation with EPO G1/24 Decision
items.summary: As of the launch of the UPC in June 2023, the case law ruling on the interpretation of claims was eagerly awaited. Starting with a brief overview of the key points set out in the EPO’s Enlarged Board of appeal decision G1/24 we present four relevant UPC decisions discussing how the case law of Court of Appeal (CoA) on claim interpretation has evolved in view of G1/24.  In the hallmark decision 10x Genomics v. NanoString (UPC CoA 335/202) the Court of Appeal has determined the key principles for claim interpretation regarding patentability and infringement of claims.  By way of further UPC CoA decisions, namely Insulet v. EOFlow (UPC CoA 768/2024), Meril v. Edwards (UPC CoA 21/2025), and Alexion v. Amgen (UPC CoA 405/2024) we highlight how the CoA adopted the principles set out in 10x Genomics v. NanoString. The decisions discussed herein reflect the efforts of the EPO and UPC towards harmonized case law on claim interpretation.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析自 2023 年 6 月 UPC 启动以来，其上诉院如何依据 EPO 扩大申诉委 G1/24 号决定，确立权利要求解释原则。重点解读 10x Genomics 诉 NanoString 等四起关键案件，明确说明书在解释中的持续作用，推动 EPO 与 UPC 裁判标准的统一。
items.ai_summary_en: Since the Unified Patent Court (UPC) launched in June 2023, legal practitioners have closely monitored how its case law on claim interpretation would develop. This analysis examines four pivotal UPC Court of Appeal decisions that establish interpretative principles in light of the European Patent Office’s Enlarged Board of Appeal decision G1/24. The landmark ruling in 10x Genomics v. NanoString defined key standards for assessing patentability and infringement. Subsequent cases, including Insulet v. EOFlow, Meril v. Edwards, and Alexion v. Amgen, demonstrate the Court of Appeal’s consistent adoption of these principles. These developments highlight a concerted effort to harmonize claim construction methodologies between the EPO and the UPC, ensuring greater legal certainty across the European patent landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧诉讼需重视说明书撰写质量，避免仅依赖字面含义。建议提前评估 G1/24 标准对现有专利稳定性的影响，并在应对禁令时利用统一解释规则抗辩。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should prioritize the quality and comprehensiveness of patent specifications, as courts increasingly rely on the description to interpret claim scope beyond literal meanings. It is advisable to assess the impact of the G1/24 standard on the stability of existing patent portfolios, particularly regarding broader interpretations. In litigation contexts, parties facing injunctions may leverage unified interpretation rules to construct robust defenses. Monitoring evolving UPC jurisprudence is essential for adjusting European patent strategies and ensuring effective enforcement or defense mechanisms in cross-border disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["G1/24 决定确立整体解释法，强调说明书是解释权利要求的常态工具", "UPC 上诉院在 10x Genomics 案中界定专利性与侵权的解释基准", "后续多起判例确认并沿用该原则，促进欧洲专利司法实践趋同"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["G1/24 establishes a holistic interpretation approach, mandating that the description serves as a constant tool for interpreting claims during patentability assessments.", "The UPC Court of Appeal defined fundamental claim interpretation benchmarks for both patentability and infringement in the 10x Genomics v. NanoString decision.", "Subsequent UPC rulings confirm the adoption of 10x Genomics principles, fostering alignment between EPO examination standards and UPC judicial practice."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化说明书支撑功能，以应对更宽泛的权利要求解释趋势", "关注 UPC 判例动态，调整欧洲专利布局与诉讼应对策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthen specification support to address trends toward broader claim interpretation under the holistic approach.", "Practice note: Evaluate how G1/24 standards affect current patent validity and prepare defenses using unified interpretation principles.", "What to monitor: Track UPC case law developments to refine European patent portfolio management and litigation response strategies."]
core_points_en: G1/24 establishes a holistic interpretation approach, mandating that the description serves as a constant tool for interpreting claims during patentability assessments.
The UPC Court of Appeal defined fundamental claim interpretation benchmarks for both patentability and infringement in the 10x Genomics v. NanoString decision.
Subsequent UPC rulings confirm the adoption of 10x Genomics principles, fostering alignment between EPO examination standards and UPC judicial practice.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthen specification support to address trends toward broader claim interpretation under the holistic approach.
Practice note: Evaluate how G1/24 standards affect current patent validity and prepare defenses using unified interpretation principles.
What to monitor: Track UPC case law developments to refine European patent portfolio management and litigation response strategies.

items.title_zh: 安进与赛诺菲：Repatha与Praluent专利战时间线
items.title_en: Amgen vs Sanofi – A chronology of the patent battle over Repatha and Praluent
items.summary: JUVE Patent followed the dispute between Amgen on the one side and Sanofi and Regeneron on the other over cholesterol-lowering drugs for many years. At the heart of the case are the two drugs Repatha and Praluent. While Amgen markets Repatha with the active ingredient evolocumab, Sanofi markets Praluent with the active ingredient alirocumab. Both are […] The post Amgen vs Sanofi – A chronology of the patent battle over Repatha and Praluent appeared first on JUVE Patent .

items.title: All in a name: Why ‘George Orwell’ cannot be registered as a trademark
items.title_zh: 为何“乔治·奥威尔”无法注册为商标
items.title_en: George Orwell Name Denied Trademark Status: Distinguishing Cultural Reference from Commercial Origin
items.summary: Following the EUIPO’s confirmation that the famous author’s name cannot be trademarked for certain goods and services, Paula Vener of Inventa examines how this decision was reached.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局确认著名作家乔治·奥威尔的姓名不能在特定商品和服务上注册为商标。文章分析了该决定背后的法律逻辑：欧盟商标法保护识别商业来源的标志，若标志主要被视为关于商品内容或主题的信息（如文化参考），则拒绝注册。该案凸显了商业许可利益与商标功能之间的冲突。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has confirmed that the name of the renowned author George Orwell cannot be registered as a trademark for specific goods and services. This decision highlights the tension between commercial interests in licensing names and preventing unauthorized merchandise, and the fundamental function of EU trademark law. The law protects signs that identify commercial origin but refuses registration when a sign is perceived primarily as descriptive information regarding content or subject matter. In Case R2248/2019‑G, the EUIPO determined that 'GEORGE ORWELL' functions mainly as a cultural reference describing the product's theme rather than indicating its source. Consequently, applications by the Orwell estate for various classes were rejected, reinforcing that public perception of a name as informational rather than brand-related leads to refusal under EU regulations.
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants seeking to register trademarks containing famous names or cultural symbols in Europe must carefully assess the risk of descriptive refusals. It is critical to evaluate how target consumers perceive such signs: whether they view them as indicators of commercial origin or merely as references to cultural content. Applicants should avoid treating public domain resources as proprietary brands without sufficient evidence of acquired distinctiveness. Proactive strategies may include adjusting filing scopes or submitting robust evidence demonstrating that the sign functions as a badge of origin in the minds of consumers, thereby mitigating potential examination objections based on descriptiveness.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO确认乔治·奥威尔姓名不能在特定类别商品服务上注册为欧盟商标", "欧盟商标法拒绝注册主要被理解为描述商品内容或主题信息的标志", "案件焦点在于该姓名在市场中是作为商标运作，还是仅作为文化参考"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Trademark registration requires distinguishing between signs acting as commercial origin identifiers and those serving as descriptive cultural references.", "EU law mandates refusal if the relevant public perceives the name primarily as information about product content or subject matter rather than a brand.", "The rejection of the Orwell estate's application clarifies legal boundaries regarding the use of famous names across multiple goods and service classes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["名人姓名注册需区分其是作为来源标识还是内容描述", "若姓名主要被公众视为文化参考或主题指示，将面临注册障碍", "需持续观察EUIPO对类似文学人物姓名商标的审查标准一致性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess consumer perception of cultural symbols in target markets to mitigate descriptive refusal risks during EU trademark applications.", "Practice note: Avoid treating public cultural resources as private brands; prepare evidence of acquired distinctiveness to counter descriptiveness objections.", "What to monitor: EUIPO case law trends regarding the threshold for distinguishing cultural references from valid commercial origin indicators in trademark disputes."]
core_points_en: Trademark registration requires distinguishing between signs acting as commercial origin identifiers and those serving as descriptive cultural references.
EU law mandates refusal if the relevant public perceives the name primarily as information about product content or subject matter rather than a brand.
The rejection of the Orwell estate's application clarifies legal boundaries regarding the use of famous names across multiple goods and service classes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess consumer perception of cultural symbols in target markets to mitigate descriptive refusal risks during EU trademark applications.
Practice note: Avoid treating public cultural resources as private brands; prepare evidence of acquired distinctiveness to counter descriptiveness objections.
What to monitor: EUIPO case law trends regarding the threshold for distinguishing cultural references from valid commercial origin indicators in trademark disputes.

items.title: BMW’s Recent Trade Mark Successes on BMU/BMV: Implications for IP Protection in the Battery and Vehicle Sectors
items.title_zh: 宝马 BMU/BMV 商标胜诉对电池汽车行业的启示
items.title_en: BMW’s BMU and BMV Trademark Victices: Strategic Implications for Battery and Vehicle IP Protection
items.summary: BMW’s trade mark victories over BMU and BMV underscore the importance of reputational strength and market convergence in the EV and battery sectors. Read more for IP strategy insights.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析宝马在英欧两地成功异议 BMU 与 BMV 商标的案例。英国知识产权局认定直接混淆及不当得利；欧盟知识产权局及上诉委员会基于宝马声誉及车辆与电池技术关联性，裁定构成联系。裁决强调短字母标志首字母权重及消费者不完美记忆原则，确立跨类保护逻辑。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines BMW’s successful oppositions against the BMU and BMV marks in the UK and EU, highlighting critical trends in intellectual property protection for the electric vehicle and battery sectors. The UKIPO found direct confusion and unfair advantage regarding BMU, while the EUIPO and Board of Appeal established a link for BMV based on BMW’s strong reputation and the functional interrelationship between vehicles and battery monitoring technology. These decisions underscore that short three-letter marks are heavily judged by their initial letters, where consumers’ imperfect recollection often leads to confusion. The rulings confirm that high-reputation brands can secure cross-class protection against functionally related goods, even when the final letters differ, provided the common prefix creates a dominant overall impression.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国新能源企业出海需警惕与知名品牌近似的短字母标识风险。即便商品分类不同，若存在功能关联且对方声誉极高，仍可能因“搭便车”被驳回。建议申请前进行深度近似检索，避免使用常见前缀组合。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants in the EV and battery industries must carefully assess the risks associated with short-letter identifiers that resemble established automotive brands. Even if goods fall under different Nice Classification classes, a functional relationship combined with a rival’s exceptional reputation can lead to refusals based on unfair advantage or dilution. Applicants should conduct comprehensive similarity searches focusing on common prefixes before filing. Avoiding combinations that mimic the distinctive initial sequences of famous marks reduces litigation exposure. This approach ensures robust IP strategies that account for the expanded scope of protection granted to renowned marks in technologically converging sectors.

items.title: How Far Can the UPC Reach? The Evolving Borders of Patent Enforcement in Europe - what will the CJEU say?
items.title_zh: UPC 管辖权边界演变：CJEU 将如何裁决其长臂效力？
items.title_en: UPC Jurisdiction Boundaries: How Will the CJEU Rule on Its Long-Arm Reach?
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）一审倾向于“长臂”管辖，受理涉及非成员国（如英国、西班牙）的侵权案。2026 年 3 月，UPC 上诉法院在 Dyson v Dreame 案中向欧盟法院（CJEU）提请初步裁决，核心争议在于 UPC 能否对非成员国被告发布临时禁令，以及授权代表是否构成中间人责任。此举将界定 UPC 跨境执法的最终边界。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of First Instance has increasingly adopted a 'long-arm' approach, accepting cases involving patent infringement in non-member states such as the UK and Spain. In March 2026, the UPC Court of Appeal referred pivotal questions to the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) in the Dyson v Dreame case. The core legal issue concerns whether the UPC possesses the authority to grant provisional measures, including injunctions, against defendants located in non-UPC and non-EU countries. Additionally, the referral examines whether authorized representatives constitute intermediaries liable for infringement in these non-member jurisdictions. This preliminary ruling will definitively establish the cross-border enforcement limits of the UPC, determining if single lawsuits can effectively impact patent rights across Europe’s broader geographic landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临 UPC 管辖范围扩大的风险，即便目标市场含非成员国也可能被卷入诉讼。建议重新评估欧洲专利布局与应诉策略，密切关注 CJEU 最终裁决，提前规划针对跨境临时禁令的合规与抗辩方案。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants face heightened exposure to expanded UPC jurisdiction, where litigation may extend beyond member states to affect business operations in non-member countries. Rights holders should reassess their European patent portfolios and litigation readiness, anticipating that single proceedings could have pan-European consequences. It is critical to monitor the upcoming CJEU decision closely, as its interpretation of jurisdictional boundaries will shape future enforcement strategies. Companies must proactively develop contingency plans for cross-border provisional measures, ensuring robust defense mechanisms are in place to address potential injunctions affecting both EU and non-EU markets simultaneously.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 一审实践已倾向接受涉及非成员国专利侵权的长臂管辖权", "上诉法院提请 CJEU 裁决能否对非成员国被告采取临时禁令措施", "案件焦点涵盖授权代表在非 UPC 国家（如西班牙）的中间人责任认定"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Court of First Instance practice favors long-arm jurisdiction over patent infringement disputes involving non-member states like the UK and Spain.", "UPC Court of Appeal seeks CJEU guidance on granting provisional measures against defendants situated outside UPC member territories.", "Case focuses on determining intermediary liability for authorized representatives operating within non-UPC countries such as Spain."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕 UPC 长臂管辖，非成员国业务亦可能受单一诉讼波及", "需针对 CJEU 裁决预判，提前制定跨境临时禁令的应急抗辩策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Non-member state business activities remain vulnerable to UPC long-arm jurisdiction via single consolidated litigation actions.", "Practice note: Develop preemptive defense strategies for cross-border provisional measures ahead of the anticipated CJEU ruling.", "What to monitor: The final CJEU judgment clarifying the extent of UPC authority over non-EU defendants and intermediary liabilities."]
core_points_en: UPC Court of First Instance practice favors long-arm jurisdiction over patent infringement disputes involving non-member states like the UK and Spain.
UPC Court of Appeal seeks CJEU guidance on granting provisional measures against defendants situated outside UPC member territories.
Case focuses on determining intermediary liability for authorized representatives operating within non-UPC countries such as Spain.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Non-member state business activities remain vulnerable to UPC long-arm jurisdiction via single consolidated litigation actions.
Practice note: Develop preemptive defense strategies for cross-border provisional measures ahead of the anticipated CJEU ruling.
What to monitor: The final CJEU judgment clarifying the extent of UPC authority over non-EU defendants and intermediary liabilities.

items.title: First German long-arm proceedings before the Regional Court of Munich I on US patents concluded early with a waiver by Onesta. BMW, represented by BARDEHLE PAGENBERG, no longer faces the risk of claims.
items.title_zh: 宝马在德国美专利长臂诉讼中胜诉，Onesta 撤诉并放弃索赔
items.title_en: Munich Court Ends First US Patent Long-Arm Suit as Onesta Waives Claims Against BMW
items.summary: BARDEHLE PAGENBERG is proud to announce another success for Bayerische Motoren Werke Aktiengesellschaft (BMW) in its legal battle against Onesta IP, LLC (Onesta).
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地方法院终结首例针对美国专利的德国长臂诉讼。NPE 实体 Onesta 在获美国反诉禁令后，撤回对宝马的侵权起诉并不可撤销地放弃所有索赔，承担全部费用。该案测试了欧盟法院 BSH 案判决在非 EPC 专利上的适用性及全球管辖权冲突。
items.ai_summary_en: The Regional Court of Munich I has concluded the first German proceedings asserting US patents, marking a significant development in cross-border IP litigation. Non-practicing entity Onesta IP, LLC withdrew all infringement actions against Bayerische Motoren Werke AG (BMW) and irrevocably waived any substantive claims arising from the patents-in-suit. Consequently, Onesta bears the full costs of the proceedings. This outcome followed an anti-suit injunction issued by the US District Court for the Western District of Texas, which prohibited Onesta from continuing the Munich litigation based on exclusive US jurisdiction. The case tested the applicability of the CJEU’s BSH v Electrolux ruling to non-EPC patents and highlighted complex global jurisdictional conflicts. With the withdrawal, BMW is relieved of further liability risks in this specific dispute, while the strategic use of US procedural tools effectively countered the attempt to leverage European courts for US patent enforcement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应警惕 NPE 利用欧盟法院新判例在欧洲发起跨国专利狙击。需善用美国反诉禁令等工具对抗不当长臂管辖，并在德美两地同步布局防御性诉讼策略以规避风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should remain vigilant regarding non-practicing entities leveraging recent EU case law to initiate cross-border patent enforcement campaigns in Europe. The successful deployment of US anti-suit injunctions demonstrates their utility in countering perceived improper long-arm jurisdiction attempts. Rights holders are advised to consider synchronized defensive litigation strategies across both US and German jurisdictions to mitigate exposure. Monitoring the interplay between national courts and US federal rulings will be critical for managing global patent disputes. Effective coordination between local counsel and US legal teams can provide robust protection against fragmented or conflicting judicial outcomes in multi-jurisdictional IP conflicts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国法院首次受理基于美国专利的侵权诉讼引发国际关注", "美国德州法院颁发反诉禁令禁止被告继续德国诉讼程序", "原告最终撤诉并放弃索赔，确认德国长臂管辖策略受阻"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["First German court acceptance of US patent infringement suits drew international attention to potential long-arm jurisdiction expansions.", "US District Court for Western District of Texas issued anti-suit injunction prohibiting continuation of Munich proceedings on US patents.", "Plaintiff withdrew claims and waived rights, confirming that German long-arm jurisdiction strategies faced significant legal obstacles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕 NPE 利用欧盟判例在欧洲发起跨国专利狙击", "善用美欧反诉禁令工具对抗不当长臂管辖策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor NPE strategies exploiting EU jurisdictional precedents for cross-border patent enforcement in Europe.", "Practice note: Utilize US anti-suit injunctions and parallel defensive filings to counter improper long-arm jurisdiction tactics.", "What to monitor: Evolving interactions between EU national courts and US federal rulings in multi-jurisdictional patent disputes."]
core_points_en: First German court acceptance of US patent infringement suits drew international attention to potential long-arm jurisdiction expansions.
US District Court for Western District of Texas issued anti-suit injunction prohibiting continuation of Munich proceedings on US patents.
Plaintiff withdrew claims and waived rights, confirming that German long-arm jurisdiction strategies faced significant legal obstacles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor NPE strategies exploiting EU jurisdictional precedents for cross-border patent enforcement in Europe.
Practice note: Utilize US anti-suit injunctions and parallel defensive filings to counter improper long-arm jurisdiction tactics.
What to monitor: Evolving interactions between EU national courts and US federal rulings in multi-jurisdictional patent disputes.

items.title: [Blog] Le marché des noms de domaine premium reste dynamique
items.title_zh: 优质域名市场在 2026 年初保持强劲活力
items.title_en: Premium Domain Market Remains Robust in Early 2026 with High-Value Transactions
items.summary: Le marché des noms de domaine premium affiche une belle santé en ce début d'année 2026.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026 年初欧洲优质域名市场表现强劲，两笔重大交易印证其吸引力：Midnight.com 以 115 万美元成交，C4.com 售价 26.5 万美元。文章指出，简短、易记及描述性域名被视为数字经济中的战略投资资产，凸显了企业在数字品牌布局中对高价值域名的持续需求与认可。
items.ai_summary_en: The European premium domain name market demonstrated strong vitality at the beginning of 2026. Two significant transactions highlight the enduring appeal of these digital assets: Midnight.com was acquired for 1.15 million dollars, while C4.com was negotiated at 265,000 dollars in early January. These substantial figures underscore the lasting value the market assigns to short, memorable, evocative, or descriptive domain names. Such domains are increasingly regarded as strategic investments within the digital economy. The activity reflects sustained demand and recognition from companies regarding high-value domains in their digital brand layouts. This trend indicates that organizations continue to prioritize securing premium digital real estate to enhance brand visibility and consumer trust in competitive online markets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应重视高端域名储备，将其纳入海外品牌战略。面对高昂的二级市场价格，建议提前布局或考虑替代方案，避免因域名缺失阻碍欧洲市场拓展。
items.ai_insight_en: Companies should integrate premium domain acquisition into their global brand strategy planning core. Given the high costs associated with secondary market purchases, organizations are advised to establish early defensive registration mechanisms. This approach helps mitigate risks related to domain speculation and ensures availability of key identifiers. Proactive portfolio management prevents potential obstacles to market expansion caused by missing or unavailable domains. Rights holders must balance immediate branding needs with long-term asset protection strategies to maintain competitive advantage in the digital landscape.

items.title: Counterfeits In The Automotive Sector And Software Piracy In Poland: How Brand Protection Works In Practice
items.title_zh: 波兰汽车假冒与软件盗版：品牌保护实务解析
items.title_en: Poland Automotive Counterfeits and Software Piracy: Practical Brand Protection Strategies
items.summary: The automotive market has long been among those most exposed to counterfeits, from spare parts to diagnostic devices. The issue rarely ends with a mere loss of sales.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析波兰汽车领域假冒零件及诊断设备软件盗版现状。指出该行为不仅导致销售损失，更引发安全风险。依据波兰海关数据，2023-2024 年查获大量假冒货物。文章详述波兰法律下，商标假冒适用工业产权法刑事处罚，软件盗版适用著作权法，且常构成加重情节。强调执法需应对证据易灭失挑战，揭示品牌保护在实务中的关键步骤。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the prevalence of counterfeit automotive parts and diagnostic software piracy in Poland. It highlights that these infringements cause not only revenue loss but also significant safety risks due to faulty components and incorrect diagnostic readings. Data from Poland’s National Revenue Administration reveals substantial seizures of counterfeit goods valued at hundreds of millions of PLN in 2023 and 2024, illustrating the scale of illicit trade. The article details the Polish legal framework, where trademark counterfeiting is penalized under Industrial Property Law and software piracy under Copyright Law. Aggravated circumstances often apply, leading to stricter criminal liability. Effective enforcement requires navigating challenges related to evidence preservation, emphasizing critical steps for rights holders to protect their brands through customs recordals and criminal proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国汽配及软件企业出口波兰时，须警惕供应链中混入假冒或盗版组件风险。建议建立严格合规审查机制，避免连带刑事责任。同时应主动利用波兰海关备案与刑事打击机制，维护自身品牌权益，防范本地化侵权冲击。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in or exporting to Poland must implement rigorous supply chain compliance checks to prevent the inclusion of counterfeit or pirated components, thereby avoiding potential joint criminal liability. Rights holders should actively utilize Poland’s customs recordal system and cooperate with law enforcement to initiate criminal actions against infringers. This proactive approach is essential for mitigating local infringement impacts and safeguarding brand integrity. Given the high volume of seizures, leveraging administrative and criminal enforcement mechanisms provides a robust strategy for effective brand protection in the automotive sector.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["汽车假冒件与盗版软件捆绑销售，构成严重安全与法律风险", "波兰海关近年查获数亿欧元假冒货物，显示侵权规模巨大", "商标假冒与软件盗版在波兰分别面临工业产权法与著作权法刑责"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Bundled sales of counterfeit auto parts and pirated diagnostic software create severe safety hazards and legal liabilities for market participants.", "Polish Customs recorded detections of counterfeit goods worth hundreds of millions of PLN recently, demonstrating the massive scale of infringement.", "Trademark counterfeiting faces criminal penalties under Industrial Property Law, while software piracy is prosecuted under Copyright Law."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业需严查供应链，防止假冒组件引发刑事连带责任", "善用波兰海关与刑事执法机制，主动打击本地侵权保护品牌"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies must enforce strict supply chain audits to prevent counterfeit components from causing joint criminal liability.", "Practice note: Leverage Polish customs recordals and criminal enforcement mechanisms to actively combat local trademark and copyright infringement.", "What to monitor: Track seizure data and enforcement trends to adapt brand protection strategies against bundled hardware-software piracy."]
core_points_en: Bundled sales of counterfeit auto parts and pirated diagnostic software create severe safety hazards and legal liabilities for market participants.
Polish Customs recorded detections of counterfeit goods worth hundreds of millions of PLN recently, demonstrating the massive scale of infringement.
Trademark counterfeiting faces criminal penalties under Industrial Property Law, while software piracy is prosecuted under Copyright Law.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies must enforce strict supply chain audits to prevent counterfeit components from causing joint criminal liability.
Practice note: Leverage Polish customs recordals and criminal enforcement mechanisms to actively combat local trademark and copyright infringement.
What to monitor: Track seizure data and enforcement trends to adapt brand protection strategies against bundled hardware-software piracy.

items.title: [Blog] L'IP Box en Europe, une stratégie fiscale d'optimisation et de valorisation des actifs de PI
items.title_zh: 欧洲 IP Box：优化与增值知识产权资产的税务策略
items.title_en: European IP Box Regimes: OECD Nexus Compliance and Tax Optimization Strategies
items.summary: L'IP Box s'impose comme un levier fiscal central pour la valorisation des actifs de Propriété Intellectuelle (PI) en Europe, dans un cadre désormais harmonisé par l'approche...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析欧洲 IP Box 制度如何在 OECD“关联法”框架下成为知识产权资产估值的核心税务工具。法国实行 10% 优惠税率，要求研发支出可追溯；卢森堡提供高达 80% 豁免，有效税率约 5%。欧盟多国如爱尔兰、比利时等均采纳类似机制，旨在通过税收优惠绑定本土研发活动，既支持创新又限制激进避税。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines how European Patent Box regimes function as central fiscal tools for valuing intellectual property assets under the harmonized OECD 'nexus' approach. France offers a reduced 10% tax rate on net IP income from patents and software, requiring precise R&D expenditure tracking aligned with OECD standards. Luxembourg provides a highly attractive regime with an 80% exemption on net income, resulting in an effective tax rate of approximately 5%. Several other EU member states, including Ireland, Belgium, and the Netherlands, have adopted comparable mechanisms with effective rates ranging from 2.5% to 10%. These systems aim to link tax benefits directly to substantive domestic R&D activities, thereby supporting local innovation while strictly limiting aggressive tax optimization strategies through mandatory nexus ratio calculations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企应评估现有 IP 架构是否符合关联法要求，利用法、卢等国差异优化税负。建议建立研发费用专项台账，确保享受优惠同时规避合规风险，提升无形资产回报率。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating within the European Union should evaluate whether their current intellectual property architectures satisfy strict OECD nexus requirements. Leveraging jurisdictional differences between France, Luxembourg, and other member states can optimize overall tax burdens while maintaining compliance. Companies are advised to establish dedicated ledgers for R&D expenses to ensure accurate calculation of nexus ratios. This approach secures eligibility for preferential tax treatments and mitigates regulatory risks, ultimately enhancing the return on investment for intangible assets across cross-border operations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["OECD 关联法原则已 harmonize 欧洲各国 IP Box 制度，强调研发实质…", "法国 IP Box 对专利等净收入征收 10% 税率，需配合研发支出精细追踪", "卢森堡等国税负更低，吸引国际高价值 IP 持有结构，但均受关联比例约束"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["OECD nexus principles have harmonized European IP Box regimes, emphasizing that tax benefits must correlate with substantial, verifiable R&D activities.", "France applies a 10% reduced tax rate on patent and software income, mandating rigorous traceability of qualifying R&D expenditures.", "Luxembourg and other jurisdictions offer lower effective rates but strictly enforce nexus ratios, attracting international high-value IP holding structures."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["核查研发支出归集逻辑，确保满足 OECD 关联比例以享税收优惠", "对比成员国税率差异，合理规划高价值专利持有地以降低整体税负"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify R&D cost allocation logic to meet OECD nexus ratios and secure available tax incentives.", "Practice note: Compare member state tax rates to strategically plan the holding location of high-value patents and reduce global tax liability.", "What to monitor: Evolving interpretations of nexus calculations and compliance obligations across key European IP jurisdictions."]
core_points_en: OECD nexus principles have harmonized European IP Box regimes, emphasizing that tax benefits must correlate with substantial, verifiable R&D activities.
France applies a 10% reduced tax rate on patent and software income, mandating rigorous traceability of qualifying R&D expenditures.
Luxembourg and other jurisdictions offer lower effective rates but strictly enforce nexus ratios, attracting international high-value IP holding structures.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify R&D cost allocation logic to meet OECD nexus ratios and secure available tax incentives.
Practice note: Compare member state tax rates to strategically plan the holding location of high-value patents and reduce global tax liability.
What to monitor: Evolving interpretations of nexus calculations and compliance obligations across key European IP jurisdictions.
