items.title: University IP and the role of AI in technology transfer
items.title_zh: 大学知识产权与 AI 在技术转移中的角色
items.title_en: Leveraging AI to Bridge the Gap in University Technology Transfer and IP Commercialization
items.summary: New methods of using artificial intelligence (AI) are helping to get inventions from the research lab to the marketplace more efficiently.
items.ai_summary_zh: 针对高校专利授权率低的问题，文章分析 AI 如何赋能技术转移办公室（TTO）。AI 能高效处理专利检索与现有技术映射，缩短分析周期，帮助识别跨学科机会。尽管 AI 无法替代人类判断商业时机，但其提供的结构化数据能加速从发现到市场的转化，提升研发投资回报率。
items.ai_summary_en: Addressing the low licensing rates of university patents, this analysis examines how artificial intelligence empowers technology transfer offices (TTOs). AI significantly enhances efficiency in patent searches and prior art mapping, drastically reducing analysis cycles. This acceleration helps identify cross-disciplinary opportunities that might otherwise remain hidden. Although AI cannot replace human judgment regarding commercial timing and strategic nuance, it provides structured data insights. These insights accelerate the transformation from scientific discovery to market application, ultimately improving the return on investment for research and development activities within academic institutions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中资企业或高校可借鉴此模式，利用 AI 工具优化专利组合管理。建议关注欧洲高校高价值未授权专利，借助 AI 精准匹配技术需求，降低许可谈判成本，挖掘潜在合作机会。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants can adopt this model to optimize patent portfolio management using AI tools. It is advisable to monitor high-value unlicensed patents from European universities. By leveraging AI for precise matching of technical needs, companies can reduce licensing negotiation costs and uncover potential collaboration opportunities. This approach facilitates efficient technology acquisition while maintaining rigorous human oversight for final decision-making.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AI 大幅缩短专利检索与现有技术对比时间，释放人力聚焦战略", "AI 通过语义搜索揭示跨学科隐藏联系，辅助评估技术成熟度与市场潜力", "AI 提供数据支撑但无法替代人类对商业动机与时机的敏锐判断"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["AI drastically reduces time for patent searches and prior art comparisons, freeing personnel to focus on high-level strategy.", "Semantic search reveals hidden cross-disciplinary connections, assisting in evaluating technology readiness and market potential.", "AI provides essential data support but cannot replace human discernment regarding commercial motivations and optimal timing."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 AI 工具筛选欧洲高校未授权专利，寻找低成本技术引进机会", "结合 AI 数据分析与人工研判，提升跨国技术转移决策的精准度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Use AI tools to screen unlicensed European university patents for cost-effective technology introduction opportunities.", "Practice note: Combine AI data analytics with human judgment to enhance the precision of cross-border technology transfer decisions.", "What to monitor: Emerging AI-driven platforms that map interdisciplinary patent overlaps to identify undervalued academic IP assets."]
core_points_en: AI drastically reduces time for patent searches and prior art comparisons, freeing personnel to focus on high-level strategy.
Semantic search reveals hidden cross-disciplinary connections, assisting in evaluating technology readiness and market potential.
AI provides essential data support but cannot replace human discernment regarding commercial motivations and optimal timing.

Practice note: Combine AI data analytics with human judgment to enhance the precision of cross-border technology transfer decisions.
What to monitor: Emerging AI-driven platforms that map interdisciplinary patent overlaps to identify undervalued academic IP assets.

items.title: Preuves d’usage de votre marque et sous-catégories autonomes : les bons réflexes pour 2026 !
items.title_zh: 欧盟商标使用证据与独立子类别：2026年合规指南
items.title_en: EU Trademark Use Evidence and Autonomous Sub-Classes: Compliance Guide for 2026
items.summary: En ce début d’année, l’heure est aux bonnes résolutions.  Collecter et conserver des preuves d’usage de votre marque est justement une bonne pratique à adopter, surtout si l’enregistrement de cette dernière approche de son cinquième anniversaire. En effet, sauf justes motifs, tout titulaire de marque doit être en mesure de démontrer l’usage sérieux de (suite) L’article Preuves d’usage de votre marque et sous-catégories autonomes : les bons réflexes pour 2026 ! est apparu en premier sur Lavoix -
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出，商标注册满五年后，持有人需证明真实使用以维持权利。重点强调在收集使用证据时，应关注商品或服务“独立子类别”的界定。若仅在广义类别下使用，可能无法覆盖所有注册项目。建议企业建立系统性证据留存机制，确保在面临撤销风险时能有效举证，避免因证据不足导致部分或全部商标权丧失。
items.ai_summary_en: This article highlights the critical importance of collecting and preserving evidence of genuine trademark use, particularly as registrations approach their fifth anniversary. Under EU law, rights holders must demonstrate serious use to maintain protection, absent justified reasons for non-use. The analysis emphasizes that evidence must specifically address autonomous sub-categories of goods or services. Relying solely on broad category descriptions is often insufficient to cover all registered items. Consequently, market participants are advised to establish systematic mechanisms for retaining proof of use. This proactive approach ensures robust evidentiary support in cancellation proceedings, preventing partial or total loss of trademark rights due to inadequate documentation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局商标时，常忽视“子类别”使用的证据留存。建议在注册后第五年前，系统整理针对不同细分市场的销售合同、发票及宣传材料。确保每类注册商品均有独立的使用证据，以防因证据笼统导致在撤销程序中败诉，保障品牌资产安全。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating in the EU frequently overlook the necessity of documenting use within specific autonomous sub-categories. To mitigate cancellation risks, it is prudent to systematically organize sales contracts, invoices, and promotional materials tailored to distinct market segments before the five-year deadline. Ensuring that each registered good or service has independent supporting evidence prevents vulnerabilities during revocation actions. This structured approach safeguards brand assets by addressing the EUIPO's strict requirements for proving genuine use across all protected specifications, thereby avoiding potential losses due to generic or insufficient proof.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["商标注册满五年需证明真实使用，否则面临被撤销风险", "证据需针对独立子类别，广义类别使用不足以覆盖全部注册项目", "建议建立系统化证据留存机制，确保证据链完整有效"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Registrations reaching five years require proof of genuine use to avoid cancellation risks.", "Evidence must target autonomous sub-categories, as broad category use may not suffice.", "Systematic evidence retention mechanisms ensure complete chains of proof for defense."]

items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Secure trademark rights by proving use in specific sub-categories rather than relying on broad classifications.", "Practice note: Implement a systematic evidence retention strategy before the five-year mark to ensure comprehensive coverage.", "What to monitor: Track registration anniversaries and prepare targeted documentation for each autonomous sub-category."]

items.title: Latest developments in EU green trademarks (Part II)
items.title_zh: 欧盟绿色商标最新进展：描述性与认证商标
items.title_en: EU Green Trademarks: Descriptiveness Risks and the Potential of Certification Marks
items.summary: In Part I (here) we shared thoughts about the phenomenon of green branding in the EU as well as the consolidation of the interpretation by the General Court (GC) of deceptiveness to combat green washing. In this second part, we look into other grounds for refusal or invalidity of green claims in trademarks, namely, descriptiveness and lack of distinctiveness, and at the big potential of EU certification marks.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文延续前篇，深入探讨欧盟绿色商标因缺乏显著性或具描述性而被驳回的风险。普通法院强调公众将环保主张视为产品质量指示而非来源标识。尽管欧盟知识产权局实践存在不一致，但已注册标志仍面临无效宣告风险。文章最后指出欧盟认证商标在推动可持续经济方面的巨大潜力。
items.ai_summary_en: This article continues the analysis of green branding in the EU, focusing on grounds for refusal or invalidity based on descriptiveness and lack of distinctiveness. The General Court has emphasized that consumers often perceive environmental claims as indications of product quality rather than source identifiers. Consequently, purely descriptive green signs cannot be protected as trademarks. While the EUIPO’s practice shows some inconsistency regarding green slogans, registered marks remain vulnerable to invalidation actions if deemed descriptive. The piece also highlights the significant potential of EU certification marks as a strategic tool for promoting sustainable economic practices and securing robust brand protection.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请绿色商标时，应避免使用直接描述环保特征的词汇，以防因缺乏显著性被驳回。建议关注并利用欧盟认证商标体系，通过合规认证提升品牌公信力，规避后续无效宣告及不正当竞争诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should avoid using terms that directly describe environmental characteristics, as these are likely to be rejected for lacking distinctiveness. Instead, rights holders should consider leveraging the EU certification mark system to build credible green brands through verified compliance. This approach helps mitigate risks associated with subsequent invalidation proceedings and unfair competition litigation. By aligning with established certification standards, companies can create a more defensible intellectual property position while demonstrating genuine commitment to sustainability goals.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["普通法院认定纯描述性绿色标志因缺乏显著性无法获准注册", "欧盟知识产权局对绿色标语的审查标准尚存不一致现象", "欧盟认证商标被视为未来促进环保与可持续发展的关键工具"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The General Court confirms that purely descriptive green signs lack distinctive character and cannot be registered as EU trademarks.", "EUIPO examination practice regarding green slogans remains inconsistent, creating uncertainty for applicants seeking registration.", "EU certification marks are identified as a key future instrument for promoting environmental sustainability and compliance."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免直接使用描述性绿色词汇，以防商标因缺乏显著性被拒", "积极布局欧盟认证商标，以合规方式构建绿色品牌护城河"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid direct descriptive green vocabulary to prevent trademark rejection due to lack of distinctiveness.", "Practice note: Actively utilize EU certification marks to construct a compliant green brand strategy and enhance credibility.", "What to monitor: Monitor EUIPO case law trends regarding the distinction between descriptive claims and protectable green slogans."]

EUIPO examination practice regarding green slogans remains inconsistent, creating uncertainty for applicants seeking registration.
EU certification marks are identified as a key future instrument for promoting environmental sustainability and compliance.

What to monitor: Monitor EUIPO case law trends regarding the distinction between descriptive claims and protectable green slogans.

items.title: Getty v Stability AI: Infringing Copies and gen-AI Training - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: Getty 诉 Stability AI：侵权复制与生成式 AI 训练分析
items.title_en: Getty v Stability AI: Analyzing Infringing Copies and Generative AI Training Copyright Issues
items.summary: The Getty Images v Stability AI [1] case has been highly anticipated for several years. In this article we examine the AI-driven copyright issues that arose during the case; analyse Mrs Justice Smith’s decision (including what, in our view, the court got right, and perhaps didn’t); and consider the future of copyright protection in a […]
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文深入剖析备受关注的 Getty Images 诉 Stability AI 案，聚焦生成式 AI 训练中的版权争议。文章详细解读了史密斯法官的判决，评估法院在认定侵权复制品及模型权重性质上的得失，并探讨在 AI 模型大规模吞噬训练数据背景下，未来版权保护的走向及原创性认定的挑战。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the high-profile Getty Images v Stability AI litigation, focusing on critical copyright disputes surrounding generative AI training. It provides a detailed evaluation of Mrs Justice Smith’s judgment, assessing the court’s reasoning regarding the classification of infringing copies and the legal nature of model weights. The article highlights both the strengths and potential weaknesses in the judicial approach to these novel technical issues. Furthermore, it explores the broader implications for future copyright protection frameworks as AI models increasingly rely on massive datasets for learning. The discussion addresses fundamental questions about originality and authorship in an era dominated by sophisticated algorithms, offering insights into how current legal standards may evolve to address the challenges posed by large-scale data consumption and automated content generation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业及 AI 开发者需警惕欧盟及英国在 AI 训练数据合规上的严监管趋势。建议提前审查训练数据来源合法性，建立数据清洗机制，避免陷入类似侵权诉讼，同时关注欧美判例对全球版权规则的塑造作用。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants deploying generative AI must navigate increasing regulatory scrutiny regarding training data compliance in the UK and EU. Rights holders should proactively audit data sources to ensure legality and implement robust data cleansing mechanisms to mitigate infringement risks. As Western jurisdictions shape global copyright norms through landmark cases like Getty v Stability AI, companies operating internationally must align their R&D and data strategies with evolving judicial precedents. Failure to establish clear provenance for training datasets may expose organizations to cross-border litigation and significant liability under emerging intellectual property frameworks.

items.title: Alright, alright, alright: Could McConaughey’s sound trademark silence AI?
items.title_zh: 麦康纳声音商标能否遏制 AI 滥用：欧盟法律视角的分析
items.title_en: McConaughey’s Sound Trademark: A Potential Shield Against AI Voice Impersonation in the EU
items.summary: His sound trademark protecting his iconic phrase “Alright, alright, alright” offers Matthew McConaughey a new route to defend his trademark voice from AI.
items.ai_summary_zh: 演员麦康纳注册标志性短语的声音商标，试图以此对抗 AI 语音合成侵权。文章指出，虽然美国已获准注册，但在欧盟现行框架下，肖像权不涵盖声音，版权因缺乏独创性亦难适用。尽管《人工智能法案》等提供部分保护，但传统知识产权在应对深度伪造时仍显不足，荷兰与丹麦正探索立法补救。
items.ai_summary_en: Actor Matthew McConaughey secured US sound trademark registration for his iconic phrase “Alright, alright, alright,” protecting specific intonation and tone. This strategy aims to combat unauthorized AI voice synthesis and deepfakes. However, under current EU frameworks, portrait rights exclude voices, and copyright protection is often unattainable due to insufficient originality in short utterances. While the EU AI Act offers some regulatory safeguards, traditional intellectual property laws remain inadequate against sophisticated voice cloning. Consequently, Nordic countries like the Netherlands and Denmark are exploring legislative reforms to grant individuals quasi-copyright protections over their voices and physical characteristics, highlighting a significant gap in existing European legal defenses.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应警惕 AI 语音侵权风险，单纯依赖传统商标可能不足。建议结合欧盟《人工智能法案》合规要求，探索数据权益保护，并关注荷丹等国立法动态，构建多维度的声音品牌防御体系。
items.ai_insight_en: Reliance on traditional trademark registrations alone may prove insufficient for defending against AI-driven voice impersonation within the EU. Rights holders should adopt a multi-layered defense strategy that integrates compliance with the EU AI Act and explores data rights protections. It is critical to assess the feasibility of sound trademarks while simultaneously establishing barriers through unfair competition laws and data privacy regulations. Given the evolving legal landscape, particularly in Nordic jurisdictions, market participants must remain agile. Proactive monitoring of legislative developments regarding the intellectual property status of human biometric features will be essential for constructing robust, future-proof brand and personality protection frameworks.

EU portrait rights do not cover voices, and copyright law generally fails to protect short spoken phrases lacking sufficient creative originality.
Nordic nations are advancing legislation to provide personal voice and bodily feature protections similar to copyright against AI misuse.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate sound trademark viability alongside unfair competition and data compliance strategies to mitigate AI voice infringement risks.
Practice note: Develop multi-dimensional defense systems combining IP rights with regulatory compliance under the EU AI Act framework.
What to monitor: Legislative trends in Nordic countries regarding quasi-copyright protections for personal voice and bodily characteristics.

items.title: Parallel imports and trademark exhaustion: How to protect your rights and limit your risks
items.title_zh: 平行进口与商标权用尽：如何保护权利并限制风险
items.title_en: Parallel Imports and Trademark Exhaustion: Protecting Rights and Limiting Risks
items.summary: Discover the importance of vigilance around parallel imports, including the risks that parallel trade will lead to exhaustion of rights in trademark portfolios.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析平行进口对品牌控制力的冲击，阐释欧洲经济区内商标权用尽原则的法律基础。指出虽原则上无法阻止区内转售，但若产品状态改变或损害声誉可例外反对。重点建议企业利用海关备案机制，拦截来自区外的未授权平行进口货物，以维护分销策略。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the impact of parallel imports on brand control, clarifying the legal basis for trademark exhaustion within the European Economic Area. While rights holders generally cannot oppose the resale of goods first marketed in the EEA with consent, exceptions exist if product condition is altered or reputation is damaged. The text highlights the strategic use of customs recordals to intercept unauthorized parallel imports originating from outside the EEA. By leveraging Applications for Action, rights holders can effectively safeguard their distribution strategies and maintain brand integrity against unapproved cross-border trade flows.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业需区分欧盟内外货源，针对区外进口积极申请海关备案。同时严格监控产品在欧流通状态，若发现重新包装或品质受损，应即时主张权利例外，防止低价平行进口冲击价格体系与品牌形象。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants must distinguish between goods sourced inside and outside the European Economic Area to manage exhaustion risks effectively. For products originating outside the EEA, proactive customs registration via Applications for Action is essential to prevent unauthorized entry. Simultaneously, continuous monitoring of product circulation within Europe is critical. If repackaging or quality degradation occurs, rights holders should promptly assert exceptions to exhaustion rules. This approach helps protect pricing structures and brand image from the disruptive effects of low-price parallel imports.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟境内商标权一旦经同意投放市场即告用尽，原则上不可阻止转售", "若产品状况被改变、包装受损或声誉受毁，权利人可反对平行进口", "通过海关行动申请备案，可有效拦截源自欧洲经济区外的未授权货物"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Trademark rights are exhausted upon first marketing in the EEA with consent, preventing opposition to further resale within the region.", "Rights holders may oppose parallel imports if product condition is altered, packaging is damaged, or the trademark's reputation suffers harm.", "Customs Applications for Action provide an effective mechanism to block unauthorized parallel imports entering the EEA from non-member countries."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["针对非欧盟来源货物，务必提前向海关提交行动申请以阻断进口", "监控产品在欧流转状态，遇改包装或损声誉情形及时行使反对权"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Submit Customs Applications for Action early to block unauthorized imports of goods originating outside the European Economic Area.", "Practice note: Monitor product circulation within Europe and promptly exercise opposition rights when repackaging or reputational damage occurs.", "What to monitor: Track changes in product condition and packaging integrity to identify valid grounds for opposing parallel trade activities."]
core_points_en: Trademark rights are exhausted upon first marketing in the EEA with consent, preventing opposition to further resale within the region.
Rights holders may oppose parallel imports if product condition is altered, packaging is damaged, or the trademark's reputation suffers harm.
Customs Applications for Action provide an effective mechanism to block unauthorized parallel imports entering the EEA from non-member countries.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Submit Customs Applications for Action early to block unauthorized imports of goods originating outside the European Economic Area.
Practice note: Monitor product circulation within Europe and promptly exercise opposition rights when repackaging or reputational damage occurs.
What to monitor: Track changes in product condition and packaging integrity to identify valid grounds for opposing parallel trade activities.

items.title: BSH before the UPC and the German courts – first applications, an emerging tendency and open guiding questions [Update on BSH case law]
items.title_zh: BSH 案在 UPC 与德国法院的首批应用及趋势分析
items.title_en: BSH Case Law: Early UPC and German Court Applications, Emerging Trends, and Open Questions
items.summary: Update on the article “BSH as a Gateway to Cross-Border Patent Enforcement: The Regeneron/Bayer vs Formycon and Onesta vs BMW Cases” from December 1, 2025 The CJEU’s decision in BSH v Electrolux (C-339/22) has by now been expressly taken up... Der Beitrag BSH before the UPC and the German courts – first applications, an emerging tendency and open guiding questions [Update on BSH case law] erschien zuerst auf BOEHMERT & BOEHMERT .
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文更新了对 BSH 诉 Electrolux 案（C-339/22）的后续观察，重点分析欧盟法院该裁决如何被统一专利法院（UPC）及德国法院采纳。文章梳理了首批相关申请案例，指出跨境专利执法中出现的司法倾向，并探讨了当前尚未明确的关键指导性问题，为跨国专利诉讼策略提供实务参考。
items.ai_summary_en: This update examines the practical impact of the CJEU’s BSH v Electrolux ruling (C-339/22) on subsequent patent litigation within the Unified Patent Court (UPC) and German national courts. The analysis details how these judicial bodies have expressly adopted the CJEU’s reasoning in their initial decisions. It identifies emerging tendencies in cross-border patent enforcement, highlighting specific early-stage applications that signal a shift in judicial approach. Furthermore, the article addresses several critical guiding questions that remain unresolved, creating legal uncertainty for rights holders and defendants alike. By mapping these developments, the piece provides essential context for understanding the current landscape of European patent enforcement, offering practitioners a clearer view of how precedent is being applied and where strategic gaps persist in the evolving jurisprudence.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更统一的专利执法环境，需关注 UPC 对 CJEU 判例的引用逻辑。建议提前评估核心产品在欧洲的侵权风险，利用判例趋势优化诉讼地选择，并针对未决法律问题制定预案以应对潜在跨境禁令。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and market participants should closely monitor how the UPC interprets and applies CJEU precedents to anticipate outcomes in cross-border disputes. Given the evolving judicial landscape, it is prudent to assess infringement risks for core products in Europe proactively. Companies should leverage observed trends to optimize venue selection for litigation strategies. Additionally, preparing contingency plans for potential cross-border injunctions is advisable, particularly concerning pending legal issues where guidance is still developing. This proactive approach helps mitigate risks associated with the increasingly unified yet complex European patent enforcement environment.

items.title: UK IPO: Combination authorisation blocks SPC for later single-ingredient product - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: 英知产局：组合授权阻碍后续单药产品 SPC 申请
items.title_en: UK IPO: Prior Combination Authorisation Bars SPC for Subsequent Single-Ingredient Product
items.summary: Most therapeutics are first developed and approved as monotherapies, with their use in combination with other actives generally introduced only at a later stage, if at all. The SPC implications of this regulatory sequence have been the subject of extensive case law, most recently addressed by the CJEU in joined cases C‑119/22 and C‑149/22 Teva […]
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局在决定 BL O/0705/25 中驳回了一项基于后期单药授权的 SPC 申请。涉案活性成分此前已获固定剂量组合授权。局方认定，根据 SPC 条例第 3(d) 条，早期的组合授权构成该活性成分的首次授权，从而阻断了基于后期单药授权获得 SPC 的可能性。此裁决延续了 CJEU 判例法精神，对从组合疗法转向单药疗法的专利策略产生重大影响。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office rejected a Supplementary Protection Certificate application in decision BL O/0705/25, ruling that an earlier fixed-dose combination authorisation constituted the first marketing authorisation under Article 3(d) of Regulation (EC) No 469/2009. The active ingredient had previously been approved within combination products before receiving standalone approval. Consequently, the later single-ingredient authorisation could not support the SPC claim. This decision aligns with established CJEU case law regarding regulatory sequences and significantly impacts patent strategies where therapy evolves from combination to monotherapy. It confirms that initial combination approvals effectively block subsequent SPC eligibility for individual components or narrower combinations containing those same ingredients, reinforcing strict interpretation of first authorisation requirements.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企在欧洲布局 SPC 时，若产品先以复方形式上市，后续开发单方制剂将无法获得 SPC 保护。建议在研发早期统筹规划上市顺序，优先争取单药授权以锁定 SPC 资格，避免因地域性审查标准差异导致核心市场保护期落空。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders managing European portfolios, this decision highlights critical risks in sequencing product launches. If a therapeutic is initially authorised as part of a fixed-dose combination, subsequent standalone approvals for individual components will likely fail to secure SPC protection. Applicants should strategically plan global marketing authorisation timelines during early R&D phases. Prioritizing single-ingredient approvals where commercially viable may preserve SPC eligibility. Failure to coordinate these regulatory steps can result in lost exclusivity periods in key markets, particularly where local offices strictly apply the first authorisation rule against later, narrower product claims.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["早期固定剂量组合授权被视为活性成分的首次上市许可", "后续单药授权无法支持针对该活性成分的 SPC 申请", "该原则适用于含相同活性成分的任何后续组合或单药情形"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Early fixed-dose combination authorisations are deemed the first marketing authorisation for the contained active ingredients under SPC regulations.", "Subsequent single-ingredient authorisations cannot support SPC applications for active ingredients already covered by prior combination approvals.", "This principle applies broadly to any subsequent combination or monotherapy scenarios involving the same active ingredient previously authorised."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["复方先于单药上市将导致单药 SPC 申请被驳回", "需在前端研发阶段优化全球上市许可申报策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Early combination launch blocks later SPC for single ingredients; strategic sequencing is vital.", "Practice note: Prioritize standalone authorizations early to secure SPC eligibility for core assets.", "What to monitor: UK IPO adherence to this precedent in non-contraceptive fixed-dose combination cases."]
core_points_en: Early fixed-dose combination authorisations are deemed the first marketing authorisation for the contained active ingredients under SPC regulations.
Subsequent single-ingredient authorisations cannot support SPC applications for active ingredients already covered by prior combination approvals.
This principle applies broadly to any subsequent combination or monotherapy scenarios involving the same active ingredient previously authorised.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Early combination launch blocks later SPC for single ingredients; strategic sequencing is vital.
Practice note: Prioritize standalone authorizations early to secure SPC eligibility for core assets.
What to monitor: UK IPO adherence to this precedent in non-contraceptive fixed-dose combination cases.

items.title: The Dracula paradox: temporal limitations in EU trademark law
items.title_zh: 德古拉悖论：欧盟商标法中的时间限制难题
items.title_en: The Dracula Paradox: CJEU Limits National Tolerance Rules in EU Trademark Law
items.summary: The CJEU's recent judgment in Lunapark (C-452/24) has sent shockwaves through EU trademark law by holding that national courts cannot limit trademark rights for reasons of tolerance beyond the concept of acquiescence provided in Articles 18(1) and 9(1)-(2) of Directive 2015/2436.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在 Lunapark 案中裁定，成员国法院不得以“容忍”为由限制商标权，除非符合指令规定的默许条款。该案推翻了芬兰法院基于合理时间的程序性抗辩，强调完全和谐化原则下，未注册标志的使用不能通过国内法原则获得保护，引发法律确定性与公平结果的争议。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union’s recent judgment in Lunapark (C-452/24) significantly impacts EU trademark law by ruling that national courts cannot restrict trademark rights based on tolerance beyond the specific acquiescence provisions in Directive 2015/2436. The case involved a dispute where Lunapark held the registered 'DRACULA' mark, while Hardeco’s predecessor had used the sign for years without exclusive rights. Despite Lunapark’s delay in initiating proceedings, the CJEU rejected the Finnish court’s application of a 'reasonable time' requirement. The Court emphasized that Article 18(1) fully harmonizes conditions for limiting rights due to inactivity, excluding unregistered signs from protection via general national principles. This decision ensures uniform application across the EU but raises concerns regarding legal certainty and the distinction between substantive limitations and procedural enforcement rules.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业需警惕：即使对手长期未维权，其注册商标仍可随时发起禁令诉讼。建议企业尽早排查自有品牌注册状态，避免依赖“默认许可”抗辩；对长期使用但未注册的标识，应加速注册或寻求共存协议以规避风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should recognize that long-standing non-enforcement by a rights holder does not automatically create a defense against future infringement claims. Reliance on implied consent or national limitation periods may be insufficient under the current harmonized framework. Companies must proactively secure registration for key brands rather than depending on prior use alone. For businesses utilizing unregistered signs, accelerating registration processes or negotiating coexistence agreements is critical to mitigate litigation risks. Furthermore, when facing delayed enforcement actions, defendants should prepare robust evidence challenging the substantive basis of infringement, as procedural time-bar defenses face heightened scrutiny following this ruling.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院禁止成员国以一般法律原则超越指令规定的默许规则限制商标权", "未注册标志的长期使用不构成对抗注册商标专用权的法定免责事由", "判决虽维护体系统一，但被指忽视程序自主及第三方长期使用的合理期待"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Member States are prohibited from using general legal principles to exceed Directive limits on restricting trademark rights through tolerance.", "Long-term use of an unregistered sign does not constitute a statutory defense against the exclusive rights of a registered EU trademark.", "The judgment preserves systemic unity but is criticized for overlooking procedural autonomy and third parties' legitimate expectations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["长期使用未注册商标无法对抗欧盟注册商标，必须尽快完成确权布局", "面对权利人迟延起诉，不可仅依国内时效抗辩，需准备实质侵权不成立证据"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Long-term non-enforcement by a rights holder does not guarantee immunity from future injunctions or damages under EU law.", "Practice note: Secure early trademark registration for core assets; do not rely on implied consent or national tolerance doctrines as primary defenses.", "What to monitor: How national courts apply equivalence and effectiveness principles to procedural time limits in light of full harmonization mandates."]
core_points_en: Member States are prohibited from using general legal principles to exceed Directive limits on restricting trademark rights through tolerance.
Long-term use of an unregistered sign does not constitute a statutory defense against the exclusive rights of a registered EU trademark.
The judgment preserves systemic unity but is criticized for overlooking procedural autonomy and third parties' legitimate expectations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Long-term non-enforcement by a rights holder does not guarantee immunity from future injunctions or damages under EU law.
Practice note: Secure early trademark registration for core assets; do not rely on implied consent or national tolerance doctrines as primary defenses.
What to monitor: How national courts apply equivalence and effectiveness principles to procedural time limits in light of full harmonization mandates.

items.title: Neurim in the mirror - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: 镜像中的 Neurim：德国法院就 SPC 首份授权定义提请欧盟法院裁决
items.title_en: German Court Refers Neurim Question to CJEU on SPC First Authorisation Definition
items.summary: There has been a new reference made to the CJEU on the correct interpretation of Article 3(d) of the SPC Regulation (Regulation (EC) No 469/2009), this time from the Bundespatentgericht (BPatG), the German Federal Patent Court. The reference concerns what counts as a “first authorisation” to place a product on the market. The CJEU has […]
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国联邦专利法院就补充保护证书（SPC）条例第 3(d) 条中“首份上市许可”的定义向欧盟法院提起新预审。案件涉及勃林格殷格翰的兽药产品，虽为该活性成分首个兽药许可，但该成分此前已获人用药品许可。德国专利局依据 Santen 案判例驳回申请，认为应基于活性成分而非治疗用途判断，此案旨在厘清人兽用药许可交叉时的 SPC 资格争议。
items.ai_summary_en: The German Federal Patent Court has referred a new question to the CJEU regarding the interpretation of Article 3(d) of the SPC Regulation. The case concerns whether a prior human marketing authorisation precludes a Supplementary Protection Certificate for a veterinary product containing the same active ingredient. Boehringer Ingelheim sought an SPC for Aservo Equihaler, the first veterinary authorisation for ciclesonide. However, the German Patent Office rejected the application, citing the CJEU’s Santen decision. The DPMA argued that Santen overruled the earlier Neurim ruling, requiring assessment based on the active ingredient rather than therapeutic use. This reference seeks clarification on whether strict regulatory distinctions between human and veterinary sectors should apply when determining the ‘first authorisation’ for SPC eligibility.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企在欧布局兽药或人药专利时，需警惕活性成分跨领域使用导致的 SPC 申请风险。建议密切关注本案裁决，若欧盟法院维持严格标准，应调整欧洲市场准入与专利延期组合策略，避免因 prior art 许可丧失独占期。
items.ai_insight_en: This referral highlights significant uncertainty in European SPC practice regarding cross-sector authorisations. Rights holders developing veterinary or human medicines with known active ingredients face potential barriers to obtaining patent term extensions if prior authorisations in other therapeutic fields are deemed relevant. The outcome will determine whether the Santen strict standard applies universally or if exceptions remain for distinct regulatory pathways. Stakeholders should assess their portfolios for similar conflicts where prior human or veterinary approvals might invalidate subsequent SPC applications, potentially impacting market exclusivity strategies across different medical sectors.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国法院提请欧盟法院解释 SPC 条例中跨物种许可是否构成首份授权", "勃林格殷格翰主张兽药与人药许可应严格区分以符合 SPC 立法目的", "欧盟法院需在 Santen 案严格立场与 Neurim 案灵活立场间做出最终澄清"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["German court asks CJEU to clarify if prior human drug authorisation blocks SPCs for veterinary products with identical active ingredients.", "Boehringer argues for strict regulatory separation between veterinary and human medicinal product authorisations to satisfy SPC requirements.", "CJEU must resolve tension between Santen's strict active ingredient approach and Neurim's flexible therapeutic use interpretation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["跨物种活性成分许可可能阻碍 SPC 获取，需提前评估欧洲合规路径", "关注 CJEU 最终裁决，动态调整在欧医药专利生命周期管理策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prior human or veterinary authorisations may block SPCs for new therapeutic uses of known active ingredients under current strict interpretations.", "Practice note: Evaluate regulatory history carefully to identify conflicting prior authorisations that could jeopardize SPC eligibility in Europe.", "What to monitor: Await CJEU ruling to determine if distinct human and veterinary regulatory frameworks preserve SPC rights despite shared active ingredients."]
core_points_en: German court asks CJEU to clarify if prior human drug authorisation blocks SPCs for veterinary products with identical active ingredients.
Boehringer argues for strict regulatory separation between veterinary and human medicinal product authorisations to satisfy SPC requirements.
CJEU must resolve tension between Santen's strict active ingredient approach and Neurim's flexible therapeutic use interpretation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prior human or veterinary authorisations may block SPCs for new therapeutic uses of known active ingredients under current strict interpretations.
Practice note: Evaluate regulatory history carefully to identify conflicting prior authorisations that could jeopardize SPC eligibility in Europe.
What to monitor: Await CJEU ruling to determine if distinct human and veterinary regulatory frameworks preserve SPC rights despite shared active ingredients.

items.title: The UPC's LD Hamburg remains firm on late-filed submissions (UPC_CFI_461/2024; UPC_CFI_718/2024)
items.title_zh: UPC 汉堡分院坚持驳回逾期提交的无效攻击与证据
items.title_en: UPC Hamburg Rejects Late-Filed Invalidity Attacks and Evidence in Dolle v. FAKRO
items.summary: Court docket: LD
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 汉堡地方法院在 Dolle 诉 FAKRO 案中裁定，被告在提出撤销反诉后逾期提交的新事实、证据及无效理由不予采纳。法院强调 UPC 程序的前置加载特性，要求所有无效攻击必须随反诉一并提出，口头审理阶段原则上禁止引入新攻击，以确保对方防御权并加速诉讼进程。
items.ai_summary_en: In the case of Dolle A/S v. FAKRO Dachflächenfenster GmbH, the UPC Local Division in Hamburg ruled that new facts, evidence, and invalidity grounds submitted by the defendant after filing the counterclaim for revocation were inadmissible. The Court emphasized the front-loaded nature of UPC proceedings, requiring all validity challenges to be introduced simultaneously with the revocation counterclaim. Consequently, late-filed attacks presented during oral hearings were rejected to ensure the opposing party’s right to prepare an adequate defense and to maintain procedural efficiency. This decision underscores strict adherence to procedural timelines, distinguishing UPC practice from more flexible approaches seen elsewhere.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对 UPC 诉讼时，必须在答辩初期穷尽所有无效证据与理由，切勿依赖后续补充。需建立前置化诉讼策略，避免因程序规则严苛导致实体权利丧失，提升海外维权精准度。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties involved in UPC litigation must adopt a front-loaded strategy, ensuring all prior art and invalidity arguments are comprehensively presented at the initial stage. Relying on later supplementation, a habit common in EPO opposition proceedings, risks exclusion of critical evidence. Rights holders and defendants should conduct thorough early searches and legal assessments to avoid losing substantive rights due to procedural non-compliance. This approach enhances precision in cross-border enforcement and defense, aligning with the UPC’s emphasis on swift, efficient resolution without compromising fair trial standards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 严格限制逾期证据，无效攻击须随撤销反诉同步提出", "口头审理阶段原则上禁止引入新的无效理由或文件", "该立场比 EPO 异议部更严，旨在保障程序效率与公平"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Invalidity attacks must be filed concurrently with the counterclaim for revocation, with strict limits on subsequent submissions.", "New invalidity grounds or documents are generally prohibited during oral hearings to protect defense rights and accelerate proceedings.", "This strict stance exceeds EPO Opposition Division flexibility, prioritizing procedural efficiency and fairness in UPC litigation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["务必在提起撤销反诉时一次性提交全部无效证据", "适应 UPC 前置程序，避免沿用 EPO 后期补正习惯"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure all invalidity evidence is submitted with the initial counterclaim to avoid inadmissibility under strict UPC rules.", "Practice note: Adapt litigation strategies to the UPC's front-loaded system, avoiding reliance on post-filing amendments common in EPO practice.", "What to monitor: Observe future Local Division decisions regarding admissibility thresholds for late-filed evidence and procedural sanctions."]
core_points_en: Invalidity attacks must be filed concurrently with the counterclaim for revocation, with strict limits on subsequent submissions.
New invalidity grounds or documents are generally prohibited during oral hearings to protect defense rights and accelerate proceedings.
This strict stance exceeds EPO Opposition Division flexibility, prioritizing procedural efficiency and fairness in UPC litigation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure all invalidity evidence is submitted with the initial counterclaim to avoid inadmissibility under strict UPC rules.
Practice note: Adapt litigation strategies to the UPC's front-loaded system, avoiding reliance on post-filing amendments common in EPO practice.
What to monitor: Observe future Local Division decisions regarding admissibility thresholds for late-filed evidence and procedural sanctions.

items.title: Limiting the unitary patent, losing the long arm?
items.title_zh: 限制单位专利是否导致丧失长臂管辖权？
items.title_en: Limiting Unitary Patents: Retroactive Effect and Loss of Jurisdiction Over Non-Member States
items.summary: IMC Créations v Mul-T-Lock France. UPC_CFI_702/2024; UPC_CFI_369/2025
items.ai_summary_zh: 巴黎地方法院审理 IMC 诉 Mul-T-Lock 案，裁定单位专利的无条件限制具有追溯力，自授权日起生效。法院区分了单位专利与瑞士国家部分，因权利人限制单位专利导致瑞士部分存在无效风险，且无并行瑞士诉讼，法院拒绝行使长臂管辖权驳回瑞士部分诉求。
items.ai_summary_en: The Paris Local Division of the Unified Patent Court addressed infringement claims involving a European patent with unitary effect and national validations, including Switzerland. The court ruled that an unconditional limitation of the unitary patent applies retroactively from the grant date, governing infringement assessments for the entire period within UPC territories. Crucially, the court strictly distinguished between the unitary patent and the Swiss national part. Since the limitation did not affect the Swiss validation, infringement there was assessed against the originally granted claims. However, recognizing a significant validity risk for the Swiss part due to the proprietor’s decision to limit the unitary patent, and noting no parallel revocation proceedings in Switzerland, the court declined to exercise jurisdiction over the Swiss infringement claim. Instead of staying proceedings, it dismissed this portion of the case, highlighting the complex interplay between patent limitations and cross-border enforcement scope.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧维权需谨慎处理单位专利限制策略。单方面限制虽可稳固欧盟境内权利，但可能导致无法通过 UPC 一并追究瑞士等非成员国侵权责任，增加跨国维权成本与复杂性。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders must carefully evaluate the strategic implications of unconditionally limiting unitary patents during UPC proceedings. While such limitations may strengthen enforceability within UPC member states by clarifying claim scope, they can inadvertently undermine enforcement capabilities in non-member states like Switzerland. The divergence in validity standards between the unitary patent and national validations creates jurisdictional gaps, potentially increasing litigation costs and complexity for multinational IP portfolios. Practitioners should coordinate limitation strategies with broader global enforcement goals to avoid losing leverage in key non-UPC markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["单位专利无条件限制具追溯力，自授权日即约束侵权判定", "法院严格区分单位专利与非成员国国家部分的有效性评估", "因有效性分歧且无并行诉讼，法院拒绝行使对非成员国的长臂管辖"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Unconditional unitary patent limitations apply retroactively from the grant date, affecting infringement assessments across the entire UPC territory.", "Courts strictly separate unitary patents from non-member state national parts, assessing Swiss infringement based on original granted claims.", "Significant validity risks for non-UPC designations, absent parallel proceedings, lead courts to dismiss infringement claims rather than stay them."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["限制单位专利可能削弱对非成员国侵权行为的长臂追责能力", "跨国布局需统筹考虑单位专利与国家专利的协同维护策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Unilateral patent limitations may restrict ability to enforce rights simultaneously in UPC and non-UPC jurisdictions.", "Practice note: Coordinate unitary patent limitation strategies with national validation maintenance to preserve cross-border enforcement options.", "What to monitor: Judicial approaches to validity risks in non-UPC states when parallel revocation actions are absent."]
core_points_en: Unconditional unitary patent limitations apply retroactively from the grant date, affecting infringement assessments across the entire UPC territory.
Courts strictly separate unitary patents from non-member state national parts, assessing Swiss infringement based on original granted claims.
Significant validity risks for non-UPC designations, absent parallel proceedings, lead courts to dismiss infringement claims rather than stay them.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Unilateral patent limitations may restrict ability to enforce rights simultaneously in UPC and non-UPC jurisdictions.
Practice note: Coordinate unitary patent limitation strategies with national validation maintenance to preserve cross-border enforcement options.
What to monitor: Judicial approaches to validity risks in non-UPC states when parallel revocation actions are absent.

items.title: World IP Day 2026 - IP and Sports: Ready, Set, Innovate
items.title_zh: 2026年世界知识产权日：体育与创新的交汇
items.title_en: World IP Day 2026: How Intellectual Property Drives Innovation in the Sports Industry
items.summary: This year’s World Intellectual Property Day, themed “IP and Sports: Ready, Set, Innovate”, celebrates how creativity and innovation shape the world of sports. From trademarks protecting team identities and sponsorships, to image rights and patented technologies, IP plays a central role across the industry. In this post, we explore how sport and intellectual property intersect - not only in the industries we support, but also in the mindset we bring to our work.  Meet Tim Robrechts, Alba Folgueiras González, Frank Eijsvogels, Paul Puche, Erik Lumens and and discover what drives them beyond the office.
items.ai_summary_zh: HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER律所发文庆祝2026年世界知识产权日，主题为“IP与体育：准备、出发、创新”。文章指出知识产权在体育产业中的核心作用，涵盖保护团队身份和赞助的商标、肖像权及专利技术。内容结合律师个人运动经历，阐述体育精神与法律工作中所需的准备、适应力及耐心之间的共通性，强调在复杂环境中保持专注与灵活应对的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER marks World Intellectual Property Day 2026, themed 'IP and Sports: Ready, Set, Innovate,' highlighting the critical role of intellectual property in shaping the global sports sector. The analysis underscores that IP protection extends beyond trademarks for team identities and sponsorships to include image rights and patented technologies essential for modern athletic performance. By integrating personal narratives from legal professionals, the firm illustrates how the discipline required in sports—such as preparation, adaptability, and patience—mirrors the strategic mindset needed in complex IP litigation. This perspective emphasizes that navigating the intersection of sports and law requires both rigorous planning and the flexibility to manage unforeseen challenges effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国体育品牌出海欧洲需构建全方位IP组合，不仅限于商标注册，更应重视技术专利布局与运动员肖像权管理。建议企业借鉴文中提到的“准备与适应”策略，建立动态IP风险预警机制，以应对赞助纠纷或侵权挑战，提升品牌在欧洲市场的合规竞争力与商业价值。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the European sports sector should adopt a holistic IP strategy that integrates trademark registration with robust patent portfolios and strict image rights management. Establishing dynamic risk monitoring mechanisms is crucial for anticipating and resolving potential sponsorship disputes or infringement issues proactively. This approach enhances regulatory compliance and protects brand value across diverse European jurisdictions. Rights holders must ensure their IP frameworks are adaptable to evolving technological and commercial landscapes, thereby securing long-term competitive advantage and minimizing exposure to legal risks in high-stakes sporting environments.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年世知日聚焦体育产业，强调IP在塑造行业中的关键角色", "体育领域IP保护涵盖商标、肖像权及专利技术等多元权利", "体育竞技中的准备与适应力类比法律实务中的专业素养要求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["World IP Day 2026 highlights the pivotal role of intellectual property in driving creativity and innovation within the global sports industry.", "Comprehensive IP protection in sports encompasses trademarks, image rights, and patented technologies to secure commercial and competitive advantages.", "The mental resilience and strategic preparation required in sports closely parallel the professional competencies essential for effective legal practice."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["中国体育品牌赴欧需构建商标、专利及肖像权综合IP防护网", "建立动态IP风险预警机制，提升应对赞助纠纷与侵权的能力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Develop comprehensive IP strategies combining trademarks, patents, and image rights to protect sports brands in Europe.", "Practice note: Implement dynamic risk monitoring systems to proactively manage sponsorship disputes and infringement challenges.", "What to monitor: Evolving IP enforcement trends in the sports sector and the increasing importance of athlete image rights management."]
core_points_en: World IP Day 2026 highlights the pivotal role of intellectual property in driving creativity and innovation within the global sports industry.
Comprehensive IP protection in sports encompasses trademarks, image rights, and patented technologies to secure commercial and competitive advantages.
The mental resilience and strategic preparation required in sports closely parallel the professional competencies essential for effective legal practice.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Develop comprehensive IP strategies combining trademarks, patents, and image rights to protect sports brands in Europe.
Practice note: Implement dynamic risk monitoring systems to proactively manage sponsorship disputes and infringement challenges.
What to monitor: Evolving IP enforcement trends in the sports sector and the increasing importance of athlete image rights management.
